title: AMD Unveils New Power-Efficient, High-Performance Mobile Graphics for Premium and Thin-and-Light Laptops, and New Desktop Graphics Cards
summary: AMD has announced new additions and enhancements to its Radeon graphics

title: AMD Unveils New Ryzen Mobile Processors Uniting “Zen 3+” core with AMD RDNA 2 Graphics in Powerhouse Design
summary: AMD has announced its new Ryzen 6000 Series processors for laptops, combining the "Zen 3+" core architecture with RDNA 2 graphics for enhanced performance and battery life. Additionally, AMD introduced the Ryzen 7 5800X3D desktop processor with 3D V-Cache technology for superior gaming and preview previewed the upcoming Ryzen 7000 Series with "Zen 4" architecture. These announcements highlight AMD's focus on innovation across mobile and desktop computing, emphasizing efficiency, graphics capabilities, and security features.

title: Qualcomm unveils ‘digital chassis’ tech at CES as it diversifies beyond smartphones
summary: Qualcomm introduced its "digital chassis" strategy at CES, aiming to expand its mobile technology expertise beyond smartphones and into the automotive industry. This includes a new Snapdragon Ride Vision System for advanced driver assistance and infotainment deals with major car manufacturers like Volvo, Honda, and Renault. The company projects its automotive revenue to reach $3.5 billion annually in five years, signaling a significant diversification effort.

title: Qualcomm, Microsoft partner on metaverse chip for AR glasses
summary: Qualcomm and Microsoft are collaborating on custom chips for lightweight augmented reality (AR) glasses, targeting both consumers and businesses for metaverse applications. The partnership aims to integrate these chips with Microsoft's Mesh software for virtual presence and Qualcomm's Snapdragon Spaces for AR functions like spatial mapping and hand-tracking. Though no release date was provided, the initiative seeks to advance wearable AR devices for broader adoption.

title: HP OMEN reveals products for 2022, including the unique cooling device, the OMEN Cryo Chamber
summary: HP OMEN has unveiled its 2022 gaming lineup, featuring the OMEN 45L Desktop with the innovative OMEN Cryo Chamber CPU cooling solution, which significantly lowers temperatures. The lineup also includes the OMEN 25L Desktop, the compact Victus by HP 15L Desktop, and the OMEN 27u 4K Gaming Monitor, designed for next-gen consoles. These new products offer advanced performance, cooling, and customization options for a diverse gaming audience.

title: The ‘Bividend’: What’s Up With BTCS’s Bitcoin Dividend?
summary: BTCS, a blockchain technology company, announced the first-ever dividend payable in bitcoin by a Nasdaq-listed firm, dubbed a "bividend." While some critics view it as a marketing stunt due to Bitcoin's speculative nature and the dividend's one-time status, the move significantly boosted BTCS's stock price. The article suggests that despite the controversy, other companies might follow suit, adopting similar crypto dividends as an attention-getting strategy.

title: CES 2022: HP shows off Elite Dragonfly G3, Dragonfly Chromebook and lots more new hardware
summary: At CES 2022, HP unveiled a comprehensive portfolio targeting the hybrid-work market, including the Elite Dragonfly G3, Dragonfly Chromebook, and several new monitors, laptops, and desktop PCs. The new Elite Dragonfly G3 is a premium ultraportable laptop with a 13.5-inch screen and 12th-gen Intel Core processors, while the Dragonfly Chromebook offers a Chrome OS alternative with unique features like a haptic trackpad. HP also introduced various conferencing and webcam monitors, updated EliteBook and ProBook business laptops, and new desktop PCs and curved displays for creators.

title: Ambarella unveils AI chips for autonomous vehicles at CES 2022
summary: Ambarella announced its new CV3 AI domain controller family at CES 2022, designed to provide high AI processing performance for autonomous vehicles. These single-chip components enable centralized, multi-sensor perception and AV path planning, supporting systems from ADAS to Level 4 AVs. The CV3 family offers significant performance boosts and hardware scalability, allowing automakers to unify software stacks across their vehicle portfolios.

title: Hesai Technology showcased new automotive-grade hybrid solid-state Lidar AT128
summary: Hesai Technology showcased its new automotive-grade hybrid solid-state lidar AT128 at CES 2022. This long-range sensor is designed for L3+ ADAS applications in mass production vehicles, featuring image-level resolution, superior ranging capability, and automotive-grade reliability, with mass production slated for 2022. Hesai also unveiled the QT128, a short-range lidar aimed at L4 applications, entering mass production in Q1 2023.

title: Maze Therapeutics Nets $190M to Propel Precision Medicine Programs
summary: Maze Therapeutics secured $190 million in financing to advance its precision medicine pipeline, which includes nine programs. The company, founded in 2019, focuses on developing treatments for diseases like Pompe disease, Phenylketonuria, ALS, and chronic kidney disease, leveraging insights from human genetics. Matrix Capital Management led the new investment round, and Andy Tran from Matrix Capital Management joined Maze's board.

title: Stellar December gives TSMC record year
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC) reported record sales for the previous year, driven by strong demand for its advanced 5-nanometer process used in emerging sectors like 5G and high-performance computing. The company's consolidated sales reached NT$1.59 trillion ($57.47 billion USD), an 18.5 percent increase from 2020. TSMC is expected to announce a significant capital expenditure budget for the current year to further expand production capacity and maintain its technological advantage.

title: Corsair One i300 gaming PC review
summary: The Corsair One i300 is a compact, high-end gaming PC featuring Intel's Core i9 12900K, an Nvidia GeForce RTX 3080 Ti, and 64GB of DDR5-4800 RAM, priced at $4,999. It delivers excellent 4K gaming performance and operates quietly due to its unique liquid cooling system, though the CPU can run hot under heavy load. The article praises its aesthetic and hardware-packed design, acknowledging its high cost but highlighting its cutting-edge components and quiet operation.

title: Why AMD May Be Worth Its Expensive Valuation
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) recently launched over 30 new products and has upcoming earnings later this month. Barclays hiked its price target for AMD to $148, boosting the stock, which is up over 41% year-over-year. Despite a high valuation, AMD has shown significant revenue and net income growth, coupled with a strong balance sheet, suggesting long-term potential.

title: 投资指南：2022年的5只精选区块链ETF
summary: 这篇文章介绍了2022年值得关注的五只精选区块链ETF，为普通投资者提供了投资区块链和加密货币类上市公司的途径。文章详细分析了每只ETF的资产规模、持股重点、费用率以及成立以来的业绩表现，旨在帮助投资者根据自身需求选择合适的区块链投资产品。

title: What will be included in 5G Advanced Release 18?
summary: The upcoming 5G Advanced Release 18 will focus on end-to-end system enhancements and expanding support for new device types and use cases. Qualcomm's Juan Montojo detailed improvements such as MIMO radio arrays, mobile Integrated Access/Backhaul, AI/ML-driven network design, enhanced mobility, and optimizing power consumption. The release also aims to develop a scalable, unified air interface to support a diverse range of devices, from high-performance XR headsets to various IoT sensors, paving the way for the future 6G vision.

title: These 3 ETFs let you play the hot semiconductor sector, where Nvidia, Micron, AMD and others are growing sales rapidly
summary: This article highlights three ETFs that allow investors to gain exposure to the rapidly growing semiconductor sector, which includes companies like Nvidia, Micron, and AMD. Despite concerns about rising interest rates, the semiconductor industry continues to show strong growth, as evidenced by Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.'s impressive Q4 sales and earnings report. The chip-focused exchange-traded funds have significantly outperformed both the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq over the past 15 years.

title: These 3 ETFs let you play the hot semiconductor sector, where Nvidia, Micron, AMD and others are growing sales rapidly
summary: The article suggests that the semiconductor sector remains a strong investment area, even with rising interest rates impacting other tech stocks. It highlights the strong performance of chip exchange-traded funds (ETFs) over the past 15 years, outperforming both the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq. This positive outlook is further supported by Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.'s impressive fourth-quarter results and significant capital expenditure plans for 2022.

title: Initial Sound Open Firmware Support For AMD Hardware Comes With Linux 5.17
summary: The Linux 5.17 kernel updates include initial Sound Open Firmware (SOF) support for AMD hardware, specifically the Renoir Audio Co-Processor. This marks a significant step for AMD, bringing its audio hardware into the open-source SOF ecosystem previously championed by Intel. The update also adds HDA support for new audio codecs and other ASoC improvements.

title: AMD’s Ryzen CPU Reportedly Cuts Down Tesla Model 3’s Driving Range
summary: A report from Teslascope suggests that the AMD Ryzen microprocessors, recently installed in Tesla Model 3 vehicles, are causing a reduction in their driving range. Tesla is reportedly asking customers who pre-ordered a Model 3 to accept a range decrease, particularly in jurisdictions with strong consumer protection laws. While Tesla’s message attributes this to a new, power-intensive computer to optimize the touchscreen experience, the Model Y's range appears to have increased due to larger batteries, contrasting with the Model 3's reduction.

title: Intel places ASML order to step up TSMC competition
summary: Intel has ordered ASML's new TWINSCAN EXE:5200 extreme ultraviolet (EUV) system, marking a move to compete with Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC) in advancing to 2-nanometer process technology. This purchase aims to accelerate Intel's production with the new technology by 2025. While Intel is the first to order, a supply chain source suggests TSMC is also likely to acquire the system and lead in commercializing 2-nanometer production.

title: Intel’s curious revelation of its ASML deal raises questions
summary: Intel's recent announcement to purchase advanced chipmaking tools from ASML has drawn scrutiny from analysts, who suggest it's a strategic move to address market doubts about its manufacturing capabilities. This acquisition of ASML’s next-generation EUV lithography machine is seen as Intel's effort to develop more advanced 2 nm chips, challenging leaders like TSMC and Samsung in the foundry market. The unusual public disclosure of such an industry deal aims to bolster confidence in Intel's renewed commitment to contract chip manufacturing.

title: Intel's $20 bln Ohio factory could become world's largest chip plant
summary: Intel announced plans to invest an initial $20 billion in a new chip manufacturing complex in Ohio, which could expand to $100 billion with eight fabrication plants, potentially making it the world's largest. This move is part of CEO Pat Gelsinger's strategy to restore Intel's chipmaking dominance and reduce U.S. reliance on Asian manufacturing amidst a global semiconductor shortage. The company is actively seeking government subsidies and expects the shortage to last into 2023.

title: Low-power AI computer chip startup Ceremorphic exits stealth with $50M funding
summary: Ceremorphic Inc., a startup focused on energy-efficient AI supercomputing chips, has emerged from stealth mode with a $50 million Series A funding round. The company, founded by tech veteran Dr. Venkat Mattela, aims to develop a "complete silicon system" incorporating a 5-nanometer processor with a unique architecture and patented multithread technology (ThreadArch) to address reliability, security, and energy consumption challenges in high-performance AI, HPC, and metaverse applications. The funding for the Series A round was entirely provided by the company's founders, many of whom have a background with Redpine Signals Inc., known for ultralow-power wireless chipsets.

title: Silicon Labs Integrates AI/ML Acceleration in Low-power Wireless SoCs
summary: Silicon Labs has released two new families of wireless SoCs, BG24 and MG24, that integrate AI/ML accelerators for low-power IoT edge processing. These SoCs support a wide range of protocols and offer significant power reduction and speed increase for AI/ML applications. The company also introduced a software platform to aid developers in deploying AI/ML algorithms for various use cases, including smart home applications and medical devices.

title: Lenovo Launches HPC As-A-Service To Fight Dell Apex, HPE GreenLake
summary: Lenovo has launched a new High-Performance Computing (HPC) as-a-service offering as part of its TruScale portfolio, directly competing with Dell Technologies' Apex and HPE GreenLake. This service provides on-premises supercomputing capabilities with a pay-per-use model, managed by Lenovo and its channel partners. The move aligns with Lenovo's "One Lenovo" vision to expand beyond PCs into a comprehensive IT solutions provider and aims to reach $100 billion in revenue by taking market share from rivals.

title: Intel scores major win as court scraps $1.2 bln EU antitrust fine
summary: Intel has successfully overturned a 1.06-billion-euro ($1.2 billion) EU antitrust fine that was imposed 12 years ago for allegedly stifling rival Advanced Micro Devices. Europe's second-highest court annulled the fine, criticizing the European Commission's analysis as incomplete. This ruling is a significant setback for EU antitrust regulators and could impact similar cases involving other major tech companies.

title: CRDO Stock Price, Forecast & Analysis | CREDO TECHNOLOGY GROUP HOLDI (NASDAQ:CRDO)
summary: The article provides a detailed analysis of CREDO Technology Group Holding (NASDAQ:CRDO), covering its stock price, forecast, and various analytical ratings. It highlights CRDO's strong performance in the past year, its technical and fundamental ratings from ChartMill, and analysts' expectations for significant future growth in both EPS and revenue. The company specializes in high-speed connectivity solutions for the data infrastructure market.

title: Western Digital Taps Semiconductor Veteran for CFO Job
summary: Western Digital has appointed Wissam Jabre, a semiconductor industry veteran, as its new Chief Financial Officer. This move aligns with the hard-drive manufacturer's strategy to expand its memory-chip business. Mr. Jabre previously served as CFO at Dialog Semiconductor PLC and held finance roles at Advanced Micro Devices Inc. and Freescale Semiconductor Inc.

title: Chip shortage to last six months, one year or 18 months-- depending on who’s talking
summary: The article discusses the varying predictions for how long the global chip shortage will last, with estimates ranging from six months to 18 months or longer. It highlights views from IDC, Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger, and Texas Instruments, alongside ongoing efforts by the U.S. Congress and Commerce Secretary to address the issue through funding and incentives for domestic chip production. The consensus indicates that while some improvements might be seen, the shortage is expected to persist through 2022 and potentially into 2023 for various sectors.

title: Reference design offers wireless docking over Wi-Fi 6E for PCs
summary: Synaptics Incorporated has introduced Gemini, a reference design that offers wireless docking over Wi-Fi 6E for PCs, claiming it's the world's first true Wi-Fi 6/6E wireless docking experience for laptops. This technology enables full dual-4K display capability and ultra-low latency, comparable to wired connections, and integrates hardware and software components like the BCM43752 Wi-Fi 6/6E radio and DL-1950 video interface IC. While initially focused on PC docking, Gemini's applications extend to tablets, gaming, and VR, with analyst Patrick Moorhead highlighting its potential to revolutionize connectivity in hybrid work environments.

title: Cadence Platform Accelerates Microchip’s Data Center Solutions SoC Development
summary: Microchip Technology Inc. has adopted Cadence's Palladium Z2 Enterprise Emulation Platform for the development of its next-generation ASIC products, specifically targeting high-performance and scalable SoC solutions for data centers. The Palladium Z2 platform offers Microchip 2X better emulation capacity and 1.5X greater performance, alongside seamless integration with Cadence's Protium Enterprise Prototyping system. This adoption significantly accelerates Microchip's time-to-market and quality requirements for complex multi-chip systems.

title: Infographic: Highlights of Advanced Micro Devices’ (AMD) Q4 results
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ: AMD) reported strong fourth-quarter 2021 earnings, with adjusted earnings per share rising to $0.92 from $0.52 year-over-year, exceeding analyst estimates. Total revenues increased by 49% annually to $4.8 billion, also surpassing Wall Street's predictions. The chipmaker saw its net income reach $974 million or $0.80 per share for the quarter.

title: ETF Flows Cool Off as Stocks Stumble Out of the Gate
summary: January 2022 saw a significant cool-off in ETF flows and a stumble in stock markets globally, marking the worst monthly showing for the Morningstar Global Markets Index since March 2020. Fixed-income ETFs also experienced outflows, their first since March 2020, as investors reallocated their money amidst interest rate hike predictions and a strong performance from the energy sector. Vanguard dominated ETF provider flows, while drug and digital-asset focused ETFs appeared cheap, reflecting market turbulence and potential undervaluation.

title: AMD's stellar report fuels chip stock rally
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) reported strong quarterly demand for its semiconductors, boosting its annual revenue forecast and surpassing analysts' expectations, which led to an almost 11% surge in its stock. This positive report ignited a rally in other chip stocks, with Nvidia, Qualcomm, and Micron Technology also seeing jumps in their share prices, despite ongoing global supply snags and volatility in the semiconductor sector. Intel, however, saw a decline in its stock.

title: Ansys free student downloads: Master simulation skills to solve real-life problems
summary: Ansys offers free student downloads of its engineering simulation software to help aspiring engineers develop practical skills for real-world problems. The article highlights five key Ansys tools: Ansys Student, Ansys Discovery Student, Ansys LS-DYNA, Ansys Electronics Desktop Student, and SCADE Student, each designed for different aspects of simulation and analysis. These tools enable students to gain experience in fields like fluid dynamics, structural mechanics, electromagnetics, and model-based design, preparing them for careers in various industries.

title: Alphabet eyes $2 trillion value after blowout results
summary: Alphabet Inc. is on the verge of joining Apple and Microsoft in the $2 trillion market valuation club after a strong quarterly report led to an over 8% surge in its shares. The company also announced a 20-to-1 stock split, aiming to make shares more accessible to retail investors. At least 20 brokerages raised their price targets on Alphabet's stock.

title: Chip Startup Ceremorphic Seeks AI, HPC Edge With New 5nm Design
summary: Ceremorphic, a chip startup, has emerged from stealth mode with plans to produce a scalable, low-energy processor for AI and high-performance computing using TSMC's 5-nanometer process node. The company's CEO, Venkat Mattela, emphasizes that access to this advanced manufacturing process, secured through his team's prior success, as well as Ceremorphic's proprietary technologies for efficiency, reliability, and security, will set its Hierarchical Learning Processor apart from competitors. The startup aims to deliver samples to customers in 2023 and begin production in 2024, following test chip validation in March.

title: Texas Instruments plans to invest billions in semiconductor chips to combat shortage
summary: Texas Instruments plans to invest $3.5 billion annually through 2025, and then 10% of annual revenue from 2026-2030, to address the global semiconductor chip shortage. These chips are crucial for car and cell phone production, and demand has increased significantly since 2019. The Lake Highlands-based company will build new factories, primarily in Sherman, Texas, to meet this demand, as auto sales alone accounted for 21% of their sales this year.

title: SoftBank’s sale of Arm to Nvidia collapses, Arm to IPO
summary: The proposed sale of Arm Ltd by SoftBank Group Corp to U.S. chipmaker Nvidia Corp has collapsed due to regulatory hurdles and scrutiny from various international bodies. Instead of the sale, which was valued at up to $80 billion, Arm will now plan for an initial public offering (IPO). The deal faced opposition from U.S., British, EU, and Chinese regulators over concerns about competition, pricing, and innovation in vital chip markets.

title: What Nvidia Can’t Buy, It Can Still Get Through An Arm Partnership
summary: Nvidia's $40 billion acquisition of Arm Holdings from SoftBank Group was terminated due to regulatory scrutiny and market resistance, resulting in a $1.25 billion hit for Nvidia. Despite the failed acquisition, Nvidia, an Arm architecture licensee, plans to release its Arm server chip "Grace" next year and may pursue a joint venture partnership with Arm, allowing it to license its GPU and network chip technologies through Arm's licensing machine. The article suggests that such a partnership could enable many of the benefits Nvidia sought through ownership without the regulatory hurdles.

title: Design Challenges Increasing For Mixed-Die Packages
summary: The semiconductor industry faces growing design challenges for mixed-die packages as it moves beyond Moore's Law towards heterogeneous integration. While combining dies from different foundries offers benefits like cost reduction and optimal IP use, it also introduces complexities in supply chain relationships, liability, and the need for standardized interconnect schemes. The article highlights that business and organizational hurdles may be more significant than technological ones in advancing chiplet adoption, requiring closer collaboration across the ecosystem.

title: AMD Completes $49B Xilinx Acquisition, Largest Chip Deal In History
summary: AMD has finalized its $49 billion acquisition of Xilinx, making it the largest chip deal in history and transforming AMD into an "industry’s high-performance and adaptive computing leader." This strategic move expands AMD's offerings beyond CPUs and GPUs into reprogrammable FPGAs, significantly boosting its presence in data centers, embedded computing, and telecommunications. The deal will also enhance AMD's total addressable market and is expected to be accretive to margins, earnings per share, and free cash flow in the first year.

title: AMD Closes Record Chip Industry Deal with about $50-Bln Purchase of Xilinx
summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) has finalized its acquisition of Xilinx Inc. for approximately $50 billion, making it a record deal in the chip industry. This acquisition allows AMD to expand into the cloud, edge, and intelligent devices market, intensifying its competition with Intel Corp. in the data center chip sector. The combined entity will be led by AMD CEO Lisa Su and aims to leverage an outsourced manufacturing strategy, primarily with Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co Ltd (TSMC).

title: AMD Closes $50 Billion Xilinx Acquisition
summary: AMD has completed its acquisition of Xilinx for approximately $50 billion, a deal initially valued at $35 billion in October 2020. This acquisition significantly boosts AMD's ability to compete with Intel in the data center chip sector by offering a comprehensive range of High Performance Computing (HPC) technologies, including CPUs, GPUs, FPGAs, and Adaptive SoCs. The former Xilinx business will operate as AMD's Adaptive and Embedded Computing Group (AECG), led by ex-Xilinx CEO Victor Peng.

title: Intel to Buy Israel’s Tower Semiconductor for $5.4 Billion
summary: Intel Corp. has agreed to acquire Israel’s Tower Semiconductor Ltd. for $5.4 billion as part of CEO Pat Gelsinger’s strategy to expand into the outsourced chip-manufacturing business. This acquisition aims to enhance Intel's expertise and customer base in the chip-foundry market, directly competing with industry leaders like Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. The deal is expected to close in about 12 months and is a key part of Intel's broader investment cycle to modernize its factories and rebuild manufacturing capabilities.

title: AMD Radeon ProRender for Rhino
summary: AMD has released an updated, free, physically-based renderer called Radeon ProRender for Rhino, which is deeply integrated into Rhino 7.0. This renderer can utilize either the CPU or GPU, with GPU acceleration offering faster performance, especially with AMD Radeon PRO W6000 series GPUs featuring hardware raytracing. It allows users to render photorealistic imagery directly within the Rhino viewport, supports existing Rhino material definitions, and offers flexible lighting and scene setup options.

title: Intel to buy Israel's Tower Semiconductor for $5.4bn
summary: Intel announced its acquisition of Israeli chipmaker Tower Semiconductor for $5.4 billion. This deal strengthens Intel's position in specialized chip production, particularly for analog chips used in various applications like automotive and medical sensors. The acquisition aims to capitalize on the increasing global demand for semiconductors.

title: Inside Pat Gelsinger’s audacious $43.5B plan to revive Intel
summary: This article details Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger's plans to revitalize the company, which has recently faced significant challenges from competitors like Apple and AMD, and struggled with manufacturing delays. Gelsinger, who rejoined Intel after a period away, aims to tackle both technological and cultural issues to restore Intel's preeminence in the chip industry. His experience at Intel during its heyday positions him to address these intricate problems, including a new business model centered on investing $43.5 billion to regain its manufacturing edge.

title: Intel delays 2023 server chip, says it needs to boost spending to catch competitors
summary: Intel announced it would need to significantly increase spending over the next two years to fund its turnaround and transformation into a semiconductor manufacturer. The company also confirmed that the release of its forthcoming server chip, codenamed Granite Rapids, has been pushed from 2023 to 2024. This delay and investment strategy led to a drop in Intel's stock as investors questioned the impact on profit margins and the capital-intensive nature of its new foundry business.

title: The Processor Chip Industry Is Another Ugly Capitalist Oligopoly
summary: This article criticizes the semiconductor industry, labeling it as an "ugly capitalist oligopoly" due to the extreme concentration of chip manufacturing among a few companies, primarily Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC). It highlights how this concentration, driven by high capital costs for fabrication plants and state subsidies, leads to supply chain vulnerabilities, inflation, and limited competition. The author argues that even efforts by governments like the US to subsidize domestic chip production won't fundamentally alter the industry's oligopolistic structure or shift its profit-driven nature.

title: AMD CPU Shipments in Japan Sink to 25% of Total, Intel Achieves 74%
summary: AMD's CPU market share in Japan fell to 25% in January, a three-year low, while Intel captured 74% of total sales. This decline follows a period in 2020 where AMD reached market share highs of 68-70%, buoyed by its Ryzen 5 series and Intel's 10nm production issues. The recent shift is attributed to Intel's recovery from manufacturing woes and the successful launch of its Alder Lake processors, which restored performance parity with AMD.

title: Like building the world's tallest tower every two years
summary: This article delves into the critical role of Intel Israel's Core Group in developing the most essential parts of Intel's processors: the cores. It explains the evolution of processor cores from single-core to multi-core designs, highlighting the challenges and innovations, including the creation of hybrid architectures with Performance and Efficiency Cores in the Alder Lake processor. The piece emphasizes the immense complexity and continuous innovation required in core development, likening it to building and constantly improving the world's tallest tower.

title: An Interview with Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger
summary: This article features an interview with Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger, discussing his IDM 2.0 strategy, the acquisition of Tower Semiconductor, and Intel's role in the semiconductor industry. Gelsinger explains why Intel is committed to manufacturing its own chips despite the success of fabless competitors, emphasizing the geopolitical importance of a Western technology provider and the benefits of integrating foundry operations. He also addresses concerns about potential overcapacity in the industry and the future of x86 architecture.

title: Behind Intel’s HPC Chip that Will Pierce the Exascale Barrier
summary: Intel has unveiled new details about its Ponte Vecchio processor, which will power the Aurora supercomputer, aiming to achieve exascale performance. The chip utilizes advanced 2D and 3D chiplet integration technologies, combining over 100 billion transistors across 47 pieces of silicon using various manufacturing processes, including TSMC's N5 and Intel 7. This heterogeneous integration and sophisticated thermal management are crucial for achieving the targeted 2 exaflops for the Aurora supercomputer.

title: UMC to Build New 22nm Wafer Fab in Singapore
summary: United Microelectronics Corp. (UMC) announced plans to build a new advanced 22nm wafer fabrication plant in Singapore, investing $5 billion. The first phase of the new fab, which will be adjacent to its existing 300mm facility (Fab12i), is expected to commence production in late 2024 with a monthly capacity of 30,000 wafers. This expansion is supported by customer multi-year supply agreements, reflecting strong demand for UMC's 22/28nm technologies in sectors like 5G, IoT, and automotive, and aims to address the ongoing structural shortage in foundry capacity.

title: Semiconductor sales to Russia banned, but that shouldn’t hurt Intel, AMD and other chip makers
summary: Semiconductor companies are banning sales to Russia in response to its invasion of Ukraine. Despite a broader market sell-off and sanctions against Russia, an analyst believes these bans will have little financial impact on major chip makers like Intel and AMD because Russia's semiconductor market is likely too small to significantly affect their revenue.

title: Use WGMI to Enter the Crypto Economy
summary: The crypto economy is diversifying beyond direct Bitcoin ownership, with new ETFs like the Valkyrie Bitcoin Miners ETF (WGMI) offering access. WGMI focuses on bitcoin mining equities and is actively managed, allowing it to adapt to a nascent asset class with high growth potential. The ETF also incorporates a renewable energy overlay for its mining components, addressing environmental concerns associated with bitcoin mining.

title: AMD, Arm, Google, Intel, TSMC and others join Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express (UCIe) group
summary: Leading semiconductor companies including AMD, Arm, Google, Intel, Meta, Microsoft, Qualcomm, Samsung, and TSMC have formed the Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express (UCIe) industry consortium. This group aims to establish an open chiplet ecosystem by standardizing die-to-die interconnects, with the UCIe 1.0 specification already ratified. This initiative leverages existing PCI Express and Compute Express Link standards to enable easier mixing and matching of chiplet components from multiple vendors for System-on-Chip (SoC) construction.

title: Leaders in Semiconductors, Packaging, IP Suppliers, Foundries, and Cloud Service Providers Join Forces to Standardize Chiplet Ecosystem
summary: A consortium of leading technology companies, including ASE, AMD, Arm, Google Cloud, Intel, Meta, Microsoft, Qualcomm, Samsung, and TSMC, has formed to standardize the chiplet ecosystem. They have ratified the Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express (UCIe) 1.0 specification, an open industry standard for die-to-die interconnects, aiming to enable multi-vendor compatibility for System-on-Chip construction. The consortium is open for membership and plans to develop future generations of the UCIe technology.

title: DSP Concepts Launches TWS Toolkit Powered by Audio Weaver
summary: DSP Concepts has launched a new toolkit for True Wireless Stereo (TWS) earbuds, powered by its Audio Weaver development platform. This toolkit features a reference design optimized for the Cadence Tensilica HiFi 5 DSP, includes algorithms from partners like Mimi Hearing Technologies and VisiSonics for personalized and spatial audio, and offers a Rapid Audio Prototyping Device (RAPID) TWS Kit to accelerate development. The initiative aims to help OEMs create advanced audio experiences for TWS products and reduce development risks.

title: Nvidia hackers allegedly attempting to blackmail company into open-sourcing GPU drivers
summary: The Lapsus$ ransomware gang, responsible for a recent attack on Nvidia, is allegedly attempting to blackmail the company into open-sourcing its GPU drivers. The attackers have threatened to leak sensitive GPU files if Nvidia does not comply with their demands by a set deadline, which also included a previous request to remove mining hashrate limiters. Nvidia has confirmed a cybersecurity incident where employee credentials and proprietary information were stolen, but denies ransomware deployment.

title: Consortium formed to standardize chiplet ecosystem
summary: A consortium comprising major tech companies like Intel, AMD, Arm, Google Cloud, Microsoft, Qualcomm, Samsung, and TSMC has been formed to standardize the die-to-die interconnect and foster an open chiplet ecosystem. They have ratified the UCIe 1.0 specification, which provides a ubiquitous interconnect standard for package-level integration, enabling easy mixing and matching of chiplet components from multiple vendors for System-on-Chip construction. The consortium aims to address customer demand for customizable package-level integration and reduce development time and cost through open standards.

title: EPAM Shares Continue to Fall on Ukraine Crisis & Russia Exit
summary: EPAM Systems' shares fell significantly due to the escalating Russia-Ukraine crisis and the company's decision to exit Russia. EPAM, with substantial exposure in Eastern Europe, announced it would discontinue services in Russia and offer humanitarian aid to Ukrainian employees, aligning with U.S. sanctions. The conflict is expected to impact the company's revenues and profitability due to its large workforce in the affected regions.

title: AMD Radeon™ PRO W6600X GPU for Mac Pro Unleashes New Creative Possibilities for Design and Content Creation Professionals
summary: AMD has announced the availability of the new AMD Radeon™ PRO W6600X GPU for Mac Pro, aimed at creative professionals. Built on the AMD RDNA™ 2 architecture, it features AMD Infinity Cache™ and GDDR6 memory, offering high performance and visual fidelity for demanding professional applications. This GPU completes the AMD Radeon PRO W6000X series, providing a mid-range solution for content creation needs.

title: Industry Leaders Join Forces to Standardize Chiplet Ecosystem
summary: A consortium of industry leaders, including AMD, Arm, Google Cloud, Intel, Meta, Microsoft, Qualcomm, Samsung, TSMC, and ASE, has formed to standardize die-to-die interconnects and foster an open chiplet ecosystem. They have ratified the Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express (UCIe) 1.0 specification, an open industry standard for package-level connectivity. This initiative aims to reduce development time and cost, promoting customizable and interoperable chiplet designs for future silicon innovations.

title: Micron CEO says chip shortage improving, but could last into 2023
summary: Micron Technology CEO Sanjay Mehrotra stated that the semiconductor shortage, which has affected numerous industries, is improving but could extend into 2023. He highlighted the automotive sector as particularly impacted, with modern vehicles becoming "data centers on wheels" that require significant memory and storage. Mehrotra emphasized the need for a partnership between the U.S. government and the private sector to establish the U.S. as a leader in the chip industry, noting that Micron plans to invest over $150 billion globally in memory manufacturing, research, and development.

title: TSMC’s largest customer makes up 26% of revenue
summary: Sales from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co's (TSMC) largest customer, believed to be Apple Inc., accounted for 26% of its total revenue last year, reaching NT$405.4 billion. Its second-largest customer, suspected to be Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD), contributed 10% of sales. The US market remained TSMC's biggest buyer, while sales to China declined due to US sanctions against Huawei Technologies Co.

title: University of Arizona Basketball Star Cate Reese Signs NIL Endorsement Deal with Newegg
summary: Newegg, a global technology e-commerce retailer, announced its first NCAA athlete endorsement deal with University of Arizona women's basketball player Cate Reese. Reese, a three-time All-Pac-12 selection, will promote Newegg during the 2022 women’s NCAA basketball tournament as part of a national Name, Image, and Likeness (NIL) agreement. She highlights how technology is integral to balancing her demanding life as a student-athlete, using devices for academics, social connections, and even gaming.

title: AMD Launches the Ultimate Gaming Processor, Brings Enthusiast Performance to an Expanded Lineup of Ryzen Desktop Processors
summary: AMD has announced the launch of its Ryzen 7 5800X3D processor, featuring groundbreaking 3D V-Cache technology that delivers up to 15% more gaming performance. Alongside this, AMD is expanding its Ryzen Desktop portfolio with six new "Zen 3" and "Zen 2" processors, offering more options for PC enthusiasts at various price points. The company also confirmed extended motherboard support for Ryzen 5000 Series processors on older AMD 300 Series chipsets.

title: Aptos raises $200M to bring the Diem blockchain back to life
summary: Aptos Labs, a startup founded by former employees of Meta's Diem blockchain project, has raised $200 million in strategic funding led by Andreessen Horowitz. The company aims to develop a safe and scalable blockchain network, learning from Diem's regulatory challenges. Aptos is building its own open-source blockchain for Web3 applications and expects to launch its public mainnet in the third quarter of 2022.

title: Introducing AMD Software: Adrenalin Edition 2022 Release and AMD FidelityFX Super Resolution 2.0 – Delivering High-Performance and Visually Stunning Gaming Experiences
summary: AMD has announced the Adrenalin Edition 2022 software release and the new FidelityFX Super Resolution (FSR) 2.0 technology, aiming to enhance gaming performance and visual quality. The Adrenalin Edition includes Radeon Super Resolution (RSR) for driver-based upscaling compatible with thousands of games, and an updated AMD Link application for personal gaming clouds. FSR 2.0, expected in Q2 2022, is an advanced temporal upscaling technology that boosts framerates with high image quality, working across various AMD and competitor GPUs without needing dedicated machine learning hardware.

title: When the world runs low on neon gas
summary: The Russia-Ukraine war has significantly impacted the global supply of neon gas, which is crucial for semiconductor manufacturing. Ukraine, home to two major neon purification companies, has halted production due to the conflict, threatening to exacerbate the existing worldwide chip shortage. This situation highlights the precarity of specialized global supply chains, where the disruption of a single component can have far-reaching consequences for the technology industry, leading to higher prices, delivery delays, and potential product unavailability.

title: Is It Finally Time to Start Buying Intel Stock?
summary: Intel, a pioneer in micro-processing, has seen its stock underperform competitors for years, but an ambitious makeover and new leadership may signal an attractive entry point for long-term investors. CEO Pat Gelsinger's plans for a massive $20 billion semiconductor plant in Ohio and other investments aim to restore Intel's market leadership. Despite investor skepticism reflected in its depressed stock price, Morningstar analysts see an appealing opportunity with a 27% discount to its fair value estimate and a 3.11% dividend yield.

title: 3rd Gen AMD EPYC Processors with AMD 3D V-Cache Technology Deliver Outstanding Leadership Performance in Technical Computing Workloads
summary: AMD has announced the general availability of its 3rd Gen EPYC processors with AMD 3D V-Cache technology, codenamed "Milan-X." These processors are the world's first data center CPUs to use 3D die stacking and are built on the "Zen 3" core architecture. They offer up to a 66% performance uplift in technical computing workloads and feature the industry's largest L3 cache for applications like CFD, FEA, and EDA.

title: Keynote Wrap-Up: Turning Data Centers into ‘AI Factories,’ NVIDIA CEO Intros Hopper Architecture, H100 GPU, New Supercomputers, Software
summary: During NVIDIA GTC, CEO Jensen Huang unveiled a vision of data centers as "AI Factories," introducing the new Hopper GPU architecture and H100 GPU, along with new AI and accelerated computing software. He also announced powerful new data-center-scale systems, including Grace CPU Superchip and DGX H100 supercomputers. Huang emphasized the role of Omniverse in integrating real and virtual worlds, speeding collaboration, and serving as a proving ground for new robotics and autonomous vehicles.

title: NVIDIA Introduces Grace CPU Superchip
summary: NVIDIA has unveiled its Grace CPU Superchip, an Arm Neoverse-based discrete data center CPU engineered for AI infrastructure and high-performance computing. This new chip boasts 144 high-performance cores and 1 terabyte/second memory, offering double the performance and energy-efficiency compared to current leading server chips. It is designed to work seamlessly with NVIDIA's existing software stacks and is expected to be available in the first half of 2023.

title: This tech group is leading the charge in sustainable Web 3.0 development
summary: Atlas Technology Management, a Singapore-headquartered technology group, is expanding its Web 3.0 computing infrastructure and committing to sustainable development. The company aims to achieve 100% green energy adoption by 2024 and plans to expand its global coverage to over 30 countries in the next five years. Atlas is also exploring advanced technologies like underwater data centers to minimize environmental impact.

title: 10 Servers With AMD EPYC Milan-X New Server CPUs
summary: AMD has launched its third-generation EPYC processors with 3-D V-Cache technology, code-named Milan-X, which is the world's first data center CPU using 3-D die stacking. This innovation allows for 768 MB of L3 cache, significantly enhancing performance for technical computing workloads like FEA, EDA, and CFD. Major server vendors such as Dell Technologies, HPE, Cisco, Lenovo, and Supermicro are rolling out new or upgraded servers supporting these processors, aiming to offer improved performance, lower total cost of ownership, and reduced power consumption.

title: Possible Nvidia, Intel GPU Alliance Could Frustrate Rivals AMD, TSMC
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang is in talks with Intel to use its foundries for GPU production, a move that could significantly impact competitors AMD and TSMC. This potential alliance would diversify Nvidia's supply base and challenge TSMC's dominance in the foundry market, while also putting pressure on AMD's supercomputing ambitions. Both Intel and Nvidia are interested in the collaboration, with Intel aiming to become the top GPU foundry by 2025.

title: Nvidia considers teaming up with Intel on chip manufacturing
summary: Nvidia is in discussions with Intel about potentially using Intel as a foundry for manufacturing its chips. This consideration comes amid a global semiconductor chip shortage and Intel's efforts to become a major U.S. foundry supplier, despite the challenges of aligning technologies and business models between the companies. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang expressed openness to the idea, noting existing close collaboration between the firms.

title: The Top 5: Biggest Automotive Semiconductor Manufacturers
summary: The automotive semiconductor market, valued at $43.6 billion in 2021, is projected to exceed $115 billion by 2030, driven by the growth of electric vehicles and autonomous technologies. This article identifies the top five automotive semiconductor manufacturers, excluding TSMC which dominates the foundry market, and details their market share, financial performance, and strategic investments. The leading companies are Infineon Technologies AG, NXP Semiconductors N.V., Renesas Electronics Corp., Texas Instruments, Inc., and STMicroelectronics N.V.

title: Synopsys Launches Industry's First Broad-Scale Cloud SaaS Solution to Transform Chip Development Landscape
summary: Synopsys has launched Synopsys Cloud, the industry's first broad-scale cloud Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) solution for chip development. This new model, optimized for electronic design automation (EDA) and deployed on Microsoft Azure, offers a pay-per-use approach to provide unparalleled flexibility and efficiency for complex chip designs. The collaboration with Microsoft aims to streamline access to compute and EDA resources, addressing the escalating demands of chip design and verification.

title: The semiconductor decade: A trillion-dollar industry
summary: The global semiconductor industry is projected to reach a trillion-dollar valuation by 2030, driven by an average annual growth rate of 6 to 8 percent. Key growth areas include automotive, computation and data storage, and wireless technologies, with automotive expected to triple demand. Industry leaders are advised to strategically focus on R&D, manufacturing, and sourcing to capitalize on this expansion.

title: AMD Buying John Chambers-Backed Pensando For $1.9B To Boost Cloud, Chip Innovation
summary: AMD announced its acquisition of Pensando, an edge and cloud startup, for $1.9 billion to enhance its data center processors with Pensando's software stack and programmable packet processors. This strategic move aims to expand AMD's offerings for cloud, enterprise, and edge customers by leveraging Pensando's distributed services platform, which has been praised for its performance and efficiency. Pensando's CEO Prem Jain and his team will join AMD's Data Center Solutions Group, continuing to execute their product roadmap with increased scale and market reach.

title: AMD-Pensando Buy: From AWS Rivalry To Cisco, HPE Links—5 Things To Know
summary: AMD is acquiring edge computing startup Pensando for $1.9 billion to enhance its data center offerings and expand its capabilities for cloud, enterprise, and edge customers. Pensando, known for its programmable processor and software stack, boasts a strong market presence, including partnerships with HPE and Oracle, and aims to challenge AWS's cloud technology. This acquisition is expected to accelerate innovation in distributed services and solidify AMD's position in the next generation of data center infrastructure.

title: AMD Stock Up 2%, Advanced Micro Devices Acquires Pensando for $2B
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) shares rose by 2.16% after the company announced its intention to acquire data center optimization startup Pensando for approximately $1.9 billion. This acquisition is expected to bolster AMD's position in high-performance computing and address the increasing demand for chipsets amidst global shortages. Despite a 35.72% gain over the past year, AMD's stock has declined about 23.19% year-to-date, reflecting mixed investor sentiment.

title: Comcast Begins Rollout of New Supersonic WiFi
summary: Comcast has launched Supersonic WiFi, offering speeds faster than 1 Gbps and 3x more capacity for Xfinity Internet Gigabit and Ultrafast customers. Enabled by its new WiFi 6E gateway, this service is designed to support the increasing number of connected devices in homes. The new gateway features three WiFi bands (2.4 GHz, 5 GHz, and 6 GHz) to reduce interference and enhance bandwidth.

title: AMD CEO Lisa Su says chipmaker is 'doubling down on data centers' with $1.9B Pensando deal
summary: AMD CEO Lisa Su stated that the company is "doubling down on data centers" through its planned $1.9 billion acquisition of Pensando. The chipmaker aims to expand its presence in the data center market with this strategic move, building on its already surging data center sales. This acquisition is expected to further boost AMD's existing data center business.

title: STMicroelectronics’ AMOLED power-management IC boosts viewing experience and battery runtime in portable devices
summary: STMicroelectronics has launched a new fully integrated Power-Management IC (PMIC), the STMP30, designed for AMOLED displays in portable devices. This PMIC features a low quiescent current and flexible output voltage control to optimize display brightness for power efficiency and visibility, including a High-Brightness Mode for outdoor use. It also supports Always-On Display mode for enhanced user convenience at very low power, incorporating important safety features.

title: Dreams of green data
summary: This article discusses the efforts by companies like Apple, Google, and Equinix, alongside Santa Clara officials, to make data centers more environmentally friendly. The primary focus is on reducing energy consumption and shifting towards carbon emission-free power sources for these energy-intensive technological backbones. The article highlights the increasing importance of cleaner power sources and less electricity use for data centers.

title: Which is the bigger company: AMD, Nvidia, or Intel?
summary: This article analyzes Intel, AMD, and Nvidia to determine which is the "bigger" company, looking beyond market capitalization to factors like employee count, revenue, product diversity, and market share. While Nvidia leads in market cap, sustained analysis reveals Intel to be the largest overall due to its revenue and employee numbers, despite AMD and Nvidia's significant recent growth. The article also touches on the unique advantage Intel holds with its chip manufacturing capabilities.

title: Do Penny Stocks Ever Make Money? Here Are 7 Success Stories
summary: This article highlights seven former penny stocks that achieved significant profitability and provided substantial returns for early investors. It demonstrates that while penny stocks are high-risk, those companies that eventually become profitable can offer life-changing gains. The article details the financial turnaround and stock performance of Advanced Micro Devices, BBQ Holdings, Syndax Pharmaceuticals, VAALCO Energy, Digital Turbine, eXp World Holdings, and The Joint Corp.

title: Ampere valuation in spotlight: Arm-based chip manufacturer files for IPO
summary: Ampere Computing LLC, an Arm-based chip manufacturer backed by Oracle, has confidentially filed for an initial public offering. This move sparks speculation about the company's valuation, which was reportedly $8 billion during earlier discussions with SoftBank. The IPO aims to secure funding for Ampere to intensify its competition with Intel and AMD in the server chip market, where its Arm-based chips are gaining traction with cloud providers like Oracle and Microsoft Azure.

title: SK hynix Accelerates DRAM Design with Cadence Spectre FX Simulator
summary: SK hynix has adopted Cadence's Spectre FX Simulator for functional verification of their DDR4 and DDR5 DRAMs, crucial for PC and mobile applications. This simulator offers high accuracy, fast performance, and an intuitive user experience, enabling SK hynix to efficiently manage large transistor-level designs and meet stringent tapeout deadlines. The technology is already in use for PC and mobile DRAMs and is being expanded to HBM and graphics DRAMs, significantly improving design methodology and productivity.

title: The end of one-chip wonders: Why Nvidia, Intel and AMD's valuations have experienced massive upheaval
summary: The semiconductor industry has seen a dramatic shift, with Nvidia now valued as much as Intel and AMD combined, a reversal from five years ago when Intel held that position. This change reflects evolving technology interaction and other companies specializing in new areas, causing Intel to fall behind. The article highlights a massive upheaval in semiconductor valuations mirroring the industry's transformation.

title: The end of one-chip wonders: Why Nvidia, Intel and AMD’s valuations have experienced massive upheaval
summary: This article discusses the dramatic shift in market capitalization within the semiconductor industry over the past five years. Once dominant Intel has been surpassed by Nvidia, which is now valued as much as Intel and AMD combined, reflecting fundamental changes in how technology is used and specialized solutions. This upheaval highlights a significant transformation in the industry's landscape.

title: Synopsys Introduces Industry's Highest Performance Neural Processor IP
summary: Synopsys has launched its DesignWare ARC NPX6 and NPX6FS NPU IP, along with the MetaWare MX Development Toolkit, to meet the increasing performance demands of AI systems-on-chip. This new NPU IP delivers the industry's highest performance, offering up to 3,500 TOPS for AI applications and support for complex neural network models, while also addressing functional safety requirements for automotive use. The comprehensive software environment aims to accelerate application development and ensure compliance with standards like ISO 26262.

title: Why dividend ETFs may not be worthwhile
summary: This article examines the viability of dividend ETFs, concluding that many are not worthwhile after accounting for taxes, fees, and capital distributions. The author discusses challenges like 30% dividend taxation for foreign investors in US ETFs, poor underlying stock performance in high-yield ETFs, and issues with capital distributions eroding value. Alternative options in Singapore, UK, and Hong Kong are also explored, but ultimately deemed insufficient, leading to the recommendation that investors build their own dividend portfolios.

title: Better Buy: Intel vs. Micron Technology
summary: This article compares Intel and Micron Technology, two semiconductor stocks, evaluating which is a better buy. While Intel is working on a turnaround with an aggressive product roadmap and expected revenue growth by 2026, Micron is already achieving strong revenue and earnings growth due to high demand for memory chips. The author concludes that Micron is the better choice for investors seeking a value stock, given its current financial performance and attractive valuation.

title: Latest Oracle Exadata servers ascend to the cloud
summary: Oracle has announced the availability of its latest Exadata Cloud Infrastructure X9M appliance in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, marking the rollout of its newest generation of Exadata processors. This new service, based on AMD microprocessors, offers significant improvements in performance, throughput, and capacity without increased costs, allowing organizations to consolidate various database workloads. Oracle claims superior performance compared to AWS RDS and Azure SQL, with the service designed for scalable deployment both on-premises and across Oracle's expanding global cloud footprint.

title: Morefine S500+ Review – An AMD Ryzen 7 5700U mini PC tested with Windows 11, Ubuntu 20.04
summary: This article reviews the Morefine S500+, an AMD Ryzen 7 5700U mini PC, detailing its hardware, installation experiences, and performance benchmarks on both Windows 11 and Ubuntu 20.04. The review highlights the PC's expandability, dual Ethernet ports, and strong performance, while noting its audible fan under load and issues with WiFi connectivity on Ubuntu initially. Comparisons are made against other mini PCs, and thermal and power consumption data are provided.

title: HPE Creates Its Own AI Stack For Large Enterprises
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) is expanding its AI software portfolio and leveraging its HPC hardware expertise to provide a comprehensive AI stack for large enterprises. This initiative includes a Machine Learning Development System that simplifies infrastructure management and facilitates collaboration for training models at scale, addressing the complexities faced by data scientists. Furthermore, HPE is promoting "swarm learning" to enable AI model training where data resides, crucial for privacy and compliance in industries like healthcare and banking.

title: Ansys simulation software to be used in CU Boulder graduate program
summary: Ansys Inc., an engineering simulation software company based in Pittsburgh, is partnering with the University of Colorado Boulder. Their software will be integrated into the university's new Professional Master’s Program in High-Speed Digital Engineering. This collaboration aims to provide students with advanced simulation tools relevant to their field.

title: Advisors Are Powering Portfolios With Thematic ETFs
summary: Thematic ETFs, which invest in companies poised to benefit from long-term transformative changes, are increasingly being adopted by financial advisors as a replacement for traditional sector funds. These ETFs offer more precise exposure to growth trends like blockchain, emergent food, robotics, and electric vehicles. Asset managers are expanding their thematic ETF offerings, with BlackRock (iShares) recently launching two new products in this space, highlighting a growing conviction in the long-term benefits of thematic investing despite some recent market volatility.

title: How To Get Your PCB Manufacturing Right
summary: This article provides essential advice for ensuring smooth and cost-effective PCB manufacturing. It covers strategies like effective BOM management, minimizing through-hole components, understanding manufacturer capabilities, providing thermal relief, implementing Poka-yoke principles, proper component placement, and silkscreen considerations to prevent common production issues. Following these guidelines can lead to reduced costs, fewer failures, and higher quality PCBs.

title: US-based Applied Materials’ India arm buys office space, land in Bangalore for Rs 338 crore
summary: Applied Materials India, the subsidiary of the US-based semiconductor equipment manufacturer, has acquired nearly 10 acres of land and 2.6 lakh sq ft of office space in Bangalore for Rs 338 crore. This acquisition is part of the company's plan to invest over Rs 1,800 crore in India to expand its engineering infrastructure and support its growth. India is projected to become the fastest-growing semiconductor consumption market globally, with demand expected to exceed $110 billion by the end of the decade.

title: AMD Confirms Zen 4 Dragon Range, Phoenix APUs for 2023
summary: AMD has released an updated roadmap confirming Zen 4 Dragon Range and Phoenix mobile APUs for 2023. Dragon Range is designed for enthusiast gaming laptops, while Phoenix targets thin and light gaming devices, both supporting PCIe 5.0 and either DDR5 or LPDDR5 memory. The company also teased Ryzen 7000 (Raphael) desktop processors, expected in late 2022, which will bring Zen 4 to the mainstream market on TSMC's 5nm process node and the new AM5 platform with DDR5-only support.

title: Intel Names Matt Poirier Senior Vice President of Corporate Development
summary: Intel Corporation has appointed Matt Poirier as senior vice president of corporate development, effective May 30, 2022. Poirier brings over 20 years of experience in leadership, finance, and corporate development, including previous roles at AMD and Xilinx. In his new role, he will lead Intel's corporate development team, focusing on strategic mergers and acquisitions to accelerate the company's growth and reinforce its leadership in the semiconductor industry, reporting directly to CEO Pat Gelsinger.

title: HP debuts 2 new Chromebooks for business in the Elite c600 series with AMD and Intel on board
summary: HP has introduced two new Chromebooks, the Elite c640 G3 and Elite c645 G2, aimed at business users but also available to consumers. These devices emphasize security and productivity for hybrid work environments, with the c645 G2 featuring AMD Ryzen 5000 C-Series processors and the c640 G3 utilizing Intel's 12th-gen processors, along with enhanced features like improved screens, advanced security options, and AI capabilities for audio and visual. Both models are designed to offer a robust and affordable option within HP's high-end Chromebook portfolio, starting in the $500 range.

title: Intel Nabs Former AMD Exec Matt Poirier To Lead M&A Quest
summary: Intel has appointed former AMD executive Matt Poirier as senior vice president of corporate development, effective May 30, to lead its mergers and acquisitions strategy. Poirier, who previously focused on M&A at AMD and Xilinx, will report directly to CEO Pat Gelsinger. This move comes as Intel actively pursues acquisitions, including recent deals for Tower Semiconductor and Siru Innovations, and highlights the growing importance of M&A for Intel's expansion in various technology sectors.

title: Pave the Way for Next Wireless Breakthroughs
summary: This article discusses the accelerating pace of wireless innovation, focusing on the design and test challenges posed by next-generation wireless communication systems like 5G and satellite communications. It highlights key technological components such as wider bandwidths, higher-order modulation schemes, and multi-antenna techniques, and emphasizes the need for scalable and accurate test solutions to overcome these challenges. The author, Eric Hsu of Keysight Technologies Inc., explains how these advancements impact device performance and require sophisticated testing methods.

title: Nvidia pays $5.5m SEC fine over crypto-mining disclosures
summary: Nvidia agreed to pay a $5.5 million fine to the SEC for failing to adequately disclose revenue from crypto mining during two quarters in 2018. The SEC stated that Nvidia omitted information about surging demand from crypto miners while discussing how digital assets impacted other business lines. This lack of disclosure made Nvidia's financial results less predictable for investors.

title: Ex-Cisco chief John Chambers says Silicon Valley's in trouble, but it's not too late to fix things
summary: Former Cisco Systems CEO John Chambers believes Silicon Valley and the U.S. are entering one of their most difficult periods in his 46-year tech career. Despite the challenges, Chambers remains optimistic that solutions can be found. He shared these views with the Silicon Valley Business Journal, highlighting concerns for the region and the nation.

title: WD Announces WD Black SN850X and P40 Game Drive
summary: Western Digital has unveiled two new additions to its WD_BLACK gaming lineup: the SN850X NVMe SSD and the P40 Game Drive SSD. The SN850X is an internal PCIe Gen4 SSD optimized for performance with speeds up to 7,300 MB/s, while the P40 is a portable external SSD featuring a USB 3.2 Gen2x2 interface and customizable RGB lighting. Both products aim to enhance the gaming experience with fast load times and improved storage options.

title: Why Marvell bought interconnect upstart Tanzanite
summary: Marvell Technology has acquired Tanzanite, a CXL startup specializing in memory expansion and pooling, to accelerate its composable infrastructure ambitions. The acquisition aims to disaggregate compute, memory, and storage resources, allowing them to be composed on the fly for memory-intensive workloads like AI/ML. While achieving fully composable data centers will take time, Marvell believes CXL technology will be crucial for integrating accelerators across its product portfolio and overcoming latency challenges associated with extending CXL benefits beyond server chassis.

title: AMD Announces Three New Radeon RX 6000 Series Graphics Cards and First Games Adding Support for AMD FidelityFX Super Resolution 2.0
summary: AMD has announced three new Radeon RX 6000 Series graphics cards: the RX 6950 XT, RX 6750 XT, and RX 6650 XT, all built on the RDNA 2 architecture with enhanced features for improved gaming performance across various resolutions. The company also revealed the first games that will support AMD FidelityFX Super Resolution (FSR) 2.0, a temporal upscaling technology designed to boost framerates while maintaining native-like image quality, with DEATHLOOP being the first to receive support on May 12, 2022. Additionally, AMD introduced an updated "Raise the Game Bundle" offering complimentary games with the purchase of eligible Radeon RX 6000 Series products.

title: SAP CEO Christian Klein: Revolutionizing the Next 50 Years of Intelligent Sustainable Innovation
summary: SAP CEO Christian Klein opened SAP Sapphire Orlando, celebrating the company's 50-year history and outlining a vision for the future focused on intelligent, networked, and sustainable enterprises. He highlighted the importance of SAP's purpose in a world facing geopolitical tensions and supply chain disruptions, emphasizing the solutions like RISE with SAP and SAP Business Network. Klein stressed sustainability as a business imperative, introducing solutions that help organizations measure and act on ESG factors, including a new "green ledger" to account for carbon就像 financials.

title: Taiwan’s Semiconductor Production in Japan is Significant for Business and Human Rights in Taiwan
summary: TSMC's new joint venture plant in Kumamoto, Japan, is a significant development not only for semiconductor production but also for the integration of business and human rights principles in Taiwanese companies. The article highlights the unique aspects of this partnership, including investments from Sony and Denso, and emphasizes Japan's proactive stance on human rights due diligence. It argues that TSMC should leverage this collaboration to bring crucial human rights expertise back to Taiwan, especially since Taiwan lacks access to UN-led technical support, to ensure Taiwanese businesses remain competitive in global markets with increasing corporate responsibility expectations.

title: Intel wants to add a new Licking County factory "every year or two," CEO says
summary: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger announced plans to open a new factory in Licking County "every year or two" as part of a $100 billion investment to make the area a major semiconductor hub. This ambitious expansion hinges on Congress passing the $52 billion CHIPS Act, which Gelsinger deems essential for competitive U.S. chip manufacturing. The project aims to rebuild the U.S. semiconductor sector and will require significant workforce development and sustained support from Ohio.

title: Broadcom Vs. Marvell: Better Chipmaker Company To Buy (NASDAQ:AVGO)
summary: This article compares Broadcom (AVGO) and Marvell (MRVL) primarily focusing on their semiconductor businesses. It concludes that Marvell has an edge in ASICs and processor technology, while Broadcom excels in analog business and demonstrates a superior M&A strategy with better revenue contribution and margins. The analysis aims to determine the better chipmaker company to buy.

title: Micron Teases 232-Layer NAND For Next-Gen Storage, Lays Out Roadmap To 500 Layers
summary: Micron announced its development of 232-layer NAND flash memory, aiming for production ramp-up by the end of the year. This "world's most advanced" NAND solution, utilizing double-stacked 3D memory cells and CMOS under array technology, is expected to enhance density, power efficiency, and bandwidth for next-generation SSDs. Micron also revealed a roadmap to eventually reach 500-layer NAND and beyond, with a focus on extending its quad-level cell leadership.

title: Intel shareholders reject executive pay
summary: Intel shareholders have rejected the company's executive compensation package in an advisory vote. While not immediately impacting executive pay, the decision signals investor scrutiny on CEO Pat Gelsinger's turnaround plan and follows a trend of shareholders voting against executive compensation, given Gelsinger's substantial 2021 pay package and Intel's share performance. Intel stated that it takes stockholder feedback seriously and is working to address concerns regarding compensation transparency and linking pay to performance.

title: 7 Cheap Semiconductor Stocks to Buy Now
summary: This article identifies seven semiconductor stocks that are considered "cheap" despite their strong growth potential, primarily due to a recent market selloff. It uses the PEG ratio (price/earnings-to-growth) as a key metric to highlight undervaluation, recommending companies like AMD, AOSL, MU, ASX, COHU, VSH, and AMKR. The author argues that these stocks offer long-term buying opportunities due to their expected earnings growth and strong cash flow, even in a bear market.

title: Here's why Pensando agreed to $1.9B AMD deal, according to Chairman John Chambers
summary: Pensando's acquisition by AMD for $1.9 billion helped solve a significant supply chain issue for the rapidly growing Milpitas-based company, according to its chairman, John Chambers. Chambers, the former CEO of Cisco Systems, indicated that the deal addressed a critical challenge Pensando was facing. This strategic acquisition points to AMD's broader focus on data centers.

title: Baidu’s Chip Affiliate Kunlun Eyes Fundraise at $2.5 Billion Valuation
summary: Baidu's chip affiliate, Kunlun, is seeking to raise 2 billion yuan ($317 million) in a new funding round, which would value the company at $2.5 billion. This initiative reflects a trend in China's venture capital landscape, where investors are pivoting from internet businesses towards core technologies like semiconductors, aligning with Beijing's strategic priorities. If successful, this would be Kunlun's second funding round, increasing its valuation significantly from its previous round.

title: HPE builds 4th HPC factory in Czech Republic
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) is establishing its fourth global high-performance compute (HPC) manufacturing facility in Kutná Hora, Czech Republic, in collaboration with Foxconn. This expansion aims to enhance supply chain resilience and support the production of HPE Apollo systems and Cray Ex supercomputers. HPC has become a significant growth driver for HPE, with its HPC and AI business generating nearly $3 billion in revenue in 2021, and the company has secured major supercomputing contracts globally.

title: Intel, AMD Ban Sees Russia Turn To Chinese Chips
summary: Following the ban on chip shipments from Intel and AMD due to the Ukraine invasion, Russian motherboard manufacturers are now using Chinese microprocessors. Russian firm Dannie introduced a new motherboard featuring Zhaoxin's KaiXian KX-6640MA processor, which utilizes the x86 microarchitecture. This shift highlights Russia's limited options and its reliance on Chinese technology, despite the Chinese chips often being based on older manufacturing processes compared to the latest from Intel and AMD.

title: HP Powers Today’s Hustle to Create Without Limitation
summary: HP has launched new Spectre and Envy laptops designed for today's hybrid world and the growing creator economy. These devices emphasize seamless collaboration and powerful performance, featuring high-resolution cameras, AI-based audio/visual enhancements, and intelligent power management. The lineup aims to offer diverse options for creators, from enhanced displays to cross-device compatibility via HP Palette, all while incorporating sustainable materials.

title: TSMC to begin R&D for 1.4nm chip technology
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) is reportedly intensifying its research and development efforts for sub-2 nanometer chip manufacturing processes, specifically targeting 1.4nm technology. The company plans to reassign its 3nm R&D team to this new endeavor starting in June. Commercial products utilizing TSMC's 1.4nm process could emerge by 2028, following the company's historical three-year cadence for new node introductions.

title: AMD Showcases Industry-Leading Gaming, Commercial, and Mainstream PC Technologies at COMPUTEX 2022
summary: At COMPUTEX 2022, AMD unveiled its upcoming Ryzen 7000 Series desktop processors with the new "Zen 4" architecture, promising significant performance gains and launching in Fall 2022. The company also announced "Mendocino" mobile processors for the budget-friendly segment, new details on the AM5 platform, and SmartAccess Storage technology for faster game loading. These innovations underscore AMD's continued commitment to advancing high-performance computing across gaming, commercial, and mainstream PC markets.

title: Corsair's First Gaming Laptop Has Mech Keyboard & Touch Bar
summary: Corsair has launched its first gaming laptop, the Corsair Voyager a1600, featuring a mechanical keyboard with Cherry MX switches and a unique "macro bar" similar to Apple's Touch Bar. The laptop boasts a 16-inch QHD+ 240Hz screen, Radeon RX 6800M graphics, and a Ryzen 9 6900HS processor. It also integrates Corsair's Slipstream wireless adapter and utilizes software compatible with the Elgato Stream Deck for its macro bar functionality.

title: Nvidia Q1 FY2023 Earnings Report Preview: What To Look For
summary: Nvidia (NVDA) is set to report Q1 FY 2023 earnings, with analysts expecting growth in adjusted EPS and revenue, albeit at a slower pace. Investors will closely watch data center revenue, which is projected to accelerate and potentially surpass gaming revenue for the first time in FY 2023. The company is investing in AI chips to continue its growth trajectory, building on demand fueled by the pandemic.

title: Semiconductor ETF | ESG-screened investment in tech
summary: The VanEck Semiconductor UCITS ETF offers investors exposure to the semiconductor sector, targeting pure-play companies with at least half their revenues from semiconductors. It is Europe's first Semiconductor ETF, designed to capture growth from the "fourth industrial revolution" driven by semiconductor advancements, and is classified as SFDR Article 8 for promoting environmental and social characteristics. The ETF focuses on liquid semiconductor stocks globally and has a strategy that has been successful in the US since 2011, with $25 billion in AUM.

title: Qualcomm maintains multiple sourcing strategy
summary: Qualcomm Inc. has stated it will maintain a multi-sourcing strategy for its chips, including advanced chips from two major suppliers, to ensure a stable supply amidst the pandemic and a slowing global economy. The company anticipates continued strong growth in 5G penetration and expects Wi-Fi 7 technology adoption to rapidly increase, with end products equipped with these chips by the end of this year.

title: Where Does the Supply Chain Crisis Stand Now?
summary: The article discusses the ongoing global supply chain crisis, examining its causes, current impact across various sectors like semiconductors, automobiles, industrials, retail, and restaurants, and potential timelines for recovery. It highlights that while some improvements are being seen, new bottlenecks, such as the war in Ukraine and lockdowns in China, continue to pose challenges, pushing companies to adapt strategies like diversifying supply chains and securing long-term contracts.

title: Next-Generation Distributed Static Timing Analysis On The Cloud
summary: The article discusses the increasing challenge of chip complexity on Electronic Design Automation (EDA) tools, especially Static Timing Analysis (STA). It highlights that parallelism, particularly distributed STA in the cloud, is the only scalable solution to accelerate analysis and sign-off processes. The Synopsys PrimeTime HyperGrid technology is presented as a solution that provides accurate, distributed STA on the cloud, achieving up to 10x speedup and memory reduction through automatic partitioning.

title: Purdue launches nation’s first comprehensive Semiconductor Degrees Program
summary: Purdue University has launched the nation's first comprehensive Semiconductor Degrees Program (SDP) to address the critical need for 50,000 trained semiconductor engineers in the U.S. within the next five years. This innovative program offers a multidisciplinary curriculum across various credentials, including master's degrees, certificates, and minors, with flexible online and residential options. The initiative is strongly supported by industry leaders and government efforts like the CHIPS Act, aiming to reassert American leadership in the semiconductor industry.

title: Ampere Roadmap Has Four Future Arm Server Chips
summary: Ampere Computing has outlined its future Arm server chip roadmap, revealing plans for three new processors beyond the recently announced AmpereOne ("Siryn") chip, extending through 2025. The company emphasizes its agile development approach and customer-driven roadmap, focusing on a continuous cadence of releases rather than yearly changes, with hyperscalers influencing chip features and lifespan. The roadmap details expected advancements in core count, process technology (moving to 5nm and 3nm), memory (DDR5, DDR6), and PCI-Express versions for these upcoming chips, while also hinting at a future shift to chiplet architectures.

title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise Ushers in New Era with World’s First and Fastest Exascale Supercomputer “Frontier” for the U.S. Department of Energy’s Oak Ridge National Laboratory
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) announced that its Frontier supercomputer, built for the U.S. Department of Energy’s Oak Ridge National Laboratory (ORNL), has become the world's first exascale supercomputer and fastest overall, reaching 1.1 exaflops. Frontier also leads in energy efficiency, ranking number one on the Green500 list. This breakthrough in supercomputing promises to accelerate scientific discoveries, AI advancements, and tackle complex problems up to 10 times faster than current top supercomputers.

title: Best semiconductor ETFs in the UK 2023
