This new chip offers significant performance and energy efficiency improvements over its predecessor, translating into lower operational costs for enterprises and cloud data centers. The launch comes as rival Intel faces delays with its own data center chip, and AMD continues to gain market share, boosting its stock by about 14%.

title: Micron Ships Data Center-Class DDR5, Ready For New AMD EPYC CPUs
summary: Micron Technology announced it is shipping its new data center-class DDR5 SDRAM memory in volume, coinciding with the release of AMD's new Zen 4 EPYC processors. This transition from DDR4 to DDR5 is described as a "once-in-a-decade change," crucial for handling the increasing data volumes and performance demands of modern data centers. The new DDR5 memory offers increased bandwidth, more channels, and improved power efficiency compared to its predecessor.

title: HPE's low-end supercomputers take aim at the AI market
summary: HPE and Dell are introducing new supercomputing and high-performance computing (HPC) systems designed to meet the growing demands of AI-driven workloads. HPE is focusing on smaller, lower-cost supercomputers with advanced cooling, while Dell is enhancing its HPC offerings with powerful GPUs, aiming to make supercomputing capabilities more accessible to a broader range of enterprise users. This convergence of supercomputing and HPC is blurring the lines between the two, as both companies compete to provide infrastructure that can efficiently handle complex AI, modeling, and simulation tasks.

title: AMD announces its reprogrammable Versal AI chips are ready for space-based deployments
summary: AMD's reprogrammable Versal AI Core chip has achieved Class B qualification, making it suitable for space-based deployments in commercial satellites. This radiation-resistant SoC enables significant advancements in onboard AI processing for satellites, offering greater performance and reconfigurability compared to traditional ASICs. Raytheon Co. is an early adopter, planning to use these chips for its next-generation space processors.

title: AMD Announces Completion of Class B Qualification for First Space-Grade Versal Adaptive SoCs Enabling On-Board AI Processing in Space
summary: AMD has announced the completion of Class B qualification for its first space-grade Versal™ adaptive SoCs, the XQR Versal AI Core XQRVC1902 devices. These radiation-tolerant chips are designed for on-board AI processing and high-bandwidth signal processing in satellites and space applications, with shipments expected to begin in early 2023. This development offers a more flexible and cost-effective alternative to custom ASICs for complex AI applications in space, allowing for in-flight reprogramming and adaptation to harsh radiation environments.

title: AMD Powers Aisin Next-Generation Automated Parking Assist System
summary: AMD announced that its Xilinx Automotive (XA) Zynq UltraScale+ MPSoC platform will power Aisin's next-generation Automated Parking-Assist (APA) system, set to ship in model year 2024 vehicles. The platform's deep learning processor and high-performance capabilities enable low-latency AI-based image processing for pedestrian and vehicle detection, allowing the APA system to autonomously park vehicles and perform emergency braking. This collaboration highlights AMD's growing role in the automotive industry, providing adaptable solutions for advanced driver-assistance and autonomous driving applications.

title: AMD chip to power auto supplier Aisin’s new automated car parking system
summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) announced that its Zynq UltraScale+ MPSoC microprocessor will power a new automated parking system developed by Japan's Aisin Corp. This system aims to make automated parking more mainstream by offering a cost-effective solution for automakers. The use of AMD's FPGA-based chip, acquired from Xilinx, will allow for post-sale software and chip updates in vehicles, with production expected to begin on 2024 models.

title: AMD and Analog Devices Resolve Patent Infringement Lawsuits
summary: AMD and Analog Devices, Inc. have announced the resolution of all their ongoing patent litigations, based on mutually agreed-upon terms. As a result, the two companies plan to collaborate on technology to develop next-generation solutions for their communications and data center customers.

title: Intel FakeCatcher detects deepfakes with a 96% accuracy rate
summary: Intel has launched FakeCatcher, a deepfake detection platform that boasts a 96% accuracy rate and provides real-time results. Unlike other solutions, FakeCatcher identifies genuine human characteristics like subtle blood flow signals in video pixels to distinguish real content from AI-generated fakes. This technology aims to restore trust in media and can be used by news organizations, social media platforms, and non-profit groups to combat the spread of manipulated videos.

title: Competition gets heated on Fungible’s DPU patch
summary: Fungible, a data processing unit (DPU) startup founded in 2015, is facing intense competition from major players like AMD, Intel, Kalray, Marvell, and Nvidia. Despite developing impressive DPU chips and acquiring composability software, Fungible has struggled to secure major customers or OEM deals and recently pivoted away from composable systems to focus on its storage technology. The company reportedly tried to raise more funding unsuccessfully and is now in a precarious position amidst rumors of acquisition.

title: Exxact Corp. Works with NVIDIA to Support Omniverse Enterprise
summary: Exxact Corporation, a high-performance computing provider, is now offering integrated solutions to the media and entertainment industry to support NVIDIA Omniverse Enterprise. This collaboration provides studios with workstations powered by NVIDIA RTX GPUs and Omniverse Enterprise, enabling real-time collaborative 3D scene creation and simulation. Exxact aims to enhance productivity, communication, and creativity for M&E clients, particularly as remote production models become more prevalent.

title: Intel Foundry head steps down, as contract chip making division tries to ramp up
summary: Randhir Thakur, the head of Intel's Foundry business, has resigned to pursue other opportunities, a move that comes amidst company-wide layoffs and spending cuts. He will remain until early 2023 to assist with the leadership transition and the acquisition of Tower Semiconductor. This departure impacts Intel's efforts to grow its contract chip-making division, which faces stiff competition from established leaders like TSMC and Samsung, despite securing major customer wins.

title: Samsung Secures 3-nm Advanced Chip Orders From NVIDIA, Qualcomm – Report
summary: Samsung Foundry has reportedly secured orders from major firms like NVIDIA, Qualcomm, IBM, and Baidu to manufacture advanced 3-nanometer chips. This move, leveraging Samsung's gate-all-around (GAA) transistor technology developed with IBM, aims to position Samsung ahead of TSMC in advanced chip manufacturing and diversify the semiconductor supply chain amidst geopolitical tensions. Deliveries in large quantities are expected to begin in early 2024.

title: Microcontrollers learn to embrace neural networks
summary: Deep learning is increasingly moving to constrained edge systems like microcontrollers due to concerns about latency, bandwidth, and privacy with cloud-based processing. Chipmakers such as NXP and Silicon Labs are integrating machine learning accelerators into their microcontrollers to improve efficiency, while Microchip Technology explores future acceleration options. The article also discusses the potential future role of Transformer architectures in microcontroller-based AI, despite their current complexity.

title: Silicon Labs CEO Matt Johnson Elected Chair of SIA
summary: Silicon Labs President and CEO Matt Johnson has been elected Chair of the Semiconductor Industry Association (SIA) Board of Directors, with Texas Instruments Chairman, President, and CEO Rich Templeton elected as Vice Chair. They will lead the SIA in 2023, focusing on implementing the CHIPS and Science Act and promoting government policies that support the semiconductor ecosystem. Both leaders bring significant industry experience and will advocate for the industry's interests in Washington and globally.

title: A Rising Star in the Biden Administration Faces a $100 Billion Test
summary: Commerce Secretary Gina Raimondo is spearheading a massive industrial policy initiative, distributing nearly $100 billion to bolster the U.S. semiconductor industry and expand broadband access. This effort aims to enhance American competitiveness in future technologies like AI and secure chip supplies, while also navigating a growing economic rivalry with China. Her success in this endeavor will significantly impact the future of the U.S. economy.

title: Supermicro W790 E-ATX Motherboard Leaks Out: Sapphire Rapids-WS CPU Support, 16 ‘8-Channel’ DDR5 DIMMs, Lots of PCIe Gen5 Slots
summary: A Supermicro W790 E-ATX motherboard supporting Intel's Sapphire Rapids-WS Xeon Workstation CPUs has leaked, revealing key features. The X13SWA-TF motherboard includes an LGA 4677 socket, 16 DDR5 DIMM slots with 8-channel memory support, and six PCIe Gen5 x16 slots. This new platform, known as Fishhawk Falls, is anticipated for workstation and HEDT PC builds, with CPU launches expected by early 2023.

title: STMicro Unveils 6-axis IMU with Embedded Sensor Fusion and AI
summary: STMicroelectronics has released a new 6-axis IMU, the LSM6DSV16X, designed for low-power sensing applications like AR/VR and wearables. This IMU incorporates embedded sensor fusion blocks and Machine Learning cores to efficiently handle and aggregate data from multiple sensors. The aim is to offload compute-intensive tasks from application processors, enabling more practical and lower-power sensing solutions.

title: President Joe Biden to visit TSMC plant being built in north Phoenix
summary: President Joe Biden is scheduled to visit Phoenix on December 6 to commemorate a key milestone, the "first tool-in," at the Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) plant in north Phoenix. This marks his first presidential visit to Arizona and highlights the administration's focus on boosting domestic semiconductor manufacturing through initiatives like the CHIPS and Science Act. The TSMC facility represents a significant $12 billion investment in Arizona, aiming to reduce the country's reliance on foreign microchip production.

title: TSMC to make higher-end chips in US: sources
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC) plans to produce advanced 4-nanometer chips at its new Arizona plant when it opens in 2024, an upgrade from initial plans, following pressure from US customers like Apple. The company is also expected to commit to a second plant for even more advanced 3-nanometer chips. This move aligns with US efforts to bolster domestic semiconductor manufacturing due to supply chain disruptions and geopolitical concerns, though Taiwan intends to keep its latest technology at home.

title: Micron Technology to invest RM4.4bil on 2nd factory in Penang
summary: Micron Technology announced a new investment of US$1 billion (RM4.4 billion) to build a second factory in Penang, adding to its existing US$1 billion investment since 2019. This expansion will bring their total factory space to 1.5 million sq ft and is expected to begin production next year. CEO Sanjay Mehrotra highlights the company's strong position in the semiconductor market despite current industry challenges, emphasizing the critical role of its Penang operations in its global network.

title: Intel says it will squeeze 1 trillion transistors onto a chip package by 2030
summary: Intel's researchers have revealed innovations in chip packaging that could lead to chips with over a trillion transistors by 2030, significantly extending Moore's Law. This development would result in computer chips ten times more powerful than current advanced silicon. The company aims to address the increasing demand for computing power driven by data consumption and AI.

title: Lattice Extends Low Power Leadership with New Lattice Avant FPGA Platform
summary: Lattice Semiconductor has launched Lattice Avant, a new FPGA platform designed to extend its low-power leadership into mid-range FPGAs. This platform offers enhanced power efficiency, advanced connectivity, and optimized computing capabilities, targeting applications in Communications, Computing, Industrial, and Automotive markets. The Avant platform, beginning with the Avant-E family, aims to double Lattice's addressable market and accelerate customer designs with superior power efficiency and performance.

title: TSMC Announces Updates for TSMC Arizona
summary: TSMC announced significant updates for its Arizona fabs, including starting construction on a second fab for 3nm production by 2026, bringing the total investment to approximately US$40 billion. The two fabs are projected to create 10,000 high-paying jobs and produce over 600,000 wafers annually. TSMC also plans an on-site Industrial Water Reclamation Plant for near zero liquid discharge, aiming for the greenest semiconductor manufacturing facility in the US.

title: Lattice Launches Avant FPGA Platform, Avant-E Parts for Mid-Range FPGA Edge Processing Tasks
summary: Lattice Semiconductor has introduced its new Avant FPGA platform, featuring the Avant-E chips designed for mid-range edge processing applications. Lattice claims significant improvements in power efficiency, performance, and connectivity over competitors, including 2.5x better power consumption and double the performance. The Avant-E FPGAs offer up to 477k logic cells and extensive DSPs, targeting on-device machine learning and AI workloads, with full support from Lattice's existing software tools.

title: TSMC triples Arizona chip plant investment, Biden hails project
summary: Taiwanese chipmaker TSMC announced it would more than triple its investment in its new Arizona plant to $40 billion, marking one of the largest foreign investments in U.S. history. President Joe Biden praised the project as a move to bring American manufacturing back and strengthen the U.S. supply chain for semiconductors. The expanded investment includes building a second facility for advanced 3-nanometer chips by 2026, aiming to create 13,000 high-tech jobs and address semiconductor shortages.

title: Apple to be TSMC US fab top customer
summary: Apple Inc. is set to become the largest customer of TSMC's new factories in Arizona, as announced by Apple CEO Tim Cook. TSMC will invest $40 billion in the US fabs, including a second plant for more advanced 3-nanometer chips, with production slated to start in 2024 for 4-nanometer chips and 2026 for 3-nanometer chips. Other major chip companies like AMD and Qualcomm also plan to source chips from these new US facilities, highlighting the growing importance of domestic semiconductor manufacturing.

title: ST SiC power modules for Hyundai EV platform ...
summary: STMicroelectronics' SiC-MOSFET based power modules have been chosen by Hyundai Motor Company for its E-GMP electric vehicle platform, specifically powering the Kia EV6. These modules offer flexible power ratings, support common EV traction operating voltages, and feature robust sintering technology for integration. ST's third-generation SiC technology aims to provide high power density, energy efficiency, and improved vehicle performance, range, and charge time.

title: The 10 Hottest Semiconductor Startup Companies Of 2022
summary: Despite a turbulent year for the semiconductor industry, driven by macroeconomic hurdles, the market for AI, ML, high-power computing, cloud, and intelligent edge business is thriving. This article highlights 10 semiconductor startups making significant strides in 2022. These companies are attracting substantial investments and developing innovative solutions for next-generation technologies.

title: TSMC Revenue Rises 50% in November Despite Covid Challenges
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) reported a 50% increase in revenue for November, reaching NT$222.7 billion ($7.3 billion). This growth occurred despite a challenging environment marked by declining consumer electronics demand and COVID-19 disruptions in China. TSMC, the world's largest contract chipmaker and exclusive supplier of Apple's Silicon chips, continues a strong sales streak amplified by pandemic-driven demand.

title: AMD Radeon RX 7900 XT Review - Circuit Board Analysis
summary: This article provides a detailed circuit board analysis of the AMD Radeon RX 7900 XT, focusing on its power delivery systems for the GPU and memory. It identifies specific components like the Monolithic Power Systems controllers and DrMOS, and details the Hynix GDDR6 memory chips. The review also highlights AMD's Navi 31 graphics processor, which utilizes a chiplet architecture with a Graphics Compute Die (GCD) and Memory Cache Dies (MCDs).

title: New business to bring more jobs to Plainview
summary: A new joint venture called DSM Semichem LLC. is set to begin construction on a facility in Plainview, Texas, in January, which will produce electronic sulfuric acid (ELSA) and create new jobs. This development is a result of Samsung's investment in a Texas facility and its need for local resources. The Plainview location will be the first U.S. facility for Dongjin Semichem Co., Ltd., and its operations are anticipated to begin after January 1, with full operation planned by the end of 2024.

title: New Intel oneAPI 2023 Tools Maximize Value of Upcoming Intel Hardware
summary: Intel has released its oneAPI 2023 tools, designed to maximize the value of upcoming Intel hardware such as 4th Gen Intel Xeon Scalable processors, Intel Xeon CPU Max Series, and Intel Data Center GPUs. These tools offer enhanced performance and productivity, cross-platform compatibility through SYCL code for non-Intel GPUs, and improved support for AI and machine learning frameworks. The update aims to address the growing need for efficient multi-architecture programming in a developer landscape increasingly targeting heterogeneous systems.

title: Red-Hot Solar Stock Earns 'Top Pick' Status
summary: Goldman Sachs has named SolarEdge Technologies Inc (NASDAQ:SEDG) as a top pick for 2023 in the solar energy industry, alongside First Solar (FSLR). This endorsement comes despite the stock being down 1.1% recently, as SEDG has shown a 37% gain this quarter and boasts strong support from its 20-day moving average. Options traders have also been overwhelmingly bullish on SEDG in the past two months, as indicated by a high call/put volume ratio.

title: Supermicro targets cloud-native workloads with new Arm-based Mt. Hamilton servers
summary: Super Micro Computer Inc. has announced an expanded lineup of Arm-based servers, dubbed Mt. Hamilton, powered by Ampere Computing LLC’s Altra and Altra Max processors. These new MegaDC servers are designed for cloud-native workloads, offering high performance per watt for applications like cloud gaming, video-on-demand, and infrastructure-as-a-service. Leveraging a single motherboard design and featuring Ampere's Arm-based CPUs with up to 128 cores, Supermicro aims to provide customers with more choices for scalable, low-latency applications.

title: What TSMC CEO CC Wei says about the semiconductor industry (1)
summary: TSMC CEO CC Wei delivered a speech where he discussed new challenges in the semiconductor industry, emphasizing the critical need for talent and a distinct business strategy from founder Morris Chang. Wei highlighted the shift towards regional division of labor and the global scramble for self-sufficient semiconductor ecosystems, while asserting that there are no shortcuts to developing semiconductor capabilities. He stressed the importance of collaboration and warned against the risks of attempting to leapfrog technological advancements without sufficient resources.

title: Despite Billions in Federal Subsidies, Chipmakers Are Laying Off Thousands of Workers
summary: Despite significant federal subsidies from the CHIPS Act, major semiconductor manufacturers like Micron Technology, Intel, AMD, Nvidia, and Qualcomm are implementing layoffs or hiring freezes due to slowing demand in key markets. Micron, one of the largest memory chip makers, plans to cut 10% of its workforce, impacting 4,800 employees, citing a "dramatic drop in demand" for PC and smartphone chips. While these companies invest heavily in new facilities to leverage tax breaks, government incentives are not alleviating their short-term financial challenges exacerbated by reduced consumer spending.

title: The bright side of the chip glut: Nvidia, AMD and Intel gaming cards are cheap and very available for Christmas
summary: The article highlights that due to a chip glut, gaming cards from Nvidia, AMD, and Intel are now readily available and significantly discounted, making them an affordable option for Christmas shoppers. This marks a significant change from the previous two years when these items were scarce and expensive. The availability addresses past disappointments for gamers hoping for these cards during the holidays.

title: Micron Technology Sees Chip Recovery by the End of 2023
summary: Micron Technology Inc. stock has fallen significantly in 2022 due to weak demand for DRAM chips and an oversupply in the market. Despite a challenging fiscal Q1 2023 with missed earnings and lowered guidance, Micron expects improving customer inventories to lead to increased revenue in the fiscal second half of 2023. Analysts have revised their ratings and price targets, with some maintaining overweight or buy ratings despite the current downturn.

title: Applied Materials Inc. stock outperforms competitors despite losses on the day
summary: Shares of Applied Materials Inc. dropped 1.17% to $94.23 on Wednesday, marking its fourth consecutive day of losses, despite outperforming the broader S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average. The stock closed significantly below its 52-week high, although it performed better than several key competitors like Broadcom, Qualcomm, and Advanced Micro Devices. Trading volume was also below its 50-day average.

title: TSMC starts mass-producing three-nanometer chips
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. Ltd. (TSMC) has begun mass-producing processors using its advanced three-nanometer chip technology. This new technology offers a 15% improvement in chip performance and up to 35% less power consumption compared to its earlier five-nanometer process. TSMC, a major contract chip manufacturer for companies like Apple, AMD, and Nvidia, plans to develop multiple versions of this technology and is building new fabrication plants in Arizona.

title: Sales of computer graphics cards slump to lowest level in two decades
summary: Sales of computer graphics cards hit a 20-year low in Q3 2022 due to a global economic slowdown and the impact of the Ethereum merge on cryptocurrency mining. Major chipmakers like AMD and Nvidia experienced significant declines in discrete desktop GPU shipments, with AMD particularly hard hit. Despite the slump, new high-end GPU launches and Intel's entry into the market (grabbing a 4% share) offer potential for future sales boosts.

title: GeForce RTX 40 Series Studio Laptops, Omniverse Updates Accelerate AI-Powered Content Creation
summary: NVIDIA announced new GeForce RTX 40 Series Studio Laptops and updates to its Omniverse platform, designed to accelerate AI-powered content creation workflows. These developments, showcased at CES, include improvements to Studio software like Broadcast and Canvas, the introduction of RTX Video Super Resolution, and new GeForce RTX 4070 Ti graphics cards for desktops. The article highlights how these technologies empower artists with significant performance boosts in 3D, video, and AI-driven creative tasks, facilitating real-time collaboration and faster content delivery.

title: The War Over the World’s Most Critical Technology: A Conversation with Chris Miller
summary: This article features a conversation with Chris Miller, author of "Chip War," discussing the geopolitical importance of semiconductors, particularly in the U.S.-China rivalry. It explores Taiwan's critical role in advanced chip manufacturing through TSMC, the potential risks of a conflict over Taiwan, and the challenges faced by the U.S. and China in reducing their reliance on Taiwanese chip production.

title: AMD Unveils Suite of New Radeon GPUs to Power High-Performance, Power-Efficient Gaming Laptops
summary: AMD announced its new Radeon RX 7000 Series Graphics for Laptops at CES 2023, built on the RDNA 3 architecture for exceptional energy efficiency and performance. This includes the Radeon RX 7000M Series for high performance-per-watt and the Radeon RX 7000S Series optimized for thin-and-light laptops. These new GPUs aim to deliver high-framerate gaming at ultra settings and support advanced features like dedicated AI and raytracing hardware, AV1 encoding, and AMD FidelityFX Super Resolution technology.

title: CES 2023: AMD seeks to attract Nvidia’s customers with advanced new AI chips
summary: AMD announced its new Instinct MI300 chip at CES 2023, aiming to compete with Nvidia in the AI market. The chip combines a CPU and GPU to accelerate the intensive process of developing artificial intelligence models in data centers. AMD also highlighted new energy-efficient laptops capable of extended video playback.

title: CES 2023: AMD, Nvidia, auto applications get the hype, but analysts say this one chip maker ruled
summary: At CES 2023, while companies like Advanced Micro Devices Inc. and Nvidia Corp. received significant attention, analysts suggest that a lower-profile chip maker is better positioned post-convention. Morgan Stanley analyst Joseph Moore noted ongoing caution regarding overall chip demand, particularly due to softness in China, but highlighted autos as a strong theme at the event.

title: Intel Launches 4th Gen Xeon Scalable Processors, Max Series CPUs and GPUs
summary: Intel has launched its 4th Gen Xeon Scalable processors, along with the Intel Xeon CPU Max Series and Intel Data Center GPU Max Series. These new processors aim to deliver significant improvements in performance, efficiency, and capabilities for AI, cloud, network, edge, and high-performance computing, emphasizing built-in accelerators and sustainability features. The company highlights broad industry adoption and performance gains across various key workloads.

title: Nvidia debuts new DGX H100 systems powered by Intel’s 4th Gen Intel Xeon Scalable chips
summary: Nvidia has unveiled new DGX H100 systems that integrate its H100 Tensor Core GPUs with Intel's recently launched 4th Gen Intel Xeon Scalable processors. These systems, along with upcoming server offerings from Nvidia's partners, promise significantly enhanced efficiency and performance for AI workloads, boasting up to 25 times more efficiency and 3.5 times better AI training/inference efficiency compared to previous generation machines. The new hardware leverages PCIe Gen 5 for faster data transfer and includes a free license for Nvidia Enterprise AI software.

title: AMD Announces CFO Transition
summary: AMD announced that its Chief Financial Officer and Treasurer, Devinder Kumar, will retire, with Jean Hu set to join as the new Executive Vice President and CFO on January 23, 2023. Kumar will remain with the company through April 2023 to ensure a smooth transition. Jean Hu previously served as CFO at Marvell and brings over 20 years of financial leadership experience in the semiconductor industry.

title: AMD poaches Marvell CFO Jean Hu to replace its retiring longtime fiscal chief
summary: Jean Hu, previously CFO at Marvell Technology, is moving to Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) to take over as CFO. This transition follows the retirement of AMD's longtime fiscal chief, creating a significant change in leadership for both companies. The move highlights a key personnel change in the semiconductor industry.

title: March 3rd Options Now Available For Advanced Micro Devices (AMD)
summary: New options contracts for Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) with a March 3rd expiration date are now available. Investors can consider a put contract at a $68.00 strike price with a current bid of $4.50, potentially leading to a 6.62% return if it expires worthless. Alternatively, a covered call strategy using a $72.00 strike price contract with a current bid of $3.70 could yield a 10.32% return if the stock is called away.

title: AMD is making history for Asian women executives
summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. is making history by appointing Jean Hu as CFO, resulting in an S&P 500 company led by women of Asian descent in the Chair, CEO, and CFO roles. This move is significant given the barriers Asian-American women face in reaching C-suite positions. Hu will transition into her role starting Jan. 23, succeeding Devinder Kumar.

title: How Both Taiwan and the US Can Benefit from TSMC's Investment
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) is increasing its investment in the U.S. to $40 billion, building a second factory in Phoenix, Arizona. This move, supported by the CHIPS and Science Act, aims to strengthen the U.S. domestic semiconductor industry and diversify global supply chains. Experts suggest that while complete self-sufficiency for the U.S. might be unrealistic, this investment helps address critical dependencies, enhances national security, and fosters innovation in chip manufacturing.

title: Taiwan ASE plant listed as 'lighthouse factory' by WEF
summary: Advanced Semiconductor Engineering Inc (ASE) has been recognized as a "lighthouse factory" by the World Economic Forum (WEF) for its Kaohsiung bumping factory. The factory was inducted into the WEF's Global Lighthouse Network due to its adoption of Fourth Industrial Revolution (4IR) technologies, specifically AI-enabled processes. These innovations led to a 67% increase in output and a 39% reduction in order lead time.

title: Biometric Sensors Market Size to Touch USD 3.5829 million by 2030 According to Acumen Research and Consulting
summary: The global biometric sensors market is projected to grow from USD 1.3195 million in 2021 to USD 3.5829 million by 2030, exhibiting a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 11.9% from 2022 to 2030. This growth is driven by increasing demand for secure authentication, advancements in technology, and wider adoption in various sectors like healthcare, banking, and government. Despite its growth, the market faces challenges due to lack of standardization, high implementation costs, and privacy concerns.

title: Spot On: Texas Instruments Announces First Chipset That Enables Cameras and Sensors to Clean Themselves
summary: Texas Instruments has introduced the first semiconductors with ultrasonic lens cleaning (ULC) technology, enabling camera systems to self-clean dirt, ice, and water using microscopic vibrations. This innovation addresses the problem of dirty camera lenses in various applications, including autonomous vehicles, by offering a cost-effective and compact solution that improves system accuracy and reduces maintenance. The chipset includes the ULC1001 digital signal processor and DRV2901 piezo transducer driver, featuring proprietary algorithms for automatic sensing and cleaning.

title: New AMD Kria ODM Ecosystem Program Helps Developers Quickly Bring Adaptive Solutions to Market
summary: AMD has launched the Kria ODM Partner Ecosystem, an initiative designed to help developers bring adaptive solutions to market faster using production-grade, Kria SOM-based platforms. This program offers turnkey solutions with full software stacks and application support from established partners like Ectron, Optomotive, and VVDN. These pre-built platforms, initially targeting sectors like smart cities and industrial IoT, leverage the adaptability of Kria SOMs and can be customized and branded for rapid deployment.

title: T.J. Rodgers has brought in a pair of chip industry vets to right the ship at Enovix
summary: T.J. Rodgers, executive chairman and major shareholder of Enovix, has brought in new leadership from the chip industry to turn around the struggling battery maker. Raj Talluri, formerly of Qualcomm, has been appointed CEO, tasked with instilling a "silicon mindset" following production setbacks. This move aims to stabilize Enovix's operations and strategy.

title: AMD Adaptive Computing Technology Enables Next-Generation DENSO LiDAR System, Delivering 20X Improvement in Resolution
summary: AMD announced that its adaptive computing technology, specifically the AMD Xilinx Automotive (XA) Zynq UltraScale+ MPSoC, will power DENSO Corporation’s next-generation LiDAR platform. This new platform, set to ship in 2025, promises a 20X improvement in resolution and extremely low latency for enhanced precision in object detection, achieving ISO 26262 ASIL-B certification. The technology enables high point-cloud density in SPAD LiDAR systems, contributing to smaller form factors and allowing for multiple LiDAR implementations in next-generation autonomous vehicles and other applications.

title: Add Exposure to Semiconductor Stocks with These 2 Invesco ETFs
summary: As semiconductor stocks experience a surge, investors can gain exposure to the industry through two Invesco ETFs: the Invesco PHLX Semiconductor ETF (SOXQ) and the Invesco Dynamic Semiconductors ETF (PSI). Both ETFs were up over 4% on the day and approximately 16% year-to-date, driven by positive analyst sentiment and significant gains in companies like Nvidia and AMD. SOXQ and PSI offer exposure to 30 U.S. semiconductor companies, with PSI focusing on investment merit criteria and SOXQ tracking the performance of the largest U.S.-listed semiconductor firms.

title: AMD can keep eating Intel’s lunch, Barclays says as stock surges
summary: Barclays upgraded AMD's stock to overweight from equal weight, leading to a more than 6% surge in AMD shares. Analyst Blayne Curtis believes AMD's Genoa and Bergamo platforms will enable further market-share gains against Intel. The upgrade also suggests AMD could benefit from Meta reaccelerating its spending.

title: Aligning with younger employees’ values is key for Canada’s Top Employers for Young People 2023
summary: Canada's Top Employers for Young People 2023 prioritize aligning with younger generations' values, offering programs in diversity, inclusion, flexibility, and career development. These employers, selected by Mediacorp Canada Inc., invest heavily in students and new graduates, providing opportunities such as internships, mentoring, and tuition subsidies. The article highlights how these organizations set standards for business by catering to the needs and values of Gen Z.

title: Intel shares drop on weak quarterly results and expectations for another quarter of losses
summary: Intel's shares fell after the company reported weaker-than-expected fourth-quarter results and provided a soft forecast for the first quarter of 2023. The chipmaker experienced a significant decline in revenue for the fourth consecutive quarter due to a downturn in PC chip sales and broader economic headwinds. The company also announced a net loss for Q4 and anticipates further losses in Q1, leading to concerns about its financial performance in the near future.

title: The uranium bull market is just getting started
summary: Frank Curzio interviews Amir Adnani, CEO of Uranium Energy Corp. (UEC), discussing why the uranium market is poised for significant growth. Adnani highlights geopolitical shifts, Japan's renewed interest in nuclear power, and the supply/demand imbalance as key drivers. He also details UEC's strategic acquisitions, particularly the Roughrider project, positioning the company as a leading unhedged uranium play.

title: Intel's 'historic collapse' erases $8 billion from market value
summary: Intel Corp saw its market value drop by about $8 billion after it issued dismal earnings projections and a surprise loss forecast for the first quarter, exacerbating fears about a slump in the personal-computer market and slowing data center growth. Analysts view this as a "historic collapse," with Intel losing market share to rivals like AMD due to superior chip technology and price-performance advantages. CEO Pat Gelsinger faces significant challenges in his efforts to restore Intel's dominance, with some analysts suggesting the company may need to cut its dividend due to high capital expenditures.

title: AMD earnings face even more scrutiny after ‘astonishingly bad’ Intel outlook
summary: AMD's upcoming earnings report is under increased scrutiny following Intel's "astonishingly bad" outlook, particularly regarding inventory and data-center issues. CEO Lisa Su had previously expressed confidence that AMD would end 2022 in a stronger position, and the market will now evaluate that claim in light of rival Intel's struggles. AMD is scheduled to report its results after market close on Tuesday.

title: An open-source option is shaking up the microchip industry
summary: RISC-V, an open-standard instruction set architecture developed at UC Berkeley, is rapidly gaining traction in the microchip industry. Unlike proprietary ISAs like x86 and ARM, RISC-V offers flexibility, scalability, and extensibility, allowing companies to design custom, optimized chips for various devices, from laptops to autonomous car systems. This open approach is saving tech companies time and money, and its adoption is expected to continue growing significantly in the coming years.

title: ‘Everybody was wrong’: The $160B memory-chip sector is suffering one of its worst routs ever despite vows to escape the boom-and-bust cycle
summary: The $160 billion memory-chip sector is currently experiencing one of its most severe and unprecedented downturns, despite earlier industry assurances of more predictable earnings. This crisis, marked by chip gluts, plunging prices, and order cuts, is severely impacting major players like SK Hynix Inc. and Micron Technology Inc., destabilizing suppliers, and affecting Asian economies. The industry is responding with production cuts and investment reductions, with consolidation expected, particularly in the NAND market, as companies navigate a complex mix of economic challenges.

title: W3BCLOUD SPAC: Everything you need to know about W3BCLOUD stock
summary: W3BCLOUD, a technology company focused on providing infrastructure for web3 technologies, plans to go public via a SPAC merger with Social Leverage Acquisition Corp I. Despite a "crypto winter" leading to 95% of shares being redeemed by investors and several other crypto companies cancelling SPAC plans, W3BCLOUD is seeking an extension to raise the necessary $150 million for the merger. The company, a joint venture between AMD and ConsenSys, builds and manages decentralized data centers and was most recently valued at $1.25 billion (though this has been adjusted due to redemptions) and aims to expand its global network to support the growing web3 ecosystem.

title: Points of light for Intel Israel
summary: Intel's recent financial results were poor, and its acquisition of Tower Semiconductor faces delays due to US-China tensions. Despite these challenges, there are positive developments for Intel Israel, including the continued success of Mobileye, positive reception for the Sapphire Rapids processor, and the upcoming Lunar Lake processor and a new Kiryat Gat fab.

title: Why AMD is faring much better than Intel in the same tough economy
summary: AMD is showing a much brighter short-term outlook compared to Intel, despite both operating in a difficult chip market. While Intel faces widespread declines, AMD's data center business is experiencing growth, aided by new chip introductions and its acquisition of Xilinx. Both companies are affected by a slumping PC market, but AMD expects to gain market share in that segment and grow its data center revenue at Intel's expense this year.

title: AMD Shares Climb as Server Market Helps Sales Forecast
summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) saw its shares climb after providing a better-than-expected sales forecast for the first quarter. The company's sales in the lucrative server market helped offset a significant decline in demand for PC chips. AMD expects revenue of up to $5.6 billion, surpassing analyst predictions.

title: Data Center Sales Drive AMD's Q4 Resurgence
summary: AMD reported better-than-expected Q4 results, driven by strong data center sales which helped offset declining demand for PC chips. The company forecasts first-quarter revenue of up to $5.6 billion and expects its data center business to continue growing, allowing it to gain market share against rivals like Intel despite an overall chip glut.

title: AMD revenue beats targets, Wall St relieved after Intel's grim outlook
summary: AMD announced revenue that surpassed Wall Street expectations, with its shares rising about 1.5% in after-hours trading, offering relief to investors following a grim outlook from rival Intel. Despite a challenging PC market leading to a 51% drop in client segment revenue for AMD, its Data Center segment saw a 42% increase. CEO Lisa Su expressed confidence in continued market share gains and a stronger second half of the year, contrasting with Intel's struggles in inventory correction and slumping PC business.

title: AMD Ryzen 7000X3D CPUs with 3D V-Cache will start shipping later this month
summary: AMD has announced the pricing and availability for its Ryzen 7000X3D series processors, featuring 3D V-Cache technology to boost performance, especially for gaming. The Ryzen 9 7950X3D and Ryzen 9 7900X3D will launch on February 28, 2023, with the Ryzen 7 7800X3D following on April 6, 2023. AMD claims its flagship chip offers a 10% improvement over Intel's high-end processors for gaming.

title: Austin FC adds sponsors including Chevron, AMD ahead of 2023 season
summary: Austin FC has secured new sponsors, including Chevron and AMD, as it prepares for the 2023 season. The club had a successful 2022, reaching the Western Conference finals and accumulating 107 sponsorships, making it one of Major League Soccer's most valuable teams. This article highlights the team's continued success in attracting corporate partnerships.

title: AMD Ryzen 7040 Phoenix Mobile Specs Show No Sign of PCIe 5.0
summary: AMD's new Ryzen 7040 Series 'Phoenix' mobile processors, designed for ultrathin laptops, will feature PCIe 4.0 with 20 native lanes, lacking support for PCIe 5.0. Although these chips include Zen 4 CPU cores and RDNA 3 graphics, they still provide a full PCIe 4.0 x16 interface for dedicated GPUs and a PCIe 4.0 x4 link for M.2 NVMe storage. The Ryzen 7 7840H, a member of this family, is also noted for its support of both LPDDR5 and LPDDR5x RAM types and up to 256GB of memory.

title: AI-designed Chips Reach Scale with First 100 Commercial Tape-outs Using Synopsys Technology
summary: Synopsys announced that its AI-driven chip design system, Synopsys DSO.ai™, has achieved 100 commercial tape-outs by major semiconductor customers. Companies like STMicroelectronics and SK hynix have seen significant productivity and power, performance, and area (PPA) improvements using this technology, often leveraging cloud-based solutions like Microsoft Azure. This milestone signifies a new era for chip design, accelerating the development of advanced-node chips and optimizing design workflows with AI.

title: Equinix uses two-phase cooling on live Metal cloud servers
summary: Equinix has implemented two-phase liquid cooling on live production servers for its Metal infrastructure-as-a-service offering, using ZutaCore technology. This system, which involves a fluid boiling and condensing to remove heat more efficiently, has been running stably for six months in its NY5 data center. The company is testing this on standard hardware to prepare for higher thermal design power (TDP) servers and address potential future industry needs for more efficient cooling solutions, despite current PUE metrics not fully capturing its benefits.

title: Qualcomm Introduces the World's First 5G NR-Light Modem-RF System to Fuel a New Wave of 5G Devices
summary: Qualcomm Technologies has announced the Snapdragon X35 5G Modem-RF System, the world’s first 5G NR-Light modem-RF system, designed to bridge the gap between high-speed mobile broadband and low-bandwidth IoT devices. This new system aims to expand 5G to new use cases like smartwatches, XR glasses, and industrial IoT by offering smaller, more cost-efficient devices with longer battery life. Customer sampling is expected in the first half of 2023, with commercial devices launching by the first half of 2024.

title: Fortinet Continues to Invest in Custom Chips to Power Security Offerings
summary: Fortinet continues to invest in its custom ASIC chip strategy, which executives claim is a unique differentiator and a key factor in the vendor's firewall market dominance. The company designs its own Arm-based ASICs for its products to achieve better performance, efficiency, and cost savings compared to traditional CPU-based solutions. This long-term investment also provides supply chain advantages and contributes to Fortinet's leading market share in firewall appliance unit shipments.

title: AMD wins nearly a third of processor market, Arm's climb slows: analyst report
summary: AMD has significantly increased its market share in central processor units, capturing nearly a third of the x86 processor market from Intel, according to a recent analyst report from Mercury Research. This growth occurred during the worst PC chip market downturn since the 1980s. Meanwhile, Arm's rise in the PC market, primarily driven by Apple's Mac computers, slowed in the fourth quarter of 2022.

title: Here's A Simple Way To Bet On Semiconductor Plays Like NVDA Stock
summary: The semiconductor sector is experiencing a resurgence fueled by a new Nasdaq "power trend," offering investors opportunities in stocks like Nvidia, Taiwan Semiconductor, Advanced Micro Devices, and Broadcom. The VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH) provides a way to capitalize on these rallies. The article highlights the SMH ETF as a simple investment vehicle for those looking to profit from the recovery and growth in semiconductor stocks.

title: How enterprises can use ChatGPT and GPT-3
summary: This article explores how enterprises can leverage chatbot platforms like ChatGPT and GPT-3 for various functions, from automating tasks and generating creative ideas to aiding in code development and customer support. It highlights the differences between ChatGPT and GPT-3, emphasizing GPT-3's broader capabilities and customizability for specific business needs through prompt engineering. The piece also addresses the critical risks associated with using these technologies, such as data security concerns and the potential for inaccurate or biased outputs, urging caution and the implementation of clear governance guidelines.

title: Wi-SUN Alliance Certifies Silicon Labs’ Wi-SUN SoC for Smart Metering
summary: Silicon Labs' ERF32FG25 Wi-SUN-enabled System-on-Chip (SoC) has received full certification from the Wi-SUN Alliance, making it available for smart city applications. This SoC addresses historical interoperability issues in the IoT and smart city industries by providing a standardized solution based on the IEEE 802.15.4g protocol. The device features a 32-bit Arm Cortex-M33 core, extensive memory, and a high-performance transceiver designed for long-range, high-throughput, and low-latency sub-GHz Wi-SUN applications.

title: Corsair Vengeance RGB DDR5-6000 C30 Review: Optimized For Zen 4
summary: The Corsair Vengeance RGB DDR5-6000 C30 memory kit is an enthusiast-grade option optimized for AMD's Ryzen 7000 processors with EXPO profiles. It offers stable performance out-of-the-box and features a clean design with customizable RGB lighting. While it performs well, especially on Intel systems, its overclocking headroom is limited due to requiring 1.4V for its advertised speeds.

title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. stock outperforms competitors despite losses on the day
summary: Shares of Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) fell by 0.90% to $85.18 on Wednesday, despite a generally positive trading session for the stock market. The decline broke a two-day winning streak for AMD, which closed significantly below its 52-week high. Compared to competitors, AMD's performance was mixed, with NVIDIA Corp. also falling, while Intel Corp. and Applied Materials Inc. saw gains.

title: The 7 Best ChatGPT Stocks to Buy Now
summary: This article identifies seven top stocks poised to benefit from the rise of ChatGPT and AI, focusing on companies with established AI development rather than speculative firms. It highlights Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), Alphabet (GOOG), Amazon (AMZN), Meta Platforms (META), Microsoft (MSFT), NVIDIA (NVDA), and Salesforce (CRM) as strong investment opportunities. The author emphasizes scrutinizing a company's long-term AI commitment and business model integration to avoid hype-driven investments.

title: How to Invest in the Right AI Stocks
summary: The article discusses the complexities of investing in AI stocks, noting that while many major tech firms use AI, it has not yet become a significant standalone revenue driver for most. It suggests that investors should focus on "picks and shovels" companies, particularly semiconductor manufacturers like Nvidia, AMD, Intel, and ASML, which supply the essential hardware for AI development. While many companies integrate AI to enhance existing products, Morningstar analysts see direct revenue opportunities primarily in the semiconductor sector.

title: AMD Puts Hopes on Packaging, Memory on Logic, Optical Comms for Decade Ahead
summary: AMD outlined its decade-long strategy for continued performance and efficiency gains in computing at ISSCC 2023. The company aims to overcome tapering process technology benefits through advanced architecture, packaging technologies like 3D CPU & GPU silicon, and tighter integration of compute and memory, including processing-in-memory solutions. Additionally, AMD plans to leverage optical communications integrated with compute dies for efficiency boosts, continuing its fierce competition in the semiconductor industry with ever faster and more efficient chips.

title: Pushing the System-in-Package Concept Into the Future
summary: Chipletz, a fabless substrate vendor, has selected Siemens semiconductor packaging technologies for their Smart Substrate based advanced packages. This technology aims to revolutionize semiconductor in-package functionality, bridging the gap between Moore's Law slowing and the rising demand for compute performance for critical AI workloads and high-performance computing. Chipletz's unique approach enables the integration of multiple ICs from different manufacturers within a single package, supported by Siemens' EDA tools.

title: Meet the $10,000 Nvidia chip powering the race for A.I.
summary: The Nvidia A100 chip, costing around $10,000, has become a critical tool in the artificial intelligence industry, powering applications like ChatGPT and Stable Diffusion. Nvidia dominates the market for GPUs used in machine learning, with the A100 serving as a "workhorse" for AI professionals and requiring substantial investment for companies to train and deploy AI models. Despite the high costs and emerging competition, Nvidia's CEO Jensen Huang asserts that their GPUs offer a much more affordable solution for developing sophisticated AI models compared to traditional CPU-based data centers.

title: As chatbots boom, Nvidia sales outlook beats Wall Street expectations
summary: Nvidia Corp (NVDA.O) surpassed Wall Street's first-quarter revenue expectations, driven by a surge in demand for its chips used in AI services like chatbots. The company announced a new cloud computing service to help businesses develop generative AI, positioning itself to significantly benefit from the growing AI sector despite a broader downturn in the PC market. Analysts project this boom in generative AI could add an additional $14 billion to Nvidia's revenue by 2027.

title: The Future of AI Stocks: 7 Companies to Keep Your Eye On
summary: This article identifies seven key AI stocks and ETFs that investors should consider due to artificial intelligence's potential to revolutionize various industries. It highlights companies like Nvidia, Microsoft, and Advanced Micro Devices for their direct involvement in AI technology, along with Innodata and C3.ai for their specific AI solutions. Additionally, it suggests AI-focused ETFs like ROBO Global Artificial Intelligence ETF and Global X Robotics & Artificial Intelligence ETF for diversified investment in the burgeoning AI sector.

title: Applied Materials stock rises on new chip-making system that helps lower costs
summary: Applied Materials Inc. shares rose after the company unveiled a new chip-making system designed to lower manufacturing costs for semiconductors. The system, called Centura Sculpta, promises to save fabs like TSMC and Intel $50 per wafer. This innovation led to a notable outperformance of Applied Materials stock compared to its peers and the broader semiconductor market.

title: Sony Becomes AMD's Largest Customer on Booming PS5 Sales
summary: Sony became AMD's largest customer in 2022, contributing 16% of the company's revenue, primarily due to soaring PlayStation 5 sales. AMD's gaming business unit, bolstered by PS5 and Xbox Series X|S console chip sales, was its top revenue generator. However, AMD anticipates a decline in gaming revenue for 2023 as console sales typically peak in their third year and component pricing is renegotiated.

title: AMD Unveils Its First 400G Network Performance IPSec Device at OFC 2023
summary: AMD announced its first 400G Inline Internet Protocol Security (IPSec) processing capabilities, featuring the AMD Versal Premium series, at OFC 2023. This new device addresses the previous limitation of IPSec solutions to 10G-200G speeds, despite modern network capabilities up to 400G and 800G. The 400G security solution will allow businesses to operate high-speed networks without compromising security, throughput, or latency, utilizing integrated crypto engines and hardened network interface IP blocks with AMD EPYC processors.

title: Aehr Comments on Recent Tesla Statements on Silicon Carbide and Reiterates its Annual Guidance
summary: Aehr Test Systems clarified recent statements made by Tesla regarding a 75% reduction in silicon carbide for new drive units, asserting that this reduction applies only to a new, lower-cost platform still under development and will not significantly impact the overall silicon carbide wafer market. Aehr believes the shift to larger, more integrated die in custom modules for new platforms will increase the need for their wafer-level test and burn-in solutions, affirming their fiscal year 2023 revenue guidance of $60 million to $70 million.

title: Microchip Technology, microchip manufacturer with capital of $50 billion, coming to Armenia
summary: American microchip manufacturer Microchip Technology, with a market capital of $50 billion, is expanding its operations into Armenia. This follows a two-year collaborative agreement with Instigate company, which culminated in Microchip Technology acquiring Instigate Semiconductor. The move is part of a larger trend of major tech and energy companies, including NVIDIA, AMD, and Shell, establishing a presence in Armenia, aiming to boost the country's productivity, innovation, and expertise in high-tech fields.

title: RANOVUS demonstrates industry’s lowest power consumption 800Gbps Ethernet interoperable link to scale AMD adaptive SoCs for AI/ML
summary: RANOVUS has demonstrated an 800Gbps Ethernet interoperable link with the industry's lowest power consumption, designed to scale AMD adaptive SoCs for AI/ML applications. This demonstration at OFC 2023 highlights the versatility of RANOVUS's Odin® optical interconnect, which offers 5pJ/bit energy efficiency for co-packaged, near-packaged, and pluggable optical modules. The technology aims to significantly reduce power consumption and optimize density and cost for hyperscale data centers facing growing AI/ML workloads.

title: Argentina’s copper rush lures top power generator Central Puerto
summary: Central Puerto SA, one of Argentina's largest power producers, is looking to diversify into the nation's burgeoning copper sector. The company aims to acquire non-operating stakes in copper projects, offering its expertise to international investors in navigating Argentina's complex regulatory environment. This move is part of a broader strategy to invest in export-oriented sectors like mining and forestry, which provide revenue in US dollars, contrasting with its domestic power sales in the devaluing Argentine peso.

title: Supermicro Expands Storage Solutions Portfolio for Intensive I/O Workloads with All-Flash Servers Utilizing EDSFF E3.S and E1.S Storage Drives
TSMC customer billed NT$529bn
summary: TSMC's largest customer, widely believed to be Apple Inc., generated NT$529.65 billion (US$17.32 billion) in revenue for the company last year, marking the first time a single customer contributed over NT$500 billion. Despite this record revenue, the customer's share of TSMC's total sales decreased from 26% in 2021 to 23% in 2022. This shift is attributed to significant growth in TSMC's automotive chip sales, which outperformed those for high-performance computing and smartphones.

title: ASML, China customers haunted by uncertainty on new Dutch chip export rules
summary: The Dutch government's newly announced restrictions on chip-technology exports to China have left ASML Holding NV and its Chinese customers uncertain about crucial details, including whether ASML can service existing chip-printing machines in China. While potentially aligning with U.S. goals, the lack of clarity on servicing limits and affected machine models poses a significant challenge for ASML's outlook and its substantial sales and service agreements in the Chinese market. The situation could lead Chinese chipmakers to focus on older technology, shifting ASML's demand to other regions.

title: Emerging Leaders 2023: Ankur Prakash
summary: Ankur Prakash, a partner at Liberty Global Ventures, has been recognized as one of the top 50 Emerging Leaders in corporate venturing for 2023. With over a decade at Liberty Global, he has significantly impacted various tech sectors through strategic investments like Arrcus, Aviatrix, and Lacework. Prakash, an entrepreneur-first investor, focuses on helping founders disrupt large markets and build exceptional companies, leveraging Liberty Global Ventures' 15 years of investment history and strong industry network in Europe.

title: AMD Shoots Down EPYC Genoa Memory Bug Claims, Says Update On Track
summary: AMD has refuted recent claims of a memory bug affecting its EPYC Genoa processors that would necessitate a costly redesign to support 2DPC memory configurations. The company stated that all shipped fourth-gen EPYC processors fully support 2DPC and that BIOS updates have already been issued to OEM partners to enable this functionality by the end of Q1 2023. This clarifies previous vague statements and assures that no respin is required for the anticipated memory capacity boost.

title: Raja Koduri, Main Driver Of Intel’s GPU Efforts, To Leave Company
summary: Raja Koduri, Intel's chief architect and the main driver behind its discrete GPU efforts, is leaving the company at the end of March to start a new software company focused on generative AI for gaming, media, and entertainment. Koduri had been with Intel for over five years, leading the buildout of its graphics capabilities and serving as chief architect for most of his tenure. His departure comes as Intel announced cuts to its data center GPU roadmap and delays to Falcon Shores.

title: Supermicro Expands GPU Solutions Portfolio with Deskside Liquid-Cooled AI Development Platform, Powered by NVIDIA
summary: Supermicro has launched a new liquid-cooled AI development platform, the SYS-751GE-TNRT-NV1, designed for deskside use. This platform features four NVIDIA A100 Tensor Core GPUs and two 4th Gen Intel Xeon Scalable CPUs, providing a powerful, quiet, and energy-efficient solution for AI/HPC workloads. It also includes a three-year subscription to NVIDIA AI Enterprise, simplifying software setup for developers.

title: Taiwan PCB makers moving production to Southeast Asia
summary: Taiwanese Printed Circuit Board (PCB) manufacturers are relocating production from China to Southeast Asian countries like Thailand, Malaysia, and Vietnam. This move is driven by factors such as investment incentives and lower manufacturing costs in these regions, despite challenges including wastewater management and anti-Chinese sentiment in some areas. Companies like Unimicron, Kinsus Interconnect Technology, and Tripod Technology are establishing or expanding facilities, capitalizing on the growing semiconductor supply chain in Malaysia and Vietnam.

title: Intel co-founder and philanthropist Gordon Moore has died at 94
summary: Gordon Moore, the visionary co-founder of Intel and profound philanthropist, has passed away at the age of 94. He was widely recognized for his groundbreaking "Moore's Law," which accurately predicted the exponential growth of computing power, significantly influencing the trajectory of the digital age. Beyond his technological contributions, Moore, alongside his wife, established a foundation that has donated over $5.1 billion to environmental conservation, science, and patient care.

title: The Strange Case of an Israeli Computer Lab Flagged by the U.S.
summary: The U.S. Commerce Department added Israeli company CNG Labs to its "unverified list," restricting its ability to do business with American firms, following a prior Israeli confiscation of CNG's processors. The Commerce Department's action marks an unusual move against an Israeli company and is a "red flag" warning, not a sanction or blacklist. CNG Labs, which claims to be a computer store, has disputed the dual-use classification of its older model processors, arguing they are only for laptops.

title: ECE Expeditions rides to the Lone Star State for a tour of engineering companies
summary: ECE Expeditions took fourteen students to Texas for a firsthand look at various engineering companies in Houston and Austin, including Hewlett Packard Enterprise, NASA, Ambiq, Dell Technologies, Torc Robotics, and NI. Students networked with Michigan alums, explored technology demos, and toured labs, gaining valuable insights into careers in electrical and computer engineering. The trip aimed to provide students with motivation and clarity on their future career paths in the engineering industry.

title: Micron Could Report Largest Ever Loss in Q2
summary: Micron Technology Inc. (MU) is projected to report its largest-ever loss in the second fiscal quarter of 2023, with an estimated net loss of nearly $1.2 billion and EPS of -$1.06, a significant drop from the prior year. This financial struggle is attributed to weak consumer demand, high inflation, inventory issues, and industry-wide supply constraints. Analysts predict negative gross margins for the first time in 15 years, with an expected inventory write-down in the range of a billion dollars.

title: Intel’s stock set for best month in 22 years as execs lay out plans to take on AMD’s rise in servers
summary: Intel's stock experienced its best month since 2001, with shares jumping 7.6% on Wednesday after an investor presentation detailed the company's strategy to regain market share in data-center chips. The company aims to reverse its manufacturing decline and compete more effectively against AMD. This surge pushed Intel's March gain to 26.4%.

title: 3 Best Desktop Computers for Business from HP India
summary: This article identifies and reviews the three best HP desktop computers for business use: the HP Workstation Z4 G4 Tower, the HP Z1 G9 Tower Desktop PC, and the HP Z2 Mini G9 Workstation Wolf Pro Security Edition. It outlines key features to consider when purchasing a business desktop, including form factor, processor, graphics, memory, storage, and security. The article emphasizes that the ideal business computer depends on specific needs like raw processing power, upgradability, or a compact design.

title: Beijing’s Micron Probe Spurs $12 Billion Rally in China Chip Stocks
summary: Chinese chip stocks experienced a significant rally, collectively adding over $14 billion in market value, following Beijing's probe into Micron Technology Inc. This surge reflects optimism that domestic firms will benefit from China's increased focus on self-reliance in the semiconductor industry. Companies like Ingenic Semiconductor and Advanced Micro-Fabrication Equipment saw substantial gains.

title: LG is creating new 4K and Ultrawide OLED displays with refresh rates of 240 Hz
summary: LG is developing new 4K and Ultrawide OLED displays with a 240 Hz refresh rate, expected to launch in 2024. These displays will come in various sizes, including 27-inch, 31.5-inch, and 42-inch 4K models, and 34-inch, 39-inch, and 45-inch ultrawide options. Some models will also feature dual refresh-rate modes, offering flexibility between resolution and performance for gamers.

title: Samsung Electronics and AMD Extend Strategic IP Licensing Agreement to Bring AMD Radeon™ Graphics to Future Mobile Platforms
summary: Samsung Electronics and AMD have announced a multi-year extension of their strategic IP licensing agreement. This collaboration will integrate multiple generations of high-performance, ultra-low-power AMD Radeon graphics solutions into an expanded portfolio of Samsung Exynos SoCs, bringing console-level graphics and optimized power consumption to mobile devices. This builds upon their initial 2019 partnership which resulted in the Samsung Xclipse mobile GPU, featuring industry-first mobile ray tracing.

title: AMD Is Space Certified As Xilinx Computers Make It To NASA’s Rocket & Ship
summary: AMD announced that its subsidiary Xilinx's field-programmable gate arrays (FPGAs) are integral to NASA's Space Launch System (SLS) rocket and Orion spacecraft, both central to the Artemis program aimed at returning humans to the Moon. These FPGAs are radiation-hardened, crucial for operating in the harsh space environment, and highlight AMD's diversification into the FPGA sector following its acquisition of Xilinx. The announcement coincided with NASA's crew selection for the Artemis 2 mission, underscoring the critical role of AMD's technology in future lunar explorations.

title: Empowering The Industry with Open System Firmware – AMD openSIL
summary: AMD has introduced openSIL, an open-source silicon initialization library, to address challenges in firmware such as security vulnerabilities and limited transparency. This initiative aims to improve platform security, functionality, and traceability by opening up firmware architecture and development to the open-source community. AMD is collaborating with several organizations to develop and demonstrate openSIL, which reorganizes its existing AGESA framework into a more flexible and scalable solution for various host firmware types.

title: 3 Best HP Pavilion Laptops For Entertainment | HP® Tech Takes
summary: This article outlines the key features to consider when choosing an entertainment laptop, focusing on display size, audio, storage, memory, and graphics. It then presents three top HP Pavilion laptop models—the Pavilion x360, Pavilion 15, and Pavilion Gaming laptop—highlighting their specifications and suitability for various entertainment needs. The guide aims to help users find a budget-friendly and high-performance device for gaming, streaming, or general multimedia consumption.

title: AMD Bolsters Embedded Portfolio with New Ryzen Embedded 5000 Series Processors for Networking Solutions
summary: AMD has announced the availability of its new Ryzen Embedded 5000 Series processors, designed for power-efficient, "always-on" networking firewalls, network-attached storage, and other security applications. These "Zen 3"-based processors offer scalable performance with up to 16 cores, ECC-supported memory, and PCIe Gen4 connectivity, ideal for demanding enterprise reliability requirements in space- and power-constrained environments. The new series expands AMD's embedded portfolio, bridging the gap between its low-power Ryzen Embedded and high-performance EPYC Embedded families.

title: Analog Devices Appoints Alan Lee as Chief Technology Officer
summary: Analog Devices, Inc. (ADI) has appointed Alan Lee as its new Chief Technology Officer (CTO). In this role, Lee will lead the identification and advancement of next-generation technologies for the semiconductor industry, collaborating with customers, universities, and partners. He brings extensive experience from his previous role as Head of Research and Advanced Development at AMD and will report directly to ADI CEO and Chair Vincent Roche.

title: Analog Devices names former AMD exec Alan Lee as CTO
summary: Analog Devices has appointed Alan Lee, formerly head of research and advanced development at AMD, as its new Chief Technology Officer. In this role, Lee will focus on identifying and advancing next-generation technologies to disrupt and shape the semiconductor industry. He will report directly to Analog Devices' CEO Vincent Roche and brings extensive experience in hardware, software, AI, and high-performance computing from his tenure at AMD, Intel, and IBM.

title: Cognitive Computing Brings Human Touch
summary: Cognitive computing, particularly neuromorphic computing, is poised to transform artificial intelligence by enabling systems to "think" like humans, learn from data, and interact naturally. This technology, which uses artificial neurons to mimic the human brain, could power various applications from autonomous vehicles to smart devices with greater efficiency. While companies developing this technology present investment opportunities, the article suggests that adopting companies leveraging it for increased productivity and growth may offer even greater upside, while also highlighting the ethical considerations and risks associated with advanced AI systems.

title: Intel says margins will recover in second half of 2023, shares rise
summary: Intel announced that its gross margins are expected to improve in the second half of 2023, after hitting historic lows in the first half of the year. The chipmaker also reported shipping its delayed Sapphire Rapids data center chip in volume and observed signs of stability in the PC market, leading to a 4% rise in its shares in after-hours trading. Despite forecasting adjusted losses for the second quarter, the company's Q1 revenue slightly exceeded estimates, and CEO Pat Gelsinger discussed the ongoing Tower Semiconductor acquisition with Chinese officials.

title: Wilmington's Analog Devices Names Alan Lee Chief Technology Officer
summary: Analog Devices, Inc. (ADI), a semiconductor leader based in Wilmington, MA, has appointed Alan Lee as its new chief technology officer. Lee, previously head of research and advanced development at AMD, will focus on advancing next-generation analog, mixed-signal, power, software, and AI capabilities for ADI. His role will involve collaborating with customers, universities, and partners to develop novel technologies and support their market entry.

title: Semiconductor supplier secures incentives in Round Rock for $185M, 50-job expansion
summary: Toppan Photomasks, a semiconductor industry supplier, has secured incentives for a $185 million expansion in Round Rock, Texas. This project is expected to create 50 new jobs at its existing site. The company counts major players like Samsung, Texas Instruments, and AMD among its clients.

title: IBM to Replace 7,800 Jobs Using Automation and AI
summary: IBM plans to replace approximately 7,800 jobs over the next five years using automation and AI technology. CEO Arvind Krishna stated that this would primarily impact non-customer-facing roles, with about 30% of back-office positions expected to be replaced. The company will also slow or suspend hiring in departments like human resources, while still looking for engineering and customer-facing talent.

title: Opinion: AMD stock is sliding after earnings, and these numbers show why
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock slid significantly after the company reported a first-quarter net loss of $139 million and a revenue decline to $5.3 billion. This downturn was largely attributed to substantial drops in PC sales and gaming, exacerbated by a big decline in profit for its key data-center group and an unoptimistic forecast. The chip maker's overall margin was severely impacted, leading to a nearly 7% drop in its shares in after-hours trading.

title: AMD to Make 'New' 2019-Era 3000G APUs to Sell Old Motherboards
summary: AMD is reportedly resuming production of its 2019-era 12nm Zen+ Ryzen 3000G APUs, specifically the Ryzen 3 3200G and Ryzen 5 3400G, with plans to produce 30,000 new chips. This move is believed to be an effort to stimulate sales of older AM4 motherboards, particularly in the Chinese market. The article notes that price adjustments will be necessary for these dated APUs to be competitive against newer, similarly priced options like the Ryzen 5 5600G.

title: Intel layoffs 2023: Targeting $10B in annual savings
summary: Intel is reportedly planning significant layoffs, potentially affecting up to 20% of employees in its client computing and data center divisions, as part of a strategy to achieve $10 billion in annual savings by 2026. These workforce reductions come amidst a challenging macroeconomic environment, slowed chip demand, and increasing competition from rivals like AMD. Despite a record first-quarter loss, Intel anticipates chip demand recovery in the second half of 2023 and is focusing on advanced manufacturing processes to regain market share.

title: Notable Wins
summary: The I/O Fund has achieved significant returns since its inception, outperforming major indices with a cumulative return of 326%. In 2025 alone, the fund returned 37%, positioning it as a top-performing hedge fund. The article details specific successful investments in companies like Bloom Energy, Astera Labs, Nvidia, and various cryptocurrencies, highlighting the fund's strategic allocation to AI and early insights into market trends.

title: Vehicle network startup Ethernovia raises $64 million
summary: Ethernovia Inc., a startup focused on vehicle networks, has successfully raised $64 million in Series A funding. The investment round saw participation from notable investors including Porsche Automobile Holding SE, Qualcomm Ventures, and AMD Ventures. The company aims to develop cutting-edge network architecture, high-performance computing, and high-bandwidth communications for the next generation of software-defined vehicles, addressing the increasing data transmission demands in the automotive industry.

title: Implications and Updates From China’s National People’s Conference
summary: China's annual "Two Sessions" parliamentary meeting revealed strategic policies for economic growth, trade, and technology, targeting a 5% GDP growth in 2023 driven by domestic consumption. The conference highlighted key implications for sectors like EVs, clean energy, cloud computing, biotechnology, and semiconductors, emphasizing self-reliance and technological innovation. Government restructuring and increased focus on digital infrastructure and R&D are expected to significantly impact various industries and investment themes in China.

title: Canaan unveils immersion-cooled Avalon Made A1366I Bitcoin miner
summary: Canaan has launched its new immersion-cooled Avalon Made A1366I Bitcoin miner, featuring a hashrate of 165 TH/s and an efficiency of 30 J/TH, which offers benefits like safer overclocking and extended lifespan. This release follows their earlier 13-series models and comes amidst competitive market conditions and fluctuating stock prices for the company, which was the first mining equipment manufacturer to go public.

title: Supermicro Launches Industry's First NVIDIA HGX H100 8 and 4-GPU H100 Servers with Liquid Cooling -- Reduces Data Center Power Costs by Up to 40%
summary: Supermicro has launched the industry's first NVIDIA HGX H100 8 and 4-GPU servers featuring liquid cooling, designed to significantly reduce data center power costs by up to 40%. These new rack-scale solutions accelerate deployment and increase performance for AI and HPC workloads, while also lowering operating expenses and improving Power Usage Effectiveness (PUE) by up to 86% in direct cooling costs. The systems offer up to 30x performance improvement over previous generations for large transformer models and boast advanced features like PCIe 5.0 and NVIDA H100 SXM5 Tensor Core GPUs.

title: Applied Materials plans $4B Silicon Valley R&D facility
summary: Applied Materials announced plans to invest up to $4 billion in a new Silicon Valley research and development facility. This "EPIC Center" will focus on advancing semiconductor manufacturing and is expected to house over $25 billion in research work within its first decade. The investment is contingent on receiving funding from the CHIPS and Science Act, aiming to accelerate the industry's ability to bring new technologies to market.

title: Applied Materials Launches Multibillion-Dollar R&D Platform in Silicon Valley to Accelerate Semiconductor Innovation
summary: Applied Materials, Inc. announced a multibillion-dollar investment to build the Equipment and Process Innovation and Commercialization (EPIC) Center in Silicon Valley. This facility will be the world’s largest and most advanced for collaborative semiconductor process technology and manufacturing equipment R&D, aiming to accelerate innovation and reduce the time to commercialization for new technologies. The center will provide dedicated space for chipmakers and university researchers, fostering collaboration and addressing critical industry challenges like talent shortages and R&D costs.

title: Latest Top500 List Highlights World's Fastest and Most Energy Efficient Supercomputers Powered by AMD
summary: AMD announced its continued leadership in high-performance computing (HPC), with its EPYC processors and Instinct accelerators powering many of the world's fastest and most energy-efficient supercomputers. The Frontier supercomputer, equipped with AMD technology, remains the fastest globally and has entered full user operations for scientific research. AMD's focus extends to supporting AI applications within HPC through its hardware and the open ROCm software platform, highlighting systems like LUMI for large language model training.

title: New AMD Ryzen™ 7020 C-Series Processors Bring Fast Performance, Longer Battery Life, and Advanced RDNA™ 2 Graphics to Chromebook Users
summary: AMD has announced its new Ryzen and Athlon 7020 C-Series processors, designed to enhance Chromebook performance, battery life, and graphics capabilities. These new processors, built on 6nm technology with "Zen 2" architecture and RDNA 2 integrated graphics, promise faster and more efficient operation for productivity, learning, and streaming.
