Chromebooks from ASUS and Dell featuring these processors are expected to be available in Q2 2023.

title: Applied Materials' Big Bet: A ‘Unique’ $4B Chip R&D Center
summary: Applied Materials is investing up to $4 billion to build the "EPIC Center," a 180,000-square-foot semiconductor R&D facility in Silicon Valley. This unique center will bring together chipmakers, academics, and ecosystem partners to accelerate manufacturing innovation, reduce costs, and shorten product development timelines, especially crucial in the complex 'angstrom era' of chipmaking. The investment's final size is contingent on subsidies from the U.S. government’s CHIPS and Science Act.

title: Intel's Arc A750 graphics card is only $200 at Newegg
summary: Intel's Arc A750 graphics card is currently available for $200 on Newegg, a $50 discount, making it an excellent option for affordable 1080p gaming. This deal is significant as GPU prices are decreasing due to increased competition from Intel's Arc series. The card performs best with motherboards supporting Resizeable BAR, especially for DirectX 12 titles.

title: Nvidia stock price is surging thanks to ChatGPT and an expected boom in generative AI
summary: Nvidia's stock price surged over 26% after forecasting Q2 2024 revenue of $11 billion, significantly above Wall Street estimates, due to the increasing demand for its powerful GPU chips needed for generative AI technologies like ChatGPT. Despite a slight decrease in Q1 2024 revenue compared to the previous year, the company's strong future outlook based on the generative AI boom is driving investor confidence, also positively impacting other chipmakers like AMD. Nvidia's CEO stated that the company is greatly increasing its supply to meet the surging demand for its data center products.

title: ETF of the Week: Goldman Sachs Hedge Industry VIP ETF (GVIP)
summary: The Goldman Sachs Hedge Industry VIP ETF (GVIP) is highlighted as the ETF of the Week. GVIP provides exposure to top stock picks from hedge funds, focusing on a consensus of leading managers rather than an overweighting of individual stocks. The ETF diversifies holdings across large-cap, mid-cap, and international companies, offering a less volatile way for investors to participate in innovative companies.

title: Markets Brief: Why Vanguard Sees a Brighter Outlook for Investors’ Portfolios
summary: Vanguard Group forecasts a brighter outlook for investors, primarily due to higher bond yields that now offer meaningful contributions to portfolios. While stock valuations remain stretched in the U.S., increasing interest rates have made fixed-income investments more attractive, particularly government bonds. Vanguard's long-term forecasts suggest improved returns for both bonds and stocks compared to recent years, highlighting a more balanced investment landscape.

title: NVIDIA Announces DGX GH200 AI Supercomputer
summary: NVIDIA has unveiled the DGX GH200 AI supercomputer, designed to handle massive models for generative AI, recommender systems, and data analytics. This new system connects 256 Grace Hopper Superchips to function as a single 1-exaflop, 144TB GPU, offering significantly more memory than its predecessor. Companies like Google Cloud, Meta, and Microsoft are among the first to explore its capabilities, and NVIDIA is also building its own Helios supercomputer based on this design.

title: Microsoft shares fix for cameras not working on Surface laptops
summary: Microsoft has released a temporary fix for an issue preventing built-in cameras from working on some ARM-based Surface devices, including the Surface Pro X. The problem, caused by a buggy driver, affects devices running Windows 10 22H2 or Windows 11 21H2/22H2 with specific Qualcomm processors. While a troubleshooter is being deployed automatically, users can apply a manual registry edit or await a permanent driver update.

title: Student profile: Gaining skills to work in signal integrity | Electrical, Computer & Energy Engineering
summary: Vivek Kamble, a professional master's student in high-speed digital engineering at CU Boulder, shares his journey from teaching in India to pursuing advanced studies in signal integrity. He highlights the practical curriculum and industry expert-taught courses that prepared him for multiple internship offers, including one with Advanced Micro Devices Inc. His advice emphasizes the high demand for signal integrity and high-speed digital engineers globally.

title: Samsung Electronics makes 17-fold gains from investment in ASML
summary: Samsung Electronics has seen its initial investment in ASML Holding N.V. grow 17-fold, reaching $4.56 billion, despite reducing its stake to 1.6%. The article highlights how major chipmakers like Samsung, Intel, and TSMC invested in ASML over a decade ago when the company needed funding for R&D. The gains underscore the critical importance of ASML's EUV scanners in the semiconductor industry, with Samsung actively securing these machines to compete with market leader TSMC.

title: Intel Reveals Fastest Arc Pro Workstation GPUs Yet
summary: Intel has launched its fastest Arc Pro workstation GPUs to date, the A60 for desktops and A60M for laptops, significantly increasing specifications over previous models. These new GPUs are designed for applications like CAD, AI inferencing, and media processing, aiming to challenge the market dominance of Nvidia and AMD. While Intel hopes to attract workstation users with improved performance and quarterly driver updates, some partners note a lack of demand and suggest Intel needs a more comprehensive solution to compete effectively.

title: Applied Materials Announces $4 Billion Investment In EPIC Center For R&D Collaboration
summary: Applied Materials is investing $4 billion into its new Equipment and Process Innovation and Commercialization (EPIC) Center, a collaborative R&D project designed to accelerate semiconductor production techniques. This initiative aims to reduce the time from foundational research to mass commercialization by 30% through overlapping R&D phases and fostering direct collaboration between universities, equipment makers, and chip companies. The EPIC Center will strengthen Applied Materials' position in the industry, benefit chip makers with faster product roadmaps, and provide invaluable learning opportunities for university students.

title: AMD launches new Instinct MI300X AI chip to take on Nvidia’s GPUs
summary: AMD has launched its new Instinct MI300X AI accelerator chip, designed to compete with Nvidia's GPUs in the generative AI market. The company also introduced its Infinity Architecture Platform, powered by eight MI300X chips, and its ROCm software ecosystem. Despite these announcements, AMD's stock fell due to a lack of major customer endorsements for the new chips and details on pricing.

title: AMD's AI chip challenge to Nvidia remains uphill fight with no big customers named
summary: AMD unveiled new details about its MI300X artificial intelligence chip, designed to challenge Nvidia's market dominance, but did not announce any major customers for the product. While the chip offers significant memory capacity aimed at cost reduction for AI services, the lack of customer commitments disappointed Wall Street, causing AMD's stock to fall. Analysts noted that Nvidia's strong market position is reinforced by its software ecosystem, which AMD is attempting to counter with its Rocm platform.

title: AMD is chasing down Nvidia in AI, but one analyst worries the company is ‘somewhat late’
summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) is pursuing the lucrative AI data-center market, estimated to be worth $150 billion, following recent product announcements. While many analysts praised AMD's efforts, Bernstein analyst Stacy Rasgon expressed concerns that AMD is "somewhat late" to the AI race compared to rival Nvidia Corp. Rasgon noted that AMD's AI discussion offered limited "hard numbers" and a timeline that seemed delayed relative to the competition.

title: The A.I. Race Is Now Between Two Chip Giants Led by Taiwanese American CEOs
summary: The generative A.I. boom has intensified competition between semiconductor giants Nvidia and AMD, who are racing to produce advanced A.I. chips. AMD recently announced the MI300X, a direct competitor to Nvidia's H100, aiming to challenge Nvidia's dominant market share in the A.I. chip sector. Both companies are led by Taiwanese American CEOs, Jensen Huang of Nvidia and Lisa Su of AMD, who share similar backgrounds and have played pivotal roles in their companies' success in the semiconductor industry.

title: Fidelity Metaverse ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (FMET)
summary: The Fidelity Metaverse ETF (FMET) has an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy" with an average price target equal to its current share price of $36.66. The rating is based on 613 analyst ratings covering 20 companies (69.5% of the portfolio) over the past year. The ETF's top holdings include major tech companies like AMD, Alphabet, Apple, and NVIDIA.

title: Cisco Silicon One Breaks the 51.2 Tbps Barrier
summary: Cisco has announced its fourth-generation Silicon One devices, the G200 and G202, three and a half years after the initial launch, demonstrating a rapid pace of innovation. The G200 is a 5 nm, 51.2 Tbps device focusing on enhanced Ethernet-based AI/ML and web-scale spine deployments, while the G202 offers similar benefits at 25.6 Tbps. These new devices leverage Cisco's high-speed ADC-based SerDes technology and a converged architecture to reduce costs, power consumption, and latency in web-scale and AI/ML networks.

title: Supermicro Unveils MicroCloud, High-Density 3U 8 Node System Utilizing AMD Ryzen Zen 4 7000 Series Processors
summary: Supermicro has launched a new MicroCloud server system, the AS -3015MR-H8TNR, which features high density with an 8-node, 3U design. This system utilizes AMD Ryzen Zen 4 7000 Series processors, optimized for server usage, offering high performance for applications like e-commerce, cloud gaming, and virtual private servers. It boasts PCIe 5.0 support, DDR5-5200 MHz memory, and up to 16 cores (32 threads) per CPU, providing a compact and efficient solution for data centers.

title: Artificial Intelligence Opens a Generational Opportunity for Chipmakers
summary: The article discusses how the expansion of AI applications, especially large language models, is creating a "generational opportunity" for chipmakers. It highlights the projected growth of AI chip spending to nearly $165 billion by the end of the decade, primarily benefiting GPU-based chip providers like Nvidia, and notes that the demand for specialized AI hardware is leading to significant investments, partnerships, and a potential "long capital investment cycle" for the semiconductor industry. The article also examines how this shift will impact traditional CPU providers and accelerate the replacement of legacy computing infrastructure with AI-first hardware.

title: AMD, Broadcom stocks both drop for seventh straight session
summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) and Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) experienced their seventh consecutive day of stock losses on Monday, marking their worst losing streaks since 2022. AMD shares fell 2.3% to close at $107.51, which is 33.6% below their record high from November 2021.

title: Nvidia and AMD shares fall on report of new U.S. ban on AI chip exports to China
summary: Shares of Nvidia Corp. and Advanced Micro Devices Inc. dropped following a report that the Biden administration is considering a new ban on sales of AI chips to China. This potential ban comes despite Nvidia managing to avoid $400 million in lost sales last year after a previous ban on some chip sales to China. The news impacted both companies with Nvidia shares falling 3% after hours and AMD shares also decreasing by 3%.

title: Oracle is no longer buying Intel processors for its data centers
summary: Oracle, one of the largest data center operators globally, has stopped purchasing Intel processors for its data centers. This decision was confirmed by Oracle Chairman Larry Ellison. Instead of Intel, Oracle is now buying billions of dollars worth of Nvidia and AMD chips.

title: Chipmaker Micron beats on demand from booming AI, easing glut
summary: Micron Technology exceeded third-quarter revenue estimates driven by high demand for its memory chips in the rapidly expanding artificial intelligence sector and an easing supply glut in traditional markets. The company's shares rose over 2% after reporting stronger-than-expected earnings and anticipating improved pricing trends. Despite ongoing U.S.-China tech tensions impacting its presence in the Chinese market, Micron expressed confidence in the memory industry's recovery.

title: Stock of the week - Advanced Micro Devices (29.06.2023)
summary: This article discusses the potential impact of new US chip export restrictions to China on Advanced Micro Devices (AMD). While such restrictions negatively affected semiconductor stocks, AMD's exposure may be limited as China's share of its revenue has significantly decreased. The article also compares AMD's stock performance to Nvidia and Intel, noting Nvidia's stronger recovery from the 2022 sell-off.

title: Lockheed, GlobalFoundries Collaborate to Advance US Semiconductor Manufacturing
summary: Lockheed Martin and GlobalFoundries (GF) are partnering to bolster U.S. semiconductor manufacturing, focusing on increasing the security, reliability, and resilience of domestic supply chains for national security systems. This collaboration, announced at GF's advanced manufacturing facility in New York, will leverage GF's technology to innovate microelectronics, including 3D heterogeneous integration, silicon photonics, and gallium nitride on silicon. The initiative aims to support the CHIPS and Science Act's goals by promoting onshore production and developing a chiplet ecosystem to produce chips more rapidly and affordably.

title: ASE, Amkor, UMC and Samsung Getting a Slice of the CoWoS Market from AI Chips, Challenging TSMC
summary: The surging demand for AI chips is overwhelming TSMC's CoWoS packaging capacity, leading chipmakers like Nvidia to diversify their supply chains. This shift creates opportunities for other foundries and OSATs such as UMC, ASE (including SPIL), and Samsung, which are developing their own advanced packaging solutions to meet the growing market needs. Samsung, with its turnkey service and significant R&D investments in 2.5D packaging, is actively pursuing a larger share of the AI chip market.

title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. – More market share gains expected
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is expected to gain further market share in the data center CPU market and capitalize on opportunities to expand its presence in the data center GPU market due to increased generative AI adoption. The company's margins are projected to grow post-acquisition of Xilinx and Pensando, driven by increasing revenue contributions from its Data Centre and Embedded segments. Phillip Securities Research initiates coverage with an Accumulate recommendation and a target price of US$125.00, anticipating marginal revenue growth in FY23e and re-acceleration in FY24e.

title: Samsung, SK Hynix in next-generation HBM chip supremacy war
summary: South Korean memory chip giants Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix are locked in a fierce battle for supremacy in the next-generation High Bandwidth Memory (HBM) chip market, driven by the booming demand for generative AI technologies like ChatGPT. SK Hynix, a long-time leader in HBM, is showcasing its advanced HBM capabilities, including the world's first 12-layer HBM3 DRAM, while Samsung is aggressively increasing its HBM3 production capacity and aims for over 50% market share. Both companies view increased HBM sales as crucial for turning their semiconductor businesses profitable amidst significant operating losses.

title: AMD EPYC CPUs Now Power SAP Applications Hosted on Google Cloud
summary: AMD announced that SAP's cloud ERP delivery operations for RISE with SAP will now run on Google Cloud's N2D virtual machines powered by AMD EPYC processors. This integration aims to provide impressive performance, energy efficiency, and scalability for critical SAP Business Suite workloads. The collaboration focuses on helping enterprises modernize their data centers and accelerate time-to-value for their applications.

title: Meet Indian-origin women on the 'America's Richest Self-Made
summary: Four Indian-origin women, Jayshree Ullal, Neerja Sethi, Neha Narkhede, and Indra Nooyi, have been named on Forbes' list of America’s 100 richest self-made women, with a combined net worth of $4.06 billion. Each woman has achieved significant success in technology, IT consulting, or consumer goods, showcasing their entrepreneurial prowess and impact in their respective industries. The list calculates net worth using stock prices from May 12, 2023, and evaluations from external experts for private companies.

title: Storage in space? The data's already there
summary: As human activity expands into space, the need for data storage in otherworldly environments is growing, presenting unique challenges for vendors. Companies like HPE and Phison are developing specialized solid-state drives (SSDs) designed to withstand the harsh conditions of space, including radiation and extreme temperatures, while also mitigating issues like high latency for data processing. These space-hardened storage solutions are crucial for research on the ISS and future lunar bases, aiming to enable on-site data processing and reduce the cost and latency associated with transmitting large datasets back to Earth.

title: Aehr Reports Record Revenue and Profit for Fiscal 2023 and Guides for Over 50% Increase in Revenue for Fiscal 2024 on Strength from Semiconductor Wafer Level Test and Burn-in Demand
summary: Aehr Test Systems announced record financial results for fiscal 2023, with net sales reaching $65.0 million and GAAP net income of $14.6 million, driven by demand for semiconductor wafer level test and burn-in products. The company also provided strong guidance for fiscal 2024, projecting revenue of at least $100 million, a 50% increase, and GAAP net income of at least $28 million, reflecting over 90% growth. This growth is attributed to increased adoption of their FOX systems and WaferPak Contactors for silicon carbide in electric vehicles, as well as new opportunities in silicon photonics and gallium nitride markets.

title: Jayshree V Ullal rises on Forbes list of richest self-made women in America
summary: Jayshree V Ullal, CEO and President of Arista Networks, has significantly climbed Forbes' list of richest self-made women in America with a net worth of $1.9 billion in 2022. Her success stems from transforming Arista Networks, taking it to IPO in 2014, and a distinguished career that included generating billions for Cisco, and holding key positions at AMD, IBM, and Hitachi. Ullal's journey highlights her leadership, expertise, and entrepreneurial spirit in the technology industry.

title: Nvidia’s stock could have a pathway to $600, Citi says
summary: Citi Research analyst Atif Malik has raised his price target for Nvidia Corp. stock to $520 from $420, citing the company's dominance in the rapidly growing artificial intelligence accelerator market. Malik also outlined a bull-case scenario where Nvidia's stock could reach $600. He anticipates blistering growth in the AI accelerator market, with Nvidia poised to outperform competitors.

title: Microsoft and Meta expand their AI partnership with Llama 2 on Azure and Windows
summary: Microsoft and Meta are expanding their AI partnership, bringing Meta's Llama 2 large language models to Azure and Windows. This collaboration aims to democratize AI by providing developers with open models, enabling them to build generative AI tools and experiences. Azure customers can now fine-tune and deploy Llama 2 models, while Windows developers can run Llama locally, integrating AI safety features from both companies.

title: Samsung primed for graphics chip leadership with GDDR7
summary: Samsung Electronics has unveiled the industry's first GDDR7 DRAM, which is faster and more energy-efficient than existing models. This new graphics memory product boasts a data transfer speed of 32 Gbps, making it able to process 50 ultra-high definition movies in one second, and consumes 20% less power than its predecessor. Samsung expects this technology to elevate user experiences in areas requiring outstanding graphics performance like AI, high-performance computing, and autonomous vehicles, positioning them to lead the graphics chip market.

title: Zacks Industry Outlook Highlights GlobalFoundries, Lattice Semiconductor and Cirrus Logic
summary: The Zacks Electronics – Semiconductors industry is experiencing growth due to the rising adoption of AI, Machine Learning, and Industry 4.0, along with strong demand for consumer electronics. Despite supply-chain challenges and geopolitical tensions, the industry is poised for continued expansion, driven by 5G deployment, smart device computing needs, and advanced packaging solutions. Zacks identifies GlobalFoundries, Lattice Semiconductor, and Cirrus Logic as promising stocks within this sector.

title: Nvidia is ‘dominating’ and could unlock $300 billion in AI revenue by 2027, analyst says
summary: Mizuho analyst Vijay Rakesh is increasingly optimistic about Nvidia's prospects, projecting the company could tap into $300 billion in AI-related revenue by 2027. Rakesh believes Nvidia will maintain a significant share in the AI server market despite potential future competition. This outlook comes as Nvidia's stock has already seen a 200% increase this year.

title: Micron Delivers Industry's Fastest, Highest-Capacity HBM to Advance Generative AI Innovation
summary: Micron Technology announced it has begun sampling the industry's first 8-high 24 GB HBM3 Gen2 memory, boasting bandwidth greater than 1.2 TB/s and pin speed over 9.2 Gb/s, a significant improvement over existing HBM3 solutions. This new memory offers a 2.5 times performance per watt improvement, setting new benchmarks for AI data center metrics and reducing training times for large language models like GPT-4. Micron's 12-high stack with 36 GB capacity is expected to sample in early 2024, further enhancing capacity and efficiency for generative AI and HPC applications.

title: UMC forecasts utilization rate to fall to 65%
summary: United Microelectronics Corp (UMC) anticipates its factory utilization rate will drop further to about 65% in the current quarter due to prolonged inventory correction and weak end-market demand. This represents the lowest utilization since Q4 2011, and the company finds it difficult to predict a bottom given customer inventory destocking through next quarter. Despite this, UMC plans to expand its 28-nanometer capacity and is doubling its silicon interposer wafer capacity to meet growing demand for AI chips.

title: U.S. chipmaker AMD to invest $400 million in India by 2028
summary: U.S. chipmaker Advanced Micro Devices (AMD.O) announced a significant investment of $400 million in India over the next five years, including the establishment of its largest design center in Bengaluru, creating 3,000 new engineering jobs. This move aligns with India's efforts to become a chipmaking hub, attracting other major players like Applied Materials and Micron with its incentive programs. AMD's expansion underscores the country's growing importance in the global semiconductor industry.

title: AMD to establish $400M chip design center in India
summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) announced plans to invest $400 million in a new chip research and engineering center in Bangalore, India, which will be its largest design center globally with 3,000 engineers. This expansion will bring AMD's total headcount in India to over 9,000 employees. The move follows similar investments by other major chipmakers like Micron Inc. and Applied Materials Inc. in India.

title: AMD earnings preview: Is the company ceding ground to Intel?
summary: This article previews AMD's upcoming earnings report, questioning whether the company is losing market share to Intel. Historically, Intel's struggles have benefited AMD, but Intel's recent performance suggests a potential shift. Wall Street will be looking for confirmation of this trend in AMD's latest quarterly results.

title: AMD stock rises 3% after earnings beat, CEO predicts ‘multiple winners’ in AI race
summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) shares rose 2.7% after hours following an earnings beat, with its CEO predicting "multiple winners" in the AI race. The company's PC sales slightly exceeded estimates, while data-center sales were just under projections. This performance follows Intel Corp.'s recent report of a data-center beat and strong PC sales.

title: Opinion: AMD’s AI era to begin near year’s end, but it’s looking more like a 2024 story
summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. needs flawless execution for the launch of its first AI-targeted graphics chips. While AMD projected better-than-expected Q3 revenue, Wall Street is more focused on Q4, when the company plans to launch its first GPU chips for AI acceleration in data centers, suggesting its AI era will truly take off in 2024.

title: JP Morgan Maintains Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Neutral Recommendation
summary: JP Morgan has maintained a Neutral recommendation for Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), with an average one-year price target suggesting a 19.45% upside. The article highlights strong institutional ownership, projected revenue growth, and key financial holdings in the company. It also includes background information on AMD and its recent financial results for Q2 2023.

title: phoenixNAP Powers Expanded AI and Cloud Services with Energy-saving, Ampere-Based Servers from Hewlett Packard Enterprise
summary: phoenixNAP, a global IT services provider, is expanding its Bare Metal Cloud platform by deploying cloud-native HPE ProLiant RL300 Gen11 servers across its 18 data centers. These servers utilize energy-efficient Ampere® Computing processors, enhancing performance and energy efficiency for AI inferencing, cloud gaming, and cloud-native workloads. This expansion addresses the growing demand for data-intensive workloads while mitigating the environmental impact of increasing energy consumption in data centers.

title: Mobile chip giant Qualcomm shares slide amid worst smartphone slump in years
summary: Qualcomm shares fell after the company gave a tepid sales forecast for the current quarter, indicating continued weak demand for mobile devices, especially in China which accounts for over 60% of its sales. The company plans further job cuts, having already recorded US$285 million in restructuring charges, as it takes a conservative view of the market amidst an industry downturn. This performance underperformed a broader chip industry rally, raising concerns about the smartphone market's recovery.

title: AMD's stock breaks with chip selloff to finish week higher as analyst cites 'important buying opportunity'
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) shares unexpectedly rose last week, up 2.6%, despite a broader chip sector selloff that saw Nvidia and the PHLX Semiconductor Index decline significantly. An analyst pointed to this performance as an "important buying opportunity" for AMD. Nvidia's earnings report is anticipated on August 23rd.

title: Micron Launches Memory Expansion Module Portfolio to Accelerate CXL 2.0 Adoption
summary: Micron Technology announced the sample availability of its CZ120 memory expansion modules, designed to accelerate CXL 2.0 adoption. These modules, available in 128 GB and 256 GB capacities, enhance server systems by providing incremental memory capacity and bandwidth, particularly beneficial for AI, SaaS, and HPC workloads. Micron is collaborating with Intel and AMD to validate the CZ120 modules on their CXL-compatible platforms.

title: BlackRock Inc. Acquires Additional Shares in Mercury Systems Inc.
summary: BlackRock Inc. has increased its stake in Mercury Systems Inc. by acquiring an additional 100,009 shares, bringing its total holdings to 5,878,104 shares. This transaction highlights BlackRock's confidence in the aerospace and defense company, which is considered undervalued by GuruFocus's GF Valuation despite recent stock declines. Mercury Systems Inc. demonstrates good outperformance potential with a strong GF Score, profitability, and growth rank, but also presents some financial risks.

title: The Case For Intel: An Illustration Of The ‘Value’ Investing Mindset
summary: This article argues for Intel as a "value investment" due to its current undervaluation despite strong long-term prospects. It delves into strategic market trends, Intel's strong industry position, favorable political and geopolitical factors, and a revived company culture under CEO Pat Gelsinger. The author emphasizes that true "value investing" focuses on a company's ability to correct its problems and adapt to strategic environmental shifts over a multi-year horizon, rather than short-term financial details.

title: Supermicro Announces High Volume Production of E3.S All-Flash Storage Portfolio with New CXL Memory Expansion Offerings
summary: Supermicro is commencing high-volume production of its E3.S all-flash storage portfolio, which includes new CXL memory expansion offerings. These solutions support PCIe Gen5 drives and CXL memory modules to meet the demands of large AI Training and HPC clusters. The company's Petascale systems are a new class of storage servers designed for high-throughput, low-latency storage, available with Intel Xeon Scalable or AMD EPYC processors.

title: Stan Druckenmiller's Duquesne adds GE, Builders FirstSource, exits AMD (NASDAQ:NWS)
summary: Stan Druckenmiller's Duquesne Family Office significantly adjusted its portfolio, adding new stakes in General Electric, Builders FirstSource, PTC, Canadian Pacific Kansas City, and DoorDash. Concurrently, the firm exited its positions in Advanced Micro Devices, Bilibili, and Workday, and reduced holdings in several major tech companies. These moves indicate a shift in investment strategy, focusing on new sectors while divesting from previous large holdings.

title: Insider Sell: AMD Chair, President & CEO Lisa Su Sells 75,000 Shares
summary: Lisa Su, Chair, President & CEO of Advanced Micro Devices Inc (NASDAQ:AMD), sold 75,000 shares on August 15, 2023, as part of a broader trend of insider selling at AMD. Over the past year, Su has sold 500,000 shares with no purchases, and AMD's insider transaction history shows 25 insider sells and no buys. Despite this, AMD's stock is considered modestly undervalued with a price-to-GF-Value ratio of 0.94.

title: LX International liquidates Yantai LCD component warehouse in China
summary: LX International has liquidated its Yantai LCD component warehouse in China due to decreased demand for LCD panels, particularly from key customer LG Display. This move follows LG Display's reduction in LCD module production at its Yantai plant as the market shifts from LCD to OLED technology for small panels. The liquidation reflects the evolving display market where OLED is expected to significantly increase its share in IT devices.

title: TSMC’s CoWoS Dominance: Amkor, ASE, JCET’s Response
summary: The article discusses how the increasing demand for high-performance computing, AI, and 5G has led to a shift towards advanced packaging techniques like 2.5D and 3D, exemplified by TSMC's CoWoS technology. Despite TSMC's significant role, traditional assembly and test firms such as ASE, Amkor, and JCET are actively developing their own advanced packaging solutions to remain competitive. These firms are securing partial orders and maintaining market share by catering to products not in the highest-end category, showcasing their continued relevance alongside wafer foundries.

title: Samsung weighs retooling Galaxy S series with Exynos chipsets
summary: Samsung Electronics is considering reintroducing its Exynos chipsets into some Galaxy S models next year to reduce its reliance on Qualcomm's Snapdragon chips, which have led to rising application processor (AP) purchasing costs. The company spent \$4.3 billion on APs in the first half of this year, and aims to regain bargaining power lost by exclusively using Qualcomm chips in its latest premium phones. Samsung is reportedly looking at a dual-chip strategy for the Galaxy S24 series, with Exynos chips potentially used in some variants.

title: Why Ireland is well positioned to capitalise on chip ‘gold rush’
summary: Ireland is well-positioned to capitalize on the booming semiconductor industry, which is projected to double by 2030, thanks to its long-standing presence of major players like Analog Devices and Intel. However, the country faces challenges including intense global competition for investment, capacity constraints for manufacturing, and a scarcity of electronic engineering talent. To maintain its competitive edge, Ireland must continue to invest in R&D, upgrade its infrastructure, and foster international collaboration within the semiconductor ecosystem.

title: GraniteShares' Single Stock ETFs Will Follow Nvidia, Tesla, AMD
summary: GraniteShares has launched four new single-stock ETFs to capitalize on the trading momentum of Nvidia, Tesla, and Advanced Micro Devices. These new ETFs offer unique leveraged and short exposures, with the GraniteShares 1.5x Short NVDA Daily ETF (NVD) launching just before Nvidia's earnings report. The asset manager plans to roll out almost three dozen more such filings, emphasizing being first to market with these trading-focused products.

title: AMD debuts two new graphics cards for the consumer market
summary: AMD has introduced two new graphics cards, the Radeon RX 7800 XT and RX 7700 XT, to compete with Nvidia in the consumer chip market. These cards, priced at $499 and $449 respectively, are positioned as more affordable alternatives to high-end GPUs while offering significant performance per dollar. Based on AMD's latest RDNA 3 architecture, they include features like upgraded machine learning accelerators, ray tracing optimization, and FSR 3 technology for improved gaming visuals.

title: New AMD Radeon RX 7800 XT and Radeon RX 7700 XT Graphics Cards Deliver High-Performance, Visually Stunning 1440p Gaming Experiences and Superior Performance-Per-Dollar
summary: AMD has introduced new Radeon RX 7800 XT and RX 7700 XT graphics cards, designed to provide high-performance 1440p gaming experiences with superior performance-per-dollar. These cards, built on RDNA 3 architecture, offer 16GB and 12GB of GDDR6 VRAM respectively, and support features like AMD HYPR-RX and the new FidelityFX Super Resolution 3 for enhanced framerates and visual fidelity. The products are expected to be available from September 6, 2023.

title: Unusual Call Option Trade in Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Worth $1,281.25K
summary: An unusually large call option trade worth $1,281.25K was made for Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) on August 28, 2023, with a strike price of $115.00 and expiring on October 20, 2023. This trade is significantly above the mean for AMD options. The article also provides details on fund sentiment, analyst price forecasts, and other significant shareholders.

title: Coherent to set up centre of excellence at IIT-Madras Research Park
summary: American firm Coherent has signed a tripartite memorandum of understanding with Guidance Tamil Nadu and IIT-Madras Research Park (IITMRP) to establish its first global Centre of Excellence (CoE) in India on the IITMRP campus. This CoE will focus on R&D for lasers, optical networking components, EV batteries, and compound semiconductor devices. The company plans to hire technical talent and start with an applications engineering lab for laser materials processing, leveraging Tamil Nadu's innovation ecosystem and engineering talent.

title: Nvidia Says It Will Invest In Arm’s IPO After Failed Acquisition
summary: Nvidia has confirmed its intention to invest in Arm's initial public offering, despite its failed acquisition attempt of the company. The GPU giant was named as a potential "cornerstone investor" in Arm's updated IPO filing, alongside other tech companies like Intel, AMD, Apple, and Google. This investment highlights the continued importance of Arm's architecture to Nvidia's products and the broader tech ecosystem.

title: Hyperscalers have been choosing Arista over Cisco for data center switches
summary: For the first time in years, enterprise spending, not cloud service provider spending, drove the growth of the data center switch market in Q2 2023, according to Dell'Oro Group. Cisco and Arista Networks remain the leading vendors, with Arista excelling in cloud service providers and Cisco in enterprise. The report also highlights that while AI investments are shifting budgets from traditional front-end networks, the Ethernet switch market continues to grow, driven by easing supply chain constraints and increased demand for higher-speed switches from hyperscalers.

title: POET Technologies’ New Module Designs Attract Major Companies at CIOE
summary: POET Technologies' joint exhibition with Super Photonics Xiamen (SPX) at CIOE in Shenzhen was a significant success, leading to numerous customer engagements and requests for samples of their 800G and 400G optical engines. The company's new module designs, based on the POET Optical Interposer, simplify assembly by eliminating the need for active alignments and attracted major end-users like Alibaba, FiberHome, and ByteDance. The positive reception at the event signals strong near-term commercial activity for POET.

title: Arm climbs 25% in Nasdaq debut after pricing IPO at $51 a share
summary: Arm Holdings saw its stock jump nearly 25% on its first day of trading on the Nasdaq after pricing its IPO at $51 per share, valuing the company at almost $60 billion. SoftBank, which took Arm private in 2016, retains about 90% of the company's outstanding shares. Despite its high valuation relative to its current growth, Arm's unique royalty-based business model, including revenue from decades-old designs, and its strategic importance in AI and chip design, are key factors in investor interest.

title: Chip Stocks Drop After Report Says TSMC Is Worried About Demand
summary: Chip stocks dropped following a report that Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC), the world's largest third-party semiconductor manufacturer, has expressed concerns about slowing chip demand. TSMC reportedly told its suppliers to delay some deliveries, indicating potential weakness in the broader chip market. This news directly impacted investor sentiment towards the technology sector.

title: Supermicro Introduces a Number of Density and Power Optimized Edge Platforms for Telco Providers, Based on the New AMD EPYC™ 8004 Series Processor
summary: Supermicro introduces new H13 WIO family of edge platforms and WIO servers, powered by AMD EPYC™ 8004 Series processors, targeting telco providers. These platforms deliver enhanced performance, energy efficiency, and density, featuring NEBS compliance and AC/DC power options, with TDPs as low as 80 watts for intelligent edge and telco applications. The systems are designed for space-constrained environments, offering PCIe 5.0 and DDR5-4800MHz memory support, making them ideal for edge networks and lowering operational costs.

title: PLDT modernises IT infrastructure to enhance business agility
summary: Philippines telecommunications company PLDT is modernizing its IT infrastructure through a multi-year collaboration with Amdocs, migrating business-critical systems to Amazon Web Services (AWS) to improve business agility and customer experience. Amdocs will provide cloud strategy, security, and operations services using an AI-driven platform, while PLDT will leverage AWS services for application building and infrastructure management. This initiative aims to foster innovation and enhance operational resilience, complementing PLDT Enterprise's existing partnership with Ericsson for AI-enabled solutions.

title: Intel sees massive demand for AI chips designed for China: report
summary: Intel is experiencing high demand for its Gaudi2 AI chips in China, which were designed to comply with US export restrictions. This surge is attributed to Chinese companies developing large language models, leading Intel to increase orders with TSMC. The company's CFO has noted a general trend of customers seeking Gaudi chips as an alternative due to supply shortages.

title: Intel hit with $400 million EU antitrust fine in decades-old case
summary: Intel has been fined 376 million euros ($400 million) by the EU for anti-competitive practices nearly two decades ago. This penalty stems from an original 2009 fine for blocking rivals like AMD, which was largely overturned by Europe's second-highest court last year. The new fine specifically addresses Intel's actions between 2002 and 2006, where it paid companies like HP, Acer, and Lenovo to delay or halt competitor products.

title: Oracle spends more than $100 million on Ampere chips
summary: Oracle Corp has agreed to prepay $104.1 million for processor chips from startup Ampere Computing and also invested $400 million in Ampere in its fiscal 2023. This investment and chip purchases are intended to give Oracle's cloud services a competitive edge against rivals like Amazon.com and Google, which develop their own server chips. Ampere designs custom server chips based on Arm technology, competing with Intel and Advanced Micro Devices.

title: AMD Unveils Purpose-Built, FPGA-Based Accelerator for Ultra-Low Latency Electronic Trading
summary: AMD announced the Alveo UL3524 accelerator card, an FPGA-based solution designed for ultra-low latency electronic trading, offering significant speed improvements for financial firms. This new accelerator delivers less than 3ns FPGA transceiver latency and supports AI-enabled trading strategies through its hardware flexibility and the open-sourced FINN development framework. Strategic partners like Alpha Data, Exegy, and Hypertec are already integrating this technology into custom solutions, expanding its ecosystem.

title: Synopsys Appoints Ann Minooka as Chief Marketing Officer
summary: Synopsys, Inc. has announced the appointment of Ann Minooka as its new Chief Marketing Officer, reporting to President and COO Sassine Ghazi. Minooka brings over two decades of marketing leadership experience in the semiconductor industry and will be responsible for Synopsys' global brand strategy, communications, and marketing initiatives. Her previous roles include CMO at Ampere Computing and Xilinx, where she demonstrated a strong track record of strengthening technology brands and driving impactful marketing campaigns.

title: Sony Interactive Entertainment CEO Jim Ryan to Retire in 2024
summary: Jim Ryan, President and CEO of Sony Interactive Entertainment, announced his retirement from the company in March 2024 after almost 30 years. Hiroki Totoki, President and COO of Sony Group Corporation, will serve as interim CEO of SIE starting April 1, 2024. Ryan cited the difficulty of reconciling living in Europe and working in North America as the primary reason for his decision.

title: AMDY Stock Fund Price and Chart — AMEX:AMDY
summary: This article provides an overview of the AMDY YieldMax AMD Option Income Strategy ETF, including its price performance, key statistics like Assets Under Management and dividend yield, and its synthetic covered call strategy. It explains how the fund aims to generate monthly income and provides exposure to AMD stock's price returns, subject to a cap on potential gains, without directly investing in AMD shares. The article also addresses frequently asked questions about ETFs and AMDY's specific characteristics.

title: Top Performing ETFs: Income and Asia ETFs Lead
summary: This article highlights the top-performing ETFs of the past week, focusing on income-generating and Asia-focused ETFs. The Simplify Interest Rate Hedge ETF (PFIX) led, demonstrating investor interest in strategies to adapt to potential rate hikes. Additionally, several YieldMax income strategies and the KraneShares SSE Star Market 50 Index ETF (KSTR), which focuses on Chinese science and technology firms, showed strong performance.

title: Top Performing ETFs: Income and Asia ETFs on Top
summary: The article highlights top-performing ETFs, with a focus on income and Asia-centric funds. The Simplify Interest Rate Hedge ETF (PFIX B) led the gains, while several YieldMax strategies focused on income also performed well. Additionally, the KraneShares SSE Star Market 50 Index ETF (KSTR B-), which targets Chinese science and technology firms, showed strong performance.

title: UT’s Texas Institute for Electronics and Infleqtion Launch Quantum Manufacturing Center of Excellence
summary: The University of Texas at Austin and Infleqtion have partnered to establish a new center of excellence for quantum manufacturing, named qNexus. This collaboration aims to scale the domestic production of quantum-enabled products for various sectors, including energy, defense, and healthcare, while also developing the future workforce for quantum and photonic manufacturing. The initiative is supported by significant funding from the Texas Legislature and builds on UT's expertise in quantum materials and algorithms.

title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc: Will AMD Stock Price Reach $120 Mark?
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock has been trading in a declining parallel channel since June 2023, despite a 56.47% year-to-date gain. The stock is attempting to recover above its 20-EMA and is targeting the $120 mark if it can break out of its current falling pattern. Technical indicators suggest a struggle, with investors needing to see increased buyer accumulation for a sustained recovery.

title: Elevate efficiency in converged infrastructure | NetApp Blog
summary: The article discusses how the FlexPod converged infrastructure, integrating NetApp, Cisco, and AMD technologies, can revolutionize IT efficiency. It highlights the benefits of this trio, combining all-flash storage, fortified Cisco compute, and powerful AMD EPYC processors, to deliver enhanced performance, cost savings, and simplified management for enterprise applications and VDI solutions. The integration aims to streamline data management, provide powerful computing, and enable rapid deployment and scaling through automation.

title: NVIDIA Omniverse is Changing Filmmaking and Exxact is Helping It Happen
summary: The article highlights how NVIDIA Omniverse and Exxact Corporation are revolutionizing filmmaking, especially in animation and VFX, by enabling real-time virtual production. It showcases how companies like Sony Pictures Animation, disguise, and Versatile Media are using Omniverse to improve workflows, accelerate production, and facilitate remote collaboration. Exxact Corporation provides integrated, high-performance computing solutions to help these companies adopt Omniverse Enterprise.

title: AI Accelerated Video Editing with DaVinci Resolve 18.6 & AMD Radeon Graphics
summary: AMD and Blackmagic Design collaborated to optimize DaVinci Resolve 18.6 for AMD hardware, significantly boosting AI feature performance. This optimization, specifically for AMD Radeon™ RX 7900 XTX graphics cards, leads to up to 4x faster neural engine performance in video editing tasks like Magic Mask, Super Scale, and Speed Warp, enhancing the content creation experience. The improvements leverage the Microsoft DirectML platform API to accelerate AI workloads.

title: Supermicro Accelerates Everything at ISC23
summary: Supermicro showcased its latest High-Performance Computing (HPC) and Artificial Intelligence (AI) solutions at ISC23 in Hamburg, Germany, emphasizing its leadership in AI infrastructure. Key highlights included liquid-cooled NVIDIA HGX H100 GPU servers, an AI development platform with NVIDIA, and an 8U PVC system featuring Intel Data Series GPUs. The company also presented high-performance storage solutions optimized for AI workloads, demonstrating its commitment to accelerating AI adoption.

title: HPE delivers Austral supercomputer to CRIANN in France
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) has delivered a new AI supercomputer named Austral to CRIANN in France. This 2 petaflops system, co-funded by the Normandy Region, French State, and EU, will be used for AI model training and simulations in various fields such as climate modeling, biotechnology, and healthcare. Austral features AMD Genoa, Intel Cooper Lake cores, and Nvidia A100 GPUs, and is hosted in a customized HPE HPC pod with direct liquid cooling.

title: Nvidia and AMD reportedly developing Arm-based PC processors
summary: Nvidia and AMD are reportedly developing new Arm-based PC processors, aiming to enter the Windows computer market as early as 2025. This move aligns with Microsoft's efforts to foster competition and introduce more energy-efficient CPUs with advanced AI features, similar to Apple's Arm-based Macs. Qualcomm currently holds an exclusivity agreement with Microsoft for Arm-based Windows chips, which is set to expire by the end of the year, potentially opening the door for Nvidia and AMD.

title: Nvidia to take on Intel, Qualcomm with Arm-based processors for PC; availability pegged for 2025
summary: Nvidia is developing Arm-based processors for personal computers, aiming to compete with Intel and Qualcomm, with availability expected as soon as 2025. This move intensifies competition in the PC chip market, especially as AMD is also reportedly working on Arm-based processors for PCs. The initiative follows Apple's successful transition to in-house Arm-based chips, boosting Mac sales, and poses a significant challenge to Intel's dominant position.

title: Micron Announces Sampling of 9.6 Gbps LPDDR5X Memory
summary: Micron Technology announced it is shipping production samples of its 1β (1-beta) mobile-optimized LPDDR5X memory, operating at a record 9.6 Gbps. This memory is designed for use with Qualcomm's Snapdragon 8 Gen 3 mobile platform to enable generative AI at the edge. It offers enhanced performance and power efficiency crucial for on-device AI applications, reducing reliance on cloud-based solutions.

title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. stock underperforms Wednesday when compared to competitors
summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) shares fell by 5.52% on Wednesday to $96.06, underperforming the broader stock market, which also experienced a rough trading session. The S&P 500 Index dropped 1.43%, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average declined 0.32%. AMD's closing price was $36.78 below its 52-week high of $132.83, achieved in June.

title: Microsoft will hold a 27% stake in OpenAI and gain access to its AI models.
summary: Microsoft and OpenAI have finalized a new agreement where Microsoft will acquire a 27% stake in OpenAI, valued at $135 billion. This agreement grants Microsoft access to OpenAI's AI technology, including AGI models, until 2032. OpenAI has also committed to investing an additional $250 billion in purchasing Azure services.

title: AMD soars as AI chip sales prediction bodes well for rivalry with Nvidia
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) shares jumped 8% after CEO Lisa Su provided an upbeat 2024 sales forecast of over $2 billion for its MI300 AI chips, positioning AMD as a clear second source to Nvidia in the AI accelerator market. This prediction helped alleviate concerns about a disappointing fourth-quarter forecast despite at least 18 analysts cutting price targets due to conservative forecasts and weakness in other AMD markets. The MI300 is set to become AMD's fastest product to reach $1 billion in sales, having secured commitments from multiple large hyperscale customers.

title: Automotive Digitalization Sparks Big Growth For Chips And Design Tools
summary: The automotive industry is experiencing significant growth in semiconductor dependency due to innovations like autonomous driving, advanced driver-assist features, and infotainment systems. This surge is driven by the adoption of a "software-defined vehicle" concept and a scalable, zonal digital chassis architecture. Companies like Cadence Design Systems are playing a crucial role by providing tools for chip design, system modeling, simulation, and multiphysics analysis to support this technological transformation.

title: Streamlining SoC Integration With the Power of Automation
summary: This article discusses the increasing complexity of System-on-Chip (SoC) integration and the critical need for automation to manage this complexity. It highlights how the IP-XACT standard and tools like Magillem from Arteris provide a standardized, automated approach to streamline SoC integration, reduce errors, and accelerate time-to-market. The piece emphasizes that automation is essential for successful SoC design, improving productivity and overall effectiveness.

title: Raspberry Pi Receives Strategic Investment from Arm, Further Extending Long-Term Partnership
summary: Arm Holdings plc has made a strategic minority investment in Raspberry Pi Ltd, deepening their partnership that began in 2008. This investment aims to further enable the Internet of Things (IoT) developer community with low-cost, high-performance computing solutions built on Arm technology, particularly as edge compute and AI applications grow. Both companies share a vision to democratize technology and remove barriers to entry for innovation across various user groups.

title: Intel CEO Admits to and Details The Company's Three Biggest Mistakes
summary: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger openly discussed what he considers Intel's three biggest missteps: the failed smartphone business, the cancellation of the early AI-oriented GPU Larrabee, and the historical lack of focus on building a robust foundry. He believes these errors led to a disadvantage in the current AI market and impacted the company's overall direction. Gelsinger is now driving Intel's IDM 2.0 initiative, opening its fabs to external customers to generate revenue and invest in manufacturing and R&D.

title: On-device AI chips: New battleground for chipmakers
summary: Global chipmakers like Samsung Electronics, Qualcomm, Intel, and AMD are intensifying their focus on on-device AI chips for smartphones, laptops, and PCs, recognizing its potential for customized services, lower costs, and enhanced privacy compared to generative AI. This shift is expected to drive significant growth in AI-enabled consumer electronics and contribute to rising memory chip prices. Both on-device AI and generative AI are seen as complementary, with companies like Samsung planning to integrate both into future smart devices.

title: Penguin Solutions to Showcase Expertise in AI and HPC at SC23
summary: Penguin Solutions will exhibit its AI and High-Performance Computing (HPC) solutions at Supercomputing 2023 (SC23) in Denver from November 12-17. The company's booth will feature technology demonstrations on designing, building, deploying, and managing AI/HPC infrastructures, including insights into immersion cooling and hybrid cloud solutions. Penguin experts will also participate in speaking engagements discussing AI factories and sustainable deployment environments like immersion cooling.

title: Cadence open-source PDK and reference design now available for SkyWater's 130nm process
summary: SkyWater Technology has announced that Cadence Design Systems has created an open-source 130 nm Process Design Kit (PDK) for their SKY130 process, which will be included in the Cadence VLSI Fundamentals Education Kit. This initiative aims to remove barriers to semiconductor design for academia and researchers by providing accessible tools without NDAs. The collaboration between SkyWater and Cadence is expected to accelerate collaboration in chip design, support semiconductor education, and strengthen the domestic semiconductor workforce.

title: Micron Announces 128GB DRAM Low-Latency, High-Capacity RDIMMs
summary: Micron Technology has launched new 128 GB DDR5 RDIMM memory modules featuring 32Gb monolithic die technology, offering best-in-class performance up to 8000 MT/s. These high-capacity, low-latency modules are designed for data center and cloud environments, particularly for AI, in-memory databases, and high-performance computing, providing significant improvements in bit density, energy efficiency, latency, and AI training performance over competitive products. AMD and Intel have expressed interest in these new RDIMMs for their latest server platforms due to the enhanced capacity and bandwidth benefits.

title: After Earnings, Is AMD Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Morningstar assesses AMD's stock post-Q3 earnings, evaluating its fair value, economic moat, and future prospects, particularly in the AI accelerator market. The company's target of over $2 billion in AI accelerator revenue for 2024 positions it as a significant player. While fair value is estimated at $125 per share, high uncertainty exists due to competition from Nvidia and potential shifts in the x86 architecture dominance.

title: New AMD Radeon PRO Workstation Graphics Card to Power Next-Generation Professional Content Creation, CAD and AI Applications
summary: AMD has launched the Radeon PRO W7700, a new workstation graphics card designed for professional content creation, CAD, and AI applications. Priced under $1,000, it offers significant performance increases in key professional software compared to competitors and features 16GB of VRAM and DisplayPort 2.1. The card also includes hardware-based AI accelerators to speed up AI workloads in various applications.

title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. stock outperforms market on strong trading day
summary: Shares of Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) rose by 2.65% to $119.88 on Tuesday, outperforming the broader market. This positive performance occurred on a strong trading day where the S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average also saw significant gains. The stock closed below its 52-week high of $132.83, which was achieved on June 13th.

title: AMD-powered Frontier system wins first place in Top500 supercomputer ranking
summary: The Top500 computing organization has again named Frontier, an AMD-powered system at Oak Ridge National Laboratory, as the world's fastest supercomputer, achieving 1.194 exaflops. This marks the system's continued dominance since June, showcasing its high performance and impressive power efficiency. The ranking also highlights the rise of other powerful systems like Aurora and Eagle, while demonstrating AMD's growing presence in the supercomputing sector.

title: AMD Brings New AI and Compute Capabilities to Microsoft Customers
summary: AMD and Microsoft are collaborating to integrate AMD's AI and compute technologies into Microsoft's cloud and PC ecosystems. This includes the AMD Instinct MI300X accelerator powering new Azure AI-optimized virtual machines and 4th Gen AMD EPYC processors enhancing general-purpose, memory-intensive, and compute-optimized VMs. Additionally, AMD is expanding its Ryzen AI presence in PCs for generative AI experiences.

title: SK's Sapeon launches new AI chip for data centers
summary: SK's AI chip designer Sapeon has unveiled its new X330 neural processing unit (NPU) chip, targeting data centers and challenging Nvidia. The X330, produced by TSMC and set for mass production in early 2024, offers significant improvements in arithmetic capability and energy efficiency over its predecessor. The alliance aims to foster open innovation and science in AI by bringing together diverse organizations to accelerate responsible AI development, establish benchmarks, and build a vibrant AI ecosystem. Its main goal is to ensure that AI advancements are harnessed in ways that prioritize safety, diversity, economic opportunity, and benefits for all, addressing societal challenges and promoting ethical AI practices.

summary: Meta Platforms and IBM are leading an industry-wide AI Alliance of over 40 companies and organizations to promote open-source artificial intelligence development. This funding will enhance ASU's MacroTechnology Works in Tempe, supporting research in areas like 6G, IoT, and machine learning, alongside workforce development. The initiative aims to strengthen Arizona's semiconductor ecosystem and build on existing efforts to establish advanced packaging in the state.

summary: Jason Banta, AMD's Vice President and General Manager, discusses the future of AI in computing, emphasizing its transformative potential to make computers more personal and anticipatory of user needs. AMD recently launched its MI300 accelerator, aiming to popularize smart laptops from 2024, with a significant inflection point expected in 2025. Banta also addresses the challenges of integrating AI into mobile devices, the importance of a diverse semiconductor supply chain, and the environmental considerations in AI development.

title: Israel grants Intel $3.2bn for new $25bn chip plant
summary: Israel has approved a $3.2 billion grant for Intel to build a new $25 billion chip manufacturing plant in southern Israel, marking the largest investment ever in the country. In addition to the grant, Intel has committed to purchasing $16.6 billion worth of goods and services from Israeli suppliers over the next decade. This investment comes amid the ongoing conflict in Gaza and is seen as a significant show of support by a major US company for Israel's economy.

title: Midday Movers: AMD Higher on Graphics Card Report, Boeing Falls on Inspection Issue
summary: U.S. equities saw marginal gains midday Thursday, with the S&P 500 nearing an all-time high amidst a "Santa Claus Rally". Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) rose on news of a new graphics card release, while Boeing (BA) shares fell after an alert about potential loose bolts on its 737 Max aircraft, adding to production concerns. Other movers included Match Group (MTCH) and Nike (NKE) which gained, and Palo Alto Networks (PANW) which declined.

title: Twelve Japanese companies join forces to develop automotive SoCs using chiplet technology
summary: A consortium named ‘Advanced SoC Research for Automotive’ (ASRA) comprising twelve leading Japanese companies, including major automakers, electrical component manufacturers, and semiconductor firms, has been established to research and develop high-performance automotive System-on-Chip (SoC) using chiplet technology. The goal is to integrate these SoCs into mass-production vehicles from 2030 onwards. Keiji Yamamoto of Toyota Motor Corporation will chair ASRA, with Nobuaki Kawahara of Denso Corporation serving as executive director.

title: Netherlands Blocks ASML Exports of Some Chip-Making Equipment to China
summary: The Netherlands has partially blocked ASML's exports of certain lithography systems to China, affecting the shipment of NXT:2050i and NXT:2100i systems in 2023. This decision follows U.S. export restrictions and impacts China's ability to produce advanced microprocessors. ASML, a crucial chip-equipment manufacturer, confirmed the Dutch government's partial revocation of the export license.

title: The U.S.-China Chip War: Who Dares to Win?
summary: The U.S. and China are engaged in a semiconductor industry rivalry, with the U.S. aiming to boost domestic chip production through the CHIPS and Science Act while imposing sanctions on China to hinder its self-reliance in the sector. However, the CHIPS Act faces challenges like high production costs and specific conditions that may deter leading companies. Other nations like the EU and Japan are also implementing strategies to increase their semiconductor manufacturing capabilities amid global supply chain concerns.

title: Cerebras Systems Appoints Shirley Li as General Counsel

Li, who previously served as Chief Legal and People Officer at Snapdocs, Inc. and held legal roles at Cadence Design Systems, Inc., will report directly to CEO Andrew Feldman. Her appointment comes at a time of significant expansion for Cerebras, following major customer partnerships and the development of key AI models in 2023.

title: Microchip wins $72 million federal CHIPS Act grant, Oregon’s first, for Gresham expansion
summary: Microchip Technology received a $72 million federal CHIPS Act grant for its $800 million Gresham factory expansion, marking Oregon's first such grant. This funding is part of a larger $162 million subsidy Microchip received, which also includes $90 million for a Colorado plant expansion, aiming to triple the domestic production of semiconductors essential for various industries. The federal grant complements an earlier $11 million award from Oregon and is expected to boost the state's economy and create 300 jobs.

title: US plans $162 million award to Microchip Technology to boost production
summary: The U.S. Commerce Department plans to award Microchip Technology $162 million in government grants to increase domestic production of semiconductors and microcontroller units. This funding aims to triple production at two U.S. factories, reducing reliance on foreign supply chains for crucial components used in various industries. This award is part of the "Chips for America" program, designed to boost U.S. semiconductor manufacturing and research.

The company's solid free cash flow generation provides flexibility for future growth initiatives.

The new AMD Ryzen™ 8000G Series for the AM5 platform introduces personal AI processing to desktop PCs with integrated AI neural processing units (NPUs) and powerful built-in graphics. AMD also extended its AM4 platform with new Ryzen™ 5000 processors, including the gaming-focused Ryzen™ 7 5700X3D with 3D V-Cache™ technology, and highlighted partnerships with OEMs like Lenovo, Razer, Asus, and Acer for new AI PCs featuring AMD Ryzen™ 8040 Series mobile processors.

title: Why Intel has declined to participate in the Science Based Targets initiative
summary: Intel has chosen not to formally participate in the Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi) despite aligning with its net-zero guidance. This decision stems from the company's long history of emissions reductions, which SBTi's near-term requirements do not fully account for, and the framework's inability to credit emissions reductions from new technologies like AI. While Intel has significantly reduced emissions historically, its absolute emissions are currently increasing due to expanded manufacturing output.

Despite the growth, the company revised its full-year fiscal 2024 revenue guidance downward due to a temporary slowing in the electric vehicle market's impact on silicon carbide orders and shifts in customer product mix impacting order timing. Aehr remains confident in long-term demand for its test solutions in various markets, including gallium nitride applications and silicon photonic optical I/O.

Additionally, Ambarella introduced two new automotive AI domain controllers, the CV3-AD635 and CV3-AD655, expanding its CV3-AD family for L2+ to L4 autonomous driving solutions.
