summary: This article examines the evolving economics of the video gaming industry in 2024, highlighting its massive revenue and attraction for investors. It discusses key trends, how to gain exposure, and strategies for finding valuable opportunities within this diverse sector. The piece also provides a brief market summary covering Evergrande's liquidation, LVMH's record revenue, UK profit warnings, Meta's dividend, US Fed rates, job openings, and Australia's inflation, before diving into gaming company types and future outlooks.

title: Exclusive | Intel Delays $20 Billion Ohio Project, Citing Slow Chip Market
summary: Intel is delaying its $20 billion chip-manufacturing project in Ohio, pushing the completion of its factories to late 2026. This delay is attributed to challenging market conditions and the slow distribution of U.S. government grants intended to boost the domestic semiconductor industry. The company's stock symbol is INTC.

title: Intel's Lunar Lake reference laptops aim to improve power efficiency with Cirrus Logic hardware
summary: Cirrus Logic has announced that its audio and power converter chips will be integrated into reference laptops powered by Intel's upcoming Lunar Lake CPUs, aiming to significantly enhance power efficiency and battery life. The new CP9314 power converter chip is expected to achieve record-breaking efficiency and enable thinner laptop designs. This move highlights Intel's focus on making its next-generation mobile CPUs as efficient as possible, potentially challenging Apple's M-series chips in the portable computing market.

title: Cadence, Avnet and Ansys launch new simulation tools for engineers
summary: Ansys has released its 2024 R1 update, focusing on UI improvements and AI integration, including tools like SimAI for generative AI workflows and speed boosts for Ansys Discovery. Cadence introduced Celsius Studio, an AI-enhanced platform for thermal design and analysis that unifies MCAD and ECAD models. Avnet launched version 3.0 of its RFSoC Explorer Toolbox for simulating over-the-air antenna to bits communications, and Cadence also released its Millennium Enterprise Multiphysics Platform, offering GPU-accelerated HPC for various simulation needs.

title: 21 Top Semiconductor Companies in India
summary: India is rapidly expanding its semiconductor industry, with a focus on both design and manufacturing, supported by the $10.2 billion India Semiconductor Mission. The article highlights 21 key companies, including major global players and local innovators, that are crucial to this growth, with the sector projected to reach $64 billion by 2026. These companies contribute to various aspects of semiconductor technology, from design and fabrication to memory solutions and AI processors.

title: Going Into Earnings, Is Nvidia a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Morningstar assesses Nvidia (NVDA) ahead of its Q4 earnings report, highlighting the dominance of its data center segment and the impact of AI growth. The company maintains a "Wide" economic moat due to intangible assets and switching costs, but its stock is considered overvalued with a "Very High" uncertainty rating and a fair value estimate of $480. Key factors to watch include supply constraints, U.S. restrictions on sales to China, and competition from hyperscalers developing in-house AI chips.

title: US-Japanese rail collaboration to inspect tracks with AI
summary: A US-Japanese rail collaboration has been established between Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) and JR Kyushu Railway Company to enhance track inspection using Artificial Intelligence. JR Kyushu, which previously relied on manual inspections for its extensive network, will now utilize an AI-based solution with the AMD Kria K26 system to improve safety and identify track anomalies like unscrewed bolts more effectively. This shift marks a significant move from labor-intensive methods to a more cost-efficient and operationally optimized approach for rail maintenance.

title: Intel Launches World’s First Systems Foundry Designed for the AI Era
summary: Intel has launched Intel Foundry, positioning it as the world's first systems foundry tailored for the AI era, with an expanded process roadmap targeting leadership by the end of the decade. The company announced new process technologies, ASAT capabilities, key customer design wins including Microsoft, and strong support from ecosystem partners. Intel Foundry also emphasized its commitment to sustainability, aiming for 100% renewable electricity and net-zero emissions by specific target dates.

title: Nvidia gives upbeat forecast as AI reaches ‘tipping point’
summary: Nvidia Corp. has announced an upbeat forecast, predicting approximately $24 billion in revenue for the current quarter, surpassing analyst estimates. This optimistic outlook follows strong fourth-quarter results, driven by surging demand for its AI accelerators, which CEO Jensen Huang states indicates AI has reached a "tipping point." The company's stock jumped significantly, reflecting its continued position as a primary beneficiary of the AI computing boom.

title: Intel Releases 2023 Product Security Report Highlighting Commitment to Security Assurance
summary: Intel has released its 2023 Product Security Report, demonstrating a strong commitment to security assurance with proactive investments accounting for 94% of vulnerability disclosures, the highest in five years. The report highlights a 39% reduction in hardware and firmware vulnerabilities compared to 2022 and notes Intel's leadership in product security assurance as validated by ABI Research. This commitment is supported by a record 256 researchers participating in Intel's Bug Bounty program, underscoring the company's transparent approach to security.

title: Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon to Deliver Keynote Speech at COMPUTEX 2024
summary: Cristiano Amon, President and CEO of Qualcomm Incorporated, will deliver a keynote speech at COMPUTEX 2024 on June 3. He will discuss how on-device generative AI and next-generation computing, particularly advanced AI PCs powered by Snapdragon® X Series, will transform productivity and creativity. The event, themed "Connecting AI," will feature other industry leaders and showcase 1,500 exhibitors.

title: How Nvidia grew to a trillion-dollar company fueling the AI revolution
summary: Nvidia has grown into a nearly $3 trillion company by specializing in Graphics Processing Units (GPUs), which have become crucial for the artificial intelligence revolution. Initially used for gaming, GPUs' ability to perform concurrent calculations makes them ideal for AI, giving Nvidia a significant first-mover advantage and market dominance. The company's unique technology and supply chain have led to widespread adoption by major tech firms, with discussions emerging about potential shortages and the need for continued investment in chip manufacturing.

title: Micron begins production of a 'better' HBM3E memory
summary: Micron Technology has started volume production of HBM3E DRAM for AI datacenters, claiming its offering consumes 30 percent less power than competitors. Its 24Gbyte 8-high HBM3E components will be featured in Nvidia's H200 tensor core GPUs shipping in Q2 2024, potentially positioning Micron ahead of rival SK Hynix in HBM3E production. The company is also preparing a 12-high 36Gbyte HBM3E component for introduction in March 2024, with its stock opening almost 5 percent higher on the news.

title: AMD CEO Lisa Su just sold $20 million in stock. Was it ‘opportunistic’?
summary: AMD CEO Lisa Su recently sold 125,000 shares of AMD stock worth over $20 million through a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. While one expert viewed this as a deviation from her usual market activity, the company stated the sale was not unusual. Su still maintains a significant stake in AMD valued at $700 million.

title: TCL QD Mini LED 115 inch TV (2024): 4K IMAX Screen for gamers
summary: TCL has launched a new 115-inch QD Mini LED TV (model 115QM891) featuring 4K resolution, up to 20,000 local dimming zones, an AIPQ ULTRA processor, and IMAX Enhanced Certification. Targeting gamers, it offers refresh rates up to 240Hz with VRR and AMD FreeSync Premium. TCL also introduced new S-series and Q-series models at CES 2024, including various QLED and Mini LED options with enhanced brightness, processing power, and gaming features.

title: Best Long-Term Semiconductor Stocks According To Redditors: Favorites Beyond Nvidia
summary: This article explores Reddit's discussions on the best long-term semiconductor stocks, moving beyond Nvidia to consider other significant players. Recommendations include diversified ETFs like VanEck Vectors Semiconductor ETF (SMH), foundational manufacturers like ASML and Applied Materials, upcoming challengers such as AMD, and growth opportunities with Broadcom and TSMC. The community emphasizes the importance of thorough research and personal financial goals in investment decisions within the semiconductor sector.

title: Citadel's Ken Griffin Oversees $500 Billion in Managed Assets: These Are His Top 5 Holdings
summary: Ken Griffin's Citadel Advisors manages $500 billion in assets and is a top-performing hedge fund. SEC filings reveal its top non-derivative holdings, which include the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust, Nvidia, Microsoft, Amazon, and Advanced Micro Devices. These investments highlight a strategy of betting on market indexes and major players in tech and AI while acknowledging potential risks and sector trends.

title: AMD Extends Market-Leading FPGA Portfolio with AMD Spartan UltraScale+ Family Built for Cost-Sensitive Edge Applications
summary: AMD has announced the Spartan™ UltraScale+™ FPGA family, expanding its portfolio of cost-optimized FPGAs and adaptive SoCs for edge applications. These new FPGAs offer high I/O counts, power efficiency (up to 30% lower than previous generations), and enhanced security features, making them suitable for embedded vision, industrial networking, and robotics. Sampling and evaluation kits are expected in the first half of 2025.

title: Vishay Intertechnology Acquires Nexperia’s Newport Wafer Fab for $177 Million
summary: Vishay Intertechnology has acquired Nexperia’s Newport wafer fabrication facility in South Wales, U.K., for approximately $177 million. This acquisition aims to position the Newport fab as a manufacturing excellence center for Net Zero Transformation, focusing on decarbonization and electrification, and accelerating Vishay's participation in the SiC MOSFETs and diodes marketplace. Vishay plans to invest in the facility to grow capacity and advance its SiC and GaN production and technology development.

title: HP Inc. CEO: Security Will Be A ‘Key Advantage’ In Our AI PC Strategy
summary: HP CEO Enrique Lores announced that security will be a "key advantage" in the company's AI PC strategy, emphasizing local large language model processing for enhanced security, lower costs, and faster performance compared to cloud solutions. HP plans to leverage its Wolf Security technologies to protect AI models running on these new PCs, which are expected to launch in the second half of 2024. Lores believes AI PCs represent a significant opportunity for channel partners due to device sales and the coming wave of AI-optimized software.

title: Intel’s Dominance in the Chip Market: An Analysis
summary: This article analyzes Intel's enduring dominance in the chip market, highlighting its strategic use of pricing, market segmentation, and significant R&D investments. It details Intel's chip advancements, its competitive landscape with rivals like AMD and NVIDIA, and the impact of the CHIPS Act on its growth. The piece also covers the rebranding of Altera and elaborates on Intel's product strategies, including its new Xeon processors and various Core i-series price points.

title: IWD 2024: From Chennai to California: What I've learned from a lifetime in STEM
summary: Gayathri Natarajan, a Program Manager at Synopsys, shares her journey in STEM from Chennai, India, to California, highlighting the often-unseen applications of STEM in daily life. She emphasizes the importance of women supporting women, crediting her grandmother's encouragement for her academic path and decision to study abroad. Natarajan advocates for more women in STEM, detailing her efforts in founding and joining women's networking groups to empower and mentor future generations.

title: HP's 6 Biggest Announcements At Amplify Partner Conference 2024
summary: At its Amplify Partner Conference 2024, HP unveiled several major advancements focusing on hybrid work and AI integration. Key announcements included new business laptops and mobile workstations featuring AI capabilities, an expanded partnership with Nvidia for AI Studio software, and a revamped Workplace Experience Platform (WEX) with AI-powered management. HP also introduced new conferencing solutions, an AI MasterClass program for partners, and new Color LaserJet Pro 3000 printers for SMBs.

title: Happy 25th QQQ
summary: The Invesco Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) celebrated its 25th anniversary, showcasing its significant growth and position as the fifth-largest ETF. The article discusses QQQ's composition, performance history, and highlights alternatives like QQQM for lower costs and QQQE for an equal-weighted approach, emphasizing QQQ's role in innovative company investments.

title: AMD CEO Lisa Su Talks the Invisible Role of A.I. Chips in Oscar-Winning Films
summary: AMD CEO Lisa Su highlighted the significant role of A.I. chips in the production of Oscar-winning animated films at SXSW, noting how they drastically accelerate rendering times. She explained that AMD's hardware is used by partners like Pixar and Wētā FX, enabling sophisticated visual effects that were previously far more time-consuming. The executive VFX producer David Conley also emphasized the critical partnership with companies like AMD for the future of filmmaking, especially with the integration of real-time processing and A.I.

title: Semiconductor Intellectual Property (IP) Companies -Arm Limited (UK) and Synopsys, Inc. (US) are the Key Players
summary: The semiconductor intellectual property (IP) market is projected to grow from USD 7.5 billion in 2024 to USD 11.2 billion by 2029, driven by increasing demand for electronics in healthcare and telecommunications. Key players like Arm Limited and Synopsys, Inc. are strengthening their market position through acquisitions and partnerships, focusing on new processor IP and advanced microcontroller units. The market faces challenges such as concerns related to Moore's Law and increasing IP thefts, but opportunities arise from the rising demand for semiconductor components in automotive and data center verticals.

title: Intel’s chief people officer: Humans need to be at the center of AI
summary: Intel's chief people officer, Christy Pambianchi, emphasizes the importance of keeping humans at the center of artificial intelligence development and integration. The company, an early pioneer in microprocessors, is now deeply invested in AI through initiatives like its AI Inside program, proprietary iGPT, and a significant AI upskilling program. Pambianchi explains how Intel plans to responsibly incorporate AI into its workforce, encourage employee adoption, and prepare staff for future AI-driven roles.

title: Applied Digital to sell 200MW Garden City data center campus to Marathon Digital
summary: Applied Digital is selling its 200MW Garden City data center campus in Texas to Marathon Digital Holdings for $87.3 million. This strategic move aims to pivot Applied Digital away from cryptomining hosting and towards high-performance computing (HPC) and AI customers, leveraging the sale to bolster its balance sheet and fund HPC infrastructure development. Marathon Digital, already a significant customer, will take over the facility, highlighting a shift in Applied Digital's business focus.

title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. stock underperforms Monday when compared to competitors
summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) stock fell by 0.21% on Monday, closing at $190.65, despite a positive trading session for the broader stock market. The S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average both saw gains. AMD's stock closed $36.65 below its 52-week high of $227.30, which it reached on March 8th.

title: AMD Showcases Networking Security Innovations, 800G Ethernet Interoperability at OFC 2024
summary: AMD will showcase its latest networking security innovations and 800G Ethernet interoperability at OFC 2024. The demonstrations include 800G Ethernet using Versal Premium devices, AI-inferencing for DDoS mitigation, and 100Gbps TLS-1.3 offload for enhanced network security. These advancements aim to address the increasing demand for network performance and security in core network infrastructures and edge nodes.

title: Gallium Nitride Market Size 2024 Industry Analysis, Key Players, Regional Demand, Revenue, Opportunity and Forecast 2032
summary: The global gallium nitride (GaN) market is projected to reach US$ 298 million by 2030, growing at a CAGR of 9.5% from 2021 to 2030, driven by its superior properties for electronic devices, energy efficiency, and applications in hybrid electric vehicles and 5G infrastructure. North America leads the market, while Asia Pacific, particularly China, shows substantial growth due to R&D and technological advancements. Key players include Cree Inc., Infineon Technologies AG, and GaN Systems Inc.

title: AMDL ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns NASDAQ:AMDL
summary: This article provides an analysis of the GraniteShares 2x Long AMD Daily ETF (AMDL), an ETF designed as a short-term tactical tool to deliver twice the daily price return of AMD stock. It details AMDL's key statistics such as its $595.63 million AUM, 1.07% expense ratio, and its active management style without an underlying index. The analysis emphasizes that AMDL does not pay dividends and highlights its recent performance, including a significant yearly increase.

title: Lenovo Vehicle Computing, WeRide Forge Partnership For Autonomous Driving Solutions
summary: Lenovo Vehicle Computing and WeRide have partnered to develop Level 4 autonomous driving solutions for commercial applications. The collaboration will leverage Lenovo's AD1 domain controller, based on the NVIDIA DRIVE Thor platform, and integrate it into WeRide One, a versatile autonomous driving solution. This partnership aims to deliver enhanced autonomous driving products and services globally, focusing on safe and reliable commercial autonomous vehicles.

title: Qualcomm Announces the Snapdragon 7+ Gen 3, Featuring Exceptional On-Device AI Capabilities
summary: Qualcomm has unveiled the Snapdragon 7+ Gen 3 Mobile Platform, bringing on-device generative AI capabilities to the Snapdragon 7 series, including support for various large language models. The new platform also enhances gaming with Snapdragon Elite Gaming features like the Game Post Processing Accelerator and Adreno Frame Motion Engine 2, and introduces Wi-Fi 7 to the 7-series. Key OEMs such as OnePlus, realme, and SHARP are expected to adopt this platform in upcoming devices.

title: What’s Inside Direxion’s Daily Semiconductor Bull and Bear 3x shares?
summary: This article describes the Direxion Daily Semiconductor Bull (SOXL) and Bear 3X Shares (SOXS) ETFs. It explains that these funds aim for daily investment results of 300%, or 300% of the inverse, of the performance of the NYSE Semiconductor Index, while cautioning that investment objectives are not guaranteed. The article also provides a link for more information about Direxion.

title: Should You Invest in the Invesco PHLX Semiconductor ETF (SOXQ)?
summary: The Invesco PHLX Semiconductor ETF (SOXQ) offers diversified and low-cost exposure to the Technology - Semiconductors sector, tracking the PHLX Semiconductor Sector Index. With $359.59 million in assets and a 0.19% expense ratio, it has shown strong performance, gaining 17.37% year-to-date and 57.63% in the last year as of March 26, 2024. The ETF holds a Zacks ETF Rank of 1 (Strong Buy), making it a recommended option for investors.

title: SOSA aligned wideband multichannel RF digitizers from Analog Devices aim at reduced SWaP
summary: Analog Devices has introduced the ADSY1100 series of wideband multichannel RF digitizers, designed in a 3U VPX SOSA-aligned format. These digitizers are intended for use in military and aerospace applications such as electronic warfare, radar, and communications, emphasizing reduced size, weight, and power (SWaP). The system features ADI's "Apollo" MXFE device with high DAC and ADC sample rates and integrated DSP to free up FPGA resources for mission-specific processing.

title: AMD’s Lisa Su to speak at Computex
summary: AMD CEO Lisa Su is scheduled to deliver the opening keynote speech at Computex Taipei on June 3, discussing the future of high-performance computing and AI. She will explore how AMD and its partners are advancing AI and HPC from data centers to end-user devices. The Computex event, themed "Connecting AI," will also feature other prominent tech leaders and is expected to include an AI cooperation announcement between Nvidia and Hon Hai.

title: Newegg Makes Tech More Affordable for Students, Faculty and Staff with Affirm Offer
summary: Newegg announced a new 10% discount for students, faculty, and staff when using an .edu email address and checking out with Affirm. This offer can be combined with other promotions, maximizing savings on technology products like laptops and PCs. The discount applies to orders over $100 and can be used multiple times, aiming to ease the financial burden of technology purchases for students.

title: Aehr wins major order for SiC wafer test and burn-in system
summary: Aehr Test Systems has secured a major order for its FOX-NP wafer level test and burn-in system for Silicon Carbide (SiC) devices from a prominent semiconductor company. This system, featuring new advanced voltage channel modules, will be used for engineering, qualification, and small lot production, with plans to transition to high-volume production using Aehr's FOX-XP systems. The customer, a leading semiconductor firm with global operations, is entering the SiC market to address applications in automotive, industrial, and electrification infrastructure.

title: The Zacks Analyst Blog Highlights Berkshire Hathaway, Procter & Gamble, Adobe, Advanced Micro Devices and AstraZeneca
summary: This Zacks Analyst Blog highlights research reports on major stocks including Berkshire Hathaway, Procter & Gamble, and Adobe, along with Advanced Micro Devices and AstraZeneca PLC. It provides an overview of each company's recent performance, key financial drivers, and potential headwinds. The article emphasizes Zacks Investment Research's stock-picking strategies and their strong historical performance.

title: The top performing funds that beat the S&P 500 in Q1
summary: This article highlights the top-performing US equity funds in Q1 2024 that outperformed the S&P 500 index. Funds from GQG, Artemis, and Alger are featured, with GQG Partners U.S. Equity leading the pack. The piece details the investment strategies of these funds and specific holdings that contributed to their success.

title: AMD Advances Open Source Initiative with MES Firmware Release for Radeon GPUs
summary: AMD announced its plan to open-source the Micro Engine Scheduler (MES) firmware for its Radeon GPUs, enhancing user control and accessibility. This move directly addresses lobbying efforts from companies like Tiny Corp and could facilitate the integration of third-party chiplets. The company also anticipates significant growth from its MI300s, projecting substantial revenue increases in the coming fiscal years.

title: TSMC Arizona and U.S. Department of Commerce Announce up to US$6.6 Billion in Proposed CHIPS Act Direct Funding, the Company Plans Third Leading-Edge Fab in Phoenix
summary: TSMC Arizona and the U.S. Department of Commerce have signed a preliminary memorandum for up to US$6.6 billion in CHIPS Act direct funding. TSMC plans to build a third advanced semiconductor fab in Phoenix, Arizona, bringing its total investment in the site to over US$65 billion. This expansion aims to enhance U.S. semiconductor manufacturing capabilities and create thousands of high-tech jobs, with production from the three fabs utilizing 4nm, 2nm, and more advanced processes.

title: AMD Extends Leadership Adaptive SoC Portfolio with New Versal Series Gen 2 Devices Delivering End-to-End Acceleration for AI-Driven Embedded Systems
summary: AMD has announced the expansion of its Versal adaptive SoC portfolio with the new Versal AI Edge Series Gen 2 and Versal Prime Series Gen 2 devices, designed for end-to-end acceleration in AI-driven embedded systems. These devices offer significantly improved TOPs-per-watt and CPU-based scalar compute compared to the first generation, enabling single-chip intelligence for various applications. Subaru Corporation has already selected the Versal AI Edge Series Gen 2 for its next-generation EyeSight ADAS vision system.

title: Intel challenges Nvidia with new Gaudi 3 AI chip as AMD expands processor lineup
summary: Intel has launched its new Gaudi 3 AI chip, promising significantly improved performance over its predecessor and competitive speeds against Nvidia's H100 and H200 chips for training and inference of large language models. Simultaneously, AMD expanded its AI processor lineup with new Versal AI Edge Series Gen 2 and Prime Series Gen 2 chips, targeting edge computing devices and leveraging customizable FPGA technology. Both companies are pushing boundaries in the AI hardware space, with Intel also focusing on an open platform for enterprise AI and AMD enhancing its offerings for connected devices.

title: 20 Chip Manufacturing Companies in India to Know
summary: This article highlights 20 key chip manufacturing companies in India, emphasizing the country's growing importance in the semiconductor industry across design, fabrication, and materials development. It details how the rise of AI has heightened the demand for advanced chipsets, positioning India as a crucial hub for global semiconductor firms. The listed companies include major players like NVIDIA, AMD, Samsung, and Intel, alongside specialized Indian firms contributing to various aspects of semiconductor technology.

title: Abbott Laboratories to close Bay Area site; almost 200 jobs to be cut
summary: Abbott Laboratories is closing its Fairfield, California location later this year, leading to the layoff of 195 employees. The company, known for brands like Similac and Pedialyte, attributed a decrease in its annual sales to an anticipated decline in COVID-19 testing-related sales, despite overall sales growth in the fourth quarter. Fairfield Mayor Cathrine Moy expressed frustration over the timing of the layoffs and the company's decision to discontinue the nutritional bar previously made at the plant.

title: Like Nvidia Stock but Prefer ETFs? This Is the Best Semiconductor ETF to Invest In Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Other Megatrends.
summary: The VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH) is highlighted as an excellent option for investors seeking exposure to the semiconductor industry and megatrends like AI, vehicle electrification, and autonomous driving, while mitigating the risk of investing in individual stocks like Nvidia (NVDA). The ETF has Nvidia as its largest holding, accounting for 20% of its portfolio, and has significantly outperformed the S&P 500 over various timeframes due to its focus on the robustly growing chip market. It provides diversification across the semiconductor value chain, including chip producers, foundries, and equipment manufacturers.

title: AMD’s stock can surge 40%, this new bull says — even as Nvidia still dominates
summary: HSBC analyst Frank Lee has upgraded AMD shares to buy from hold, setting a new price target of $225, which suggests a 40% upside. This comes despite Nvidia's dominance in the AI GPU market, as Lee believes there will be multiple winners in this sector. The upgrade reflects a positive outlook for AMD's performance in the burgeoning AI chip industry.

title: AMD and Nvidia unveil new processors for AI PCs
summary: AMD and Nvidia have both launched new processors designed for AI PCs, aiming to capitalize on the growing market for computers capable of running AI workloads on-device. AMD introduced its Ryzen PRO 8040 and 8000 Series processors, emphasizing their AI-enabled features for business users, while Nvidia unveiled new RTX A400 and A1000 GPUs with enhanced Tensor and RT Cores for accelerating AI and graphics tasks. This move by both companies highlights the intensifying competition in the AI PC sector, which analysts predict will drive new growth in the overall PC market.

title: Steering Clients Through Unstable Markets
summary: Paul Schatz, president of Heritage Capital, discussed with etf.com his strategies for navigating client portfolios through current market instability characterized by higher inflation and interest rates. He has reduced exposure to "hot" AI and tech stocks, finding new opportunities in "unloved" sectors like pharmaceuticals, chemicals, and staples. Schatz also shared his market outlook, anticipating a first-quarter peak followed by a minor pullback and potential for fresh highs over the summer, while also discussing his cautious approach to bonds.

title: Micron Poised to Get Over $6 Billion in Chips Grants in Announcement Next Week
summary: Micron Technology Inc. is expected to receive $6.1 billion in grants from the Commerce Department under the CHIPS Act. This funding will support domestic factory projects as part of efforts to boost US semiconductor production. The announcement is anticipated next week, and Micron, like Intel and TSMC, will also accept loans as part of its award package.

title: US to Provide $6.1 Billion for Micron Technology Chip Plants
summary: The Biden administration will provide $6.1 billion in government support for Micron Technology to produce advanced memory computer chips in New York and Idaho. This funding, from the 2022 CHIPS and Science Act, aims to revive the manufacturing sector, create 50,000 jobs, and bolster domestic semiconductor production. Micron plans to invest a total of $100 billion in upstate New York over two decades and $15 billion in Boise, Idaho.

title: The Latest Breakthroughs in Quantum Computing
summary: This article discusses recent breakthroughs in quantum computing, focusing on two key developments. Researchers at Kyushu University have created a qubit that achieved quantum coherence at room temperature for 100 nanoseconds, a significant step towards practical quantum computers. Additionally, scientists have found that thin films of bismuth display the non-linear Hall effect at room temperature, which could lead to new components for high-speed electronics and prevent supercomputers from overheating.

title: TSMC Accelerates Nearshoring to Diversify Away from China
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) is significantly expanding its manufacturing presence in the United States, increasing its investment from $40 billion to $65 billion, primarily in Phoenix, Arizona. This strategic move, supported by $6.6 billion in U.S. government subsidies, aims to decentralize chip production away from Taiwan, enhance U.S. technological sovereignty, and create thousands of jobs. The expansion reflects a broader industry trend towards nearshoring and diversifying supply chains to mitigate geopolitical risks and reduce reliance on Asian markets for advanced semiconductor manufacturing.

title: A Brief and Personal History of EDA, Part 5: The Acquisition Era
summary: This article, part five of a personal history of EDA, details the acquisition era of Electronic Design Automation (EDA) and the significant role played by leading companies like Cadence, Synopsys, and Mentor Graphics. It highlights the aggressive acquisition strategies employed by these firms to dominate the market and lists numerous acquired companies and their technologies, emphasizing the dynamic nature of the EDA industry. The author also touches on the role of consultants like Jim Hogan in fostering and preparing startups for acquisition.

title: Cadence emulator and prototyping duo can handle up to 48 billion gate SoCs
summary: Cadence Design Systems has introduced its third-generation Palladium Z3 emulation and Protium X3 FPGA prototyping systems, offering significant capacity and performance improvements for advanced SoC development. These systems can handle designs from 16 million up to 48 billion gates, enabling comprehensive testing of large SoCs. Powered by Nvidia's BlueField DPU and Quantum InfiniBand, the solutions are designed to accelerate hardware verification and software validation, especially for AI, automotive, and hyperscale applications.

title: Advanced Packaging Market Aims for Remarkable Growth, Targeting USD 79.9 Billion by 2033 at 8.4% CAGR
summary: The advanced packaging market is projected to grow from USD 35.7 billion in 2023 to USD 79.9 billion by 2033, achieving an 8.4% CAGR. This growth is driven by the increasing demand for miniaturization of devices and advanced packaging solutions for integrated circuits in various sectors, especially consumer electronics and automotive. The Asia Pacific region is anticipated to dominate the market due to its prominent semiconductor manufacturing industry.

title: Micron Becomes First Brand to Produce 200+ Layer QLC NAND SSDs for Client and Data Centers
summary: Micron Technology announced that its 232-layer QLC NAND is now in mass production and shipping in select Crucial SSDs, as well as being sampled to OEM PC manufacturers in the Micron 2500 NVMe SSD. This makes Micron the first brand to produce 200+ layer QLC NAND SSDs for both client and data center applications, offering significant improvements in bit-density, I/O speeds, and read/programming performance. The Micron 2500 client SSD is highlighted for its ability to enhance the PC user experience and accelerate QLC adoption with its superior performance and endurance.

title: CEOs of Microsoft, Nvidia and other tech giants join federal AI advisory board
summary: A federal AI advisory board has been established, comprising prominent tech executives, academics, and civil rights leaders, including CEOs from Microsoft, Nvidia, and OpenAI. Led by Secretary of Homeland Security Alejandro Mayorkas, the board will advise the Department of Homeland Security on the safe application of AI in critical infrastructure and issue recommendations for organizations like power grid operators and manufacturers. This initiative stems from President Biden's executive order on AI, which also mandates data sharing on new AI models' safety with the government.

title: AMD Q1 Earnings Preview: Analysts Bullish, But Warn Of Factors That 'Can Derail The Semiconductor Industry'
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ: AMD) is set to release its Q1 earnings, with analysts expecting revenue of $5.46 billion and earnings of 61 cents per share. While many analysts are bullish due to strong AI MI300 and EPYC performance, they also highlight risks such as market share declines, intense competition, and macroeconomic uncertainties that could impact the semiconductor industry. AMD shares have performed well over the last year, but some analysts have adjusted price targets ahead of the report.

title: AMD Earnings: A snapshot of Advanced Micro Devices’ Q1 2024 results
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) reported an increase in revenues and adjusted earnings for the first quarter of 2024. Adjusted earnings rose to $0.62 per share, and March-quarter revenues increased to $5.47 billion, signaling a recovery in demand. The company also shifted from a net loss in the prior-year period to a net income of $123 million in Q1 2024.

title: 4 Undervalued Semiconductor Stocks
summary: Despite the AI boom driving up semiconductor stock prices, Morningstar analysts have identified four undervalued semiconductor stocks: Infineon Technologies, STMicroelectronics, MediaTek, and Skyworks Solutions. While digital chipmakers are largely overvalued due to AI, analog/mixed-signal chip valuations are still below fair value estimates. These top picks are favored for their strong positions in automotive semis, leadership in silicon-carbide, expansion into new markets, and essential RF products for mobile devices.

title: AMD is the latest company to show that AI is an expensive proposition
summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) shares slid 9% after its latest earnings report, highlighting that artificial intelligence is an expensive endeavor for both chip buyers and manufacturers. AMD discussed increased investment spending to support its AI product portfolio, which is impacting the gross-margin profile for its data-center graphics processing units. This indicates that while AI presents significant opportunities, it also demands substantial financial commitment.

title: AMD AI chip revenue forecast fails to impress, shares dive 7%
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) shares fell by about 7% after its forecast for AI chip sales for 2024, at roughly $4 billion, failed to meet Wall Street's expectations, despite being an increase from its prior estimate. The company faces stiff competition from Nvidia in the booming AI server semiconductor market and is experiencing weakness in traditional server, gaming, and embedded segments. AMD also reported first-quarter revenue that slightly beat estimates and provided a second-quarter revenue forecast in line with analyst expectations.

title: Astera Labs Inc call volume above normal and directionally bullish
summary: Astera Labs Inc. (ALAB) is experiencing unusually high and bullish call option volume, with 2,217 calls trading, exceeding expected levels, and implied volatility increasing. The most active options are the May-24 80 calls and Jul-24 100 calls, accounting for nearly 920 contracts, while the Put/Call Ratio stands at 0.33. Earnings for Astera Labs are anticipated on May 7th.

title: AI In Chip Design Market Size, Share | CAGR of 31.4%
summary: The global AI in chip design market is projected to reach USD 27.6 billion by 2033, growing from USD 1.8 billion in 2023 at a CAGR of 31.4%. This growth is driven by the increasing integration of AI in consumer electronics and the expansion of AI applications in sectors like healthcare, despite challenges such as high development costs and technological complexity. North America currently dominates the market due to its robust technological infrastructure and significant investments in R&D.

title: Who’s making chips for AI? Chinese manufacturers lag behind US tech giants
summary: Chinese researchers are reportedly 5-10 years behind their US counterparts in AI chip manufacturing due to US export restrictions. The rapid advancements in generative AI have intensified a global race for computer chips, highlighting China's struggle to keep pace with key technologies under these limitations.

title: Rapid7 Earnings: What To Look For From RPD
summary: Cybersecurity software maker Rapid7 (NASDAQ: RPD) is poised to release its earnings report tomorrow after market close. Analysts are expecting revenue growth of 11.4% year-on-year to $204 million and adjusted earnings of $0.54 per share. The company's stock has seen a 7.1% decline in the last month amidst broader volatility in growth stocks and an average analyst price target of $59.7.

title: ST rolls a new FD-SOI microcontroller with phase-change memory (PCM) for automotive applications
summary: STMicroelectronics has announced a new generation of automotive microcontrollers featuring embedded phase-change memory (ePCM), fabricated by Samsung using a jointly developed 18nm FD-SOI process. These microcontrollers target the automotive industry with its stringent temperature requirements, addressing a historical challenge for PCM technology. ST plans to sample these devices to automotive customers in the second half of 2024, with production slated for the latter half of 2025.

title: Robert Dennard, Inventor of Computer DRAM at IBM, Dies at 91
summary: Robert Dennard, the IBM engineer credited with inventing dynamic random access memory (DRAM) in the late 1960s, has passed away at the age of 91. His groundbreaking invention was crucial in the development of personal computers, mobile phones, tablets, and video game players. Dennard's death was announced on April 23, according to a notice in the Journal News.

title: Should You Buy Nvidia Stock Before the AI Chipmaker Reports Earnings on May 22?
summary: Nvidia's stock has seen a significant surge due to demand for its GPUs driven by generative AI. With first-quarter earnings approaching on May 22nd, analysts have high expectations for the chipmaker. Investors are advised to be cautious due to the stock's current pricey valuation, despite its strong market position and growth potential in the AI economy.

title: Nvidia's next-generation GPU revealed: integrating eight HBM 4, TSMC N3 process
summary: Nvidia's upcoming R100 AI chip is expected to enter mass production in Q4 2025, utilizing TSMC's N3 process and integrating eight HBM4s. The company is also focusing on improving energy consumption alongside AI computing power in its next-generation chips. This aggressive roadmap reflects Nvidia's strategy to accelerate product release cycles to maintain market leadership against competitors like AMD.

title: Liqid composability tech aims to turn Dell servers into GPU powerhouses
summary: Liqid's composability technology aims to transform Dell servers into powerful AI model training systems by enabling a single Dell R760 server to access up to 30 NVIDIA L40S GPUs. This UltraStack configuration, developed with Dell and NVIDIA, promises significant reductions in capital and operational expenses while boosting AI performance, efficiency, and sustainability. Liqid's SmartStack technology also allows efficient allocation of GPU resources to multiple clients or modular server nodes, supporting various Dell PowerEdge systems.

title: HP Unlocks New Era of AI Experiences Powering Work and Creation
summary: HP has unveiled new AI PCs, the HP OmniBook X AI PC and HP EliteBook Ultra AI PC, designed with ARM architecture and Snapdragon X Elite processors to power AI experiences locally. These devices aim to enhance productivity and creativity with features like Copilot+ PC and improved collaboration tools. HP highlights their sleek design, long battery life, sustainability, and robust security for both consumer and commercial users.

title: AMD Instinct MI300X Accelerators Power Microsoft Azure OpenAI Service Workloads and New Azure ND MI300X V5 VMs
summary: AMD announced that its Instinct MI300X accelerators are powering Microsoft Azure OpenAI Service workloads and the new Azure ND MI300X V5 VMs. These VMs are now generally available, with Hugging Face as a key customer, leveraging the MI300X and ROCm software to achieve leading price/performance for GPT workloads. The collaboration also extends to AMD Ryzen AI processors for edge deployments and Alveo MA35D media accelerators for live streaming video services, highlighting a broad partnership between AMD and Microsoft in AI and enterprise computing.

title: Supermicro Introduces High-Density, Efficient, and Cost Optimized Solutions Powered by the AMD EPYC™ 4004 Series Processors
summary: Supermicro has announced new additions to its H13 generation of CPU Servers, featuring AMD EPYC 4004 Series processors, designed for high-density, efficiency, and cost optimization. These solutions include a 3U MicroCloud multi-node server capable of supporting up to ten nodes for cloud-native workloads, offering significant density improvements and reduced TCO for data centers. The new servers are targeted at cloud hosting providers, SMBs, and departmental customers, providing powerful yet compact computing for various applications.

title: NVIDIA Announces Financial Results for First Quarter Fiscal 2025
summary: NVIDIA reported record quarterly revenue of $26.0 billion for Q1 FY25, a 262% increase year-over-year, driven by strong demand for AI in its Data Center segment. The company also announced a ten-for-one forward stock split and a 150% increase in its quarterly cash dividend. NVIDIA outlined significant platform advancements, including the Blackwell platform and Spectrum-X, positioning itself for continued growth in the AI market.

title: Trading Strategies For Nvidia Stock Before And After Q1 Earnings, A Top Holding In Spear Invest ETF (SPRX)
summary: This article discusses trading strategies for Nvidia stock ahead of its Q1 earnings report, noting its historical post-earnings surges and current technical indicators. It also highlights the Spear Alpha ETF (SPRX) as a diversified investment option for those bullish on Nvidia, given Nvidia's significant weighting in the fund alongside other tech companies like AMD and Snowflake. The ETF focuses on long-term capital growth by investing in companies capitalizing on emerging trends in industrial technology.

title: With the rise of Ethernet, Nvidia Infiniband is being encroached upon
summary: Nvidia's InfiniBand sales are being encroached upon by the growing demand for Ethernet in data centers, particularly from hyperscale enterprises and cloud builders. While InfiniBand still accounts for a large portion of Nvidia's network sales, the company is actively pushing its Spectrum X Ethernet products, expecting them to become a multi-billion dollar product line. Despite this internal cannibalization, Nvidia anticipates continued strong growth, with CEO Jensen Huang emphasizing the company's commitment to both InfiniBand and Ethernet as computing structures, alongside NVLink.

title: AMD, Workday, Alibaba, Super Micro Computer, and Other Tech Stocks in Focus Today
summary: Tech stocks, including AMD, Workday, Alibaba, and Super Micro Computer, are in focus today. The rebound comes after Nvidia's strong earnings report failed to prevent losses in the previous trading session. The article highlights the current market sentiment surrounding these technology companies.

title: HP CEO: AI PCs To Make Up A Tenth Of Shipments In Fiscal Year’s Second Half
summary: HP CEO Enrique Lores predicts that AI PCs will constitute 10% of the company's client computer shipments in the latter half of its fiscal year, driven initially by consumer sales. He anticipates a significant increase in AI PC demand over the next few years, forecasting that AI PCs will represent 40-50% of total PC shipments within three years. Despite a decline in printing revenue, HP's Personal Systems business saw growth in commercial PC sales.

title: SoFi, AMD, Robinhood, Salesforce, and Other Tech Stocks in Focus Today
summary: Tech stocks, including SoFi, AMD, Robinhood, and Salesforce, experienced a downturn early on Wednesday, signalling a loss of momentum in their recent rally. This dip is attributed to broader market weakness impacting the sector. The article highlights these specific companies as being in focus due to the market's response.

title: Stocks to Watch | Nvidia Stock: 4 Reasons to Buy, 4 Reasons to Sell
summary: This article provides an analysis of Nvidia's stock, outlining four reasons to buy and four reasons to sell. It discusses Nvidia's impressive Q1 fiscal 2025 earnings, its dominance in the AI market, and its valuation compared to competitors, while also addressing concerns about competition, regulatory challenges, and potential supply gluts. Ultimately, the author concludes that Nvidia's strengths still outweigh its weaknesses.

title: Marvell Expands Connectivity Portfolio With New PCIe Retimer Product Line to Scale Compute Fabrics of Accelerated Infrastructure
summary: Marvell Technology Inc. has launched its new Alaska P PCIe retimer product line, designed to scale data center compute fabrics in accelerated servers, CXL systems, and disaggregated infrastructure. These 8- and 16-lane PCIe Gen 6 retimers, built on Marvell's 5nm PAM4 technology, are crucial for connecting AI accelerators, GPUs, and CPUs as AI applications demand significantly higher bandwidth and faster data rates, necessitating reliable communication over greater distances. The new product line extends Marvell's PAM4 leadership beyond Ethernet and InfiniBand, addressing the growing market for PCIe and CXL connectivity within server systems with industry-leading low power consumption.

title: Computer Industry Joins NVIDIA to Build AI Factories and Data Centers for the Next Industrial Revolution
summary: NVIDIA, in collaboration with top computer manufacturers, unveiled a new array of NVIDIA Blackwell architecture-powered systems, featuring Grace CPUs and NVIDIA networking, designed for building AI factories and data centers. These systems aim to drive the next wave of generative AI breakthroughs, with offerings ranging from single to multi-GPUs and various cooling technologies. Key industry players like ASRock Rack, ASUS, GIGABYTE, and Supermicro are among those delivering these advanced AI systems.

title: AMD Extends AI and High-Performance Leadership in Data Center and PCs with New AMD Instinct, Ryzen and EPYC Processors at Computex 2024
summary: At Computex 2024, AMD unveiled an expanded roadmap for its Instinct accelerators, including the MI325X arriving in Q4 2024 and the MI350 series in 2025, demonstrating a commitment to annual leadership AI accelerator releases. They also introduced new Ryzen AI 300 Series processors for Copilot+ PCs and Ryzen 9000 Series desktop processors, emphasizing AI and high-performance computing across data centers and consumer devices. Additionally, AMD previewed 5th Gen EPYC server processors for 2H 2024 and showcased leadership in edge AI innovation with Versal AI Edge Series Gen 2, highlighting partnerships with Microsoft, HP, Lenovo, and Asus.

title: AMD announces new AI chips amid intensifying competition with Nvidia, Intel
summary: AMD announced new AI chips at the Computex tech conference in Taipei, emphasizing AI as its top priority and a transformative force in the computing market. This move intensifies its competition with rivals Nvidia and Intel, both of whom are also rapidly introducing new AI-focused processors. AMD plans to release new AI chip technology annually, outlining a roadmap for Instinct accelerators and EPYC server processors through 2026.

title: AMD debuts ‘world’s fastest’ consumer desktop processor and AI-optimized server chips
summary: AMD has unveiled its Ryzen 9 9950X, a new consumer desktop CPU that the company claims is the fastest in its category. Additionally, AMD introduced two AI-optimized laptop chips, the Ryzen AI 300 Series, and detailed three upcoming machine learning accelerators aimed at the data center AI market, including the Instinct MI325X, MI350, and MI400. The company also announced its Epyc Turin server CPU line, based on the new Zen 5 architecture, which promises significant AI performance upgrades.

title: AMD, TSMC to produce new chips
summary: AMD will collaborate with TSMC to produce its next-generation chips using 3-nanometer process technology, despite speculation about using Samsung's technology. AMD CEO Lisa Su emphasized the strong partnership with TSMC and Taiwan's importance to the semiconductor ecosystem. She also discussed an annual update cycle for AMD's AI chips, with the Instinct MI325X planned for release later this year.

title: Which AI Stocks Are Turning Hype Into Revenue?
summary: This article identifies AI stocks that have successfully converted hype into tangible revenue and growth during the first fiscal quarter. It highlights how companies like Nvidia, Microsoft, Amazon, Arista Networks, Broadcom, and Marvell Technology have seen increased fair value estimates due to strong demand for their AI-related products and services. While many AI-related valuations are considered high, the author suggests investment opportunities in Nvidia, certain auto industry stocks, and undervalued software and cybersecurity companies such as Adobe, Fortinet, and Palo Alto Networks.

title: ASML to Ship Its $380 Million Chip Machine to TSMC This Year
summary: ASML Holding NV is set to ship its latest $380 million high-NA extreme ultraviolet (EUV) chipmaking machine to Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) and Intel Corp. by the end of this year. This latest technology is crucial for advanced chip manufacturing. TSMC had previously expressed concerns about the machine's cost, while Intel has already received its first unit.

title: Nvidia’s Huang champions Supermicro’s direct liquid cooling tech
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang publicly endorsed Supermicro's direct liquid cooling (DLC) technology for AI data centers at Computex Taipei, alongside Supermicro CEO Charles Liang. Huang highlighted DLC as crucial for handling the exponential growth in data processing driven by generative AI, estimating a US$3 trillion investment in new data centers by 2030 will boost demand for Supermicro's solutions. Liang emphasized DLC's energy and cost-saving benefits over traditional air cooling, aiming for it to become a mainstream solution for AI data centers efficiency.

title: TSMC Shares Hit Record High as AI Demand Boosts Chip Industry
summary: Shares of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC), the world's largest contract chip maker, reached a new record high due to surging global demand for advanced AI chips. The company's stock rose 5.15% in Taipei, pushing its market capitalization to $717.58 billion and contributing to an all-time high for the benchmark Taiex Index. This surge also follows recent announcements of a new share buyback program and a strategic partnership.

title: Coherent Corp. Gets a New CEO
summary: Coherent Corp. announced James R. Anderson as its new CEO, replacing Vincent D. Mattera, Jr. Anderson's appointment, following his successful tenure at Lattice Semiconductor, caused Coherent's stock to jump 23% while Lattice's fell 15.5%. The move signals Coherent's focus on AI, datacom, and industrial markets, building on Mattera's legacy of significant growth and acquisitions.

title: NVIDIA and AMD Make Their Next Move in the AI Chip War
summary: NVIDIA and AMD recently unveiled their next-generation AI chips, with NVIDIA introducing its "Rubin" architecture and AMD launching its Ryzen AI 300 and Ryzen 9000 series. While both companies are making significant advancements in the AI chip market, the author argues that NVIDIA remains the stronger investment due to its superior fundamentals and market performance, despite AMD's efforts to increase its market share in AI-powered PCs. The article advises investors to look for fundamentally strong companies rather than falling for general AI hype.

title: Microcontroller operations in an OCP ORV3 smart battery backup unit
summary: This article details the main microcontroller's software operations in an Open Compute Project (OCP) Open Rack Version 3 (ORV3) smart battery backup unit (BBU), following up on a previous discussion of its electrical and mechanical design. It outlines six critical processes managed by the microcontroller, including housekeeping, charging, discharging, fault handling, and Modbus communication, ensuring the BBU's high efficiency and capacity in the new 48V architecture. The piece emphasizes the collaboration between hardware and software to deliver reliable system-level solutions.

title: Who are the highest-paid S&P 500 CEOs of 2023?
summary: The median total compensation for S&P 500 CEOs increased by 12.6% in 2023, reaching $16.3 million, largely due to stock awards. Broadcom Inc CEO Hock Tan was the highest-paid chief executive with over $161 million, significantly more than other top earners like Fair Isaac Corporation CEO William Lansing and Apple CEO Tim Cook. This surge in CEO pay outpaced the 5.2% increase for the median S&P 500 employee, further widening the CEO-to-employee pay ratio.

title: Five thoughts from Cisco Live 2024
summary: Cisco Live 2024 showcased the company's aggressive push into AI and the integration of Splunk across its portfolio. Key takeaways include Splunk's expansion beyond security and observability, Cisco's emerging comprehensive AI infrastructure story, significant advancements in its security offerings, and the growing importance of ThousandEyes for network visibility. The event highlighted Cisco's transition towards a cloud-centric strategy and the need for more cross-cloud solutions to create integrated customer experiences.

title: South Korean AI chip developers Rebellions and Sapeon to merge
summary: South Korean AI chip developers Rebellions Inc and Sapeon Korea Inc are merging to challenge global AI chip leader Nvidia. The consolidation, driven by Sapeon parent SK Telecom, aims to secure a strong position in the global AI semiconductor market within the next two to three years, with the merged company expected to launch by the end of the year. Rebellions will manage the merged entity, focusing on neural processing units (NPUs).

title: Archaea Energy and Republic Services celebrate first RNG plant in landmark partnership
summary: Archaea Energy, a bp subsidiary, and Republic Services have launched their first renewable natural gas (RNG) plant in Fort Wayne, Indiana, as part of their Lightning Renewables joint venture. This facility, utilizing Archaea Modular Design (AMD), is the first of approximately 40 planned landfill gas-to-RNG projects aiming to convert landfill gas into sustainable energy. Once operational, the Fort Wayne plant is expected to process enough gas to heat over 25,000 homes annually, significantly contributing to both companies' sustainability goals and increasing domestic energy production.

title: ‘Record demonstration’: Dark horse in quantum race passes new milestone
summary: Diraq, an Australian quantum startup, has achieved a significant milestone with 99.9% control accuracy for a quantum bit, a precision level needed for error-corrected quantum computing. The company is partnering with GlobalFoundries Inc and IMEC to manufacture its compact, silicon-based quantum computer chip. Diraq aims to produce affordable and compact quantum computers, positioning its technology as a competitive alternative to larger, more energy-intensive systems developed by rivals like PsiQuantum.

title: AMD Confident About Increasing Market Share In India
summary: AMD sees India as a promising market for significant growth due to increasing investments in data centers, AI, and technology upgrades. The company plans to expand its workforce in India by adding 3,000 more engineers over the next five years as part of a $400 million investment. AMD acknowledges the importance of talent development and collaboration with academia to meet the growing demands of India's semiconductor ecosystem.

title: This Stock Is About to Get an AI Boost. No One Is Paying Attention—Yet.
summary: The article highlights Corning (GLW) as an overlooked investment opportunity poised for an AI boost, despite its traditional glassmaking reputation. While other AI-related stocks like Nvidia, Arm Holdings, C3.ai, and Taiwan Semiconductor have seen significant rallies, Corning's potential in AI has yet to capture widespread investor attention. The author suggests it might be an opportune time to buy the stock.

title: Nvidia Becomes Most Valuable Public Company, Topping Microsoft (Published 2024)
summary: Nvidia has surpassed Microsoft and Apple to become the world's most valuable public company, achieving a market capitalization of $3.34 trillion. This rapid ascent, largely attributed to the boom in generative artificial intelligence and high demand for its GPUs, highlights the transformative impact of AI on leading corporations. CEO Jensen Huang's early bet on GPUs for AI systems has positioned Nvidia to control over 80 percent of the market for AI chips.

title: Nvidia surpasses Microsoft to become the world’s most valuable company
summary: Nvidia Corp. has surpassed Microsoft Corp. to become the world's most valuable publicly traded company, reaching a market capitalization of $3.34 trillion. This milestone follows a surge in Nvidia's stock, driven by its dominance in the AI chip market, particularly with its GPUs essential for generative AI development. The company's exceptional growth contrasts with Microsoft and Apple, which have also benefited from AI but not to the same extent, solidifying Nvidia's unique position in the tech landscape.

title: To address talent shortage, chipmakers skilling engineering students
summary: Semiconductor companies in India, including Intel India, AMD, Applied Materials, and Tessolve, are actively addressing the industry's talent shortage by partnering with engineering students and academic institutions. They are establishing labs, driving industry-academia collaborations for research in core design and AI training, and sponsoring student research. These initiatives aim to develop fresh talent, build a skilled workforce, and improve diversity within the rapidly growing Indian semiconductor sector, which is projected to more than double by 2026.

title: Former TSMC and UMC leadership unite to chart future semiconductor ecosystem
summary: The Semi Impact Forum 2024 in London brought together leaders from Taiwan's semiconductor industry, including former TSMC R&D Director Konrad Young and former UMC CEO Shih-Wei Sun, with UK investors and policymakers. The event focused on collaboration opportunities and insights into Taiwan's semiconductor ecosystem, emphasizing the importance of international partnerships, early-stage technology investment, and the future role of education and AI in the industry. Both sides discussed their respective strengths, with Taiwan excelling in manufacturing and the UK in innovation, aiming for mutual growth.

title: Here’s why AMD’s stock was just dubbed a top pick
summary: Piper Sandler has identified AMD as a top stock pick, citing its relative valuation and potential benefits from easing supply constraints on its AI offerings. Despite its positive outlook, AMD's stock performance has lagged behind other major chip companies and the broader market this year. The company's shares are down 14% over the past three months and up only 5% year-to-date, contrasting with the S&P 500's 15% rise and significant gains seen by competitors like Nvidia, Broadcom, and Micron Technology.

title: Here’s why AMD’s stock was just dubbed a top pick
summary: Piper Sandler has named AMD a top stock pick due to its relative valuation and potential benefits from easing supply constraints on its AI offerings. Despite a recent lag in stock performance compared to other chip companies, AMD is positioned for growth. The company's shares are down 14% over the past three months and up only 5% this year, underperforming the S&P 500's 15% gain.

title: How Nvidia surpassed Microsoft and Apple to become world’s most valuable company
summary: Nvidia has overtaken Microsoft and Apple to become the world's most valuable company, driven by its critical role in the booming AI market. This surge in market value, exceeding $3.35 trillion, is attributed to the immense demand for its H100 accelerators and its dominant position in providing chips for AI applications. The company's strategic pivot to AI, visionary leadership, and robust R&D investments have been key factors in its spectacular financial growth and market leadership.

title: AOSL is a new GB200 supplier, leading beneficiaries of NVIDIA's process certification changes
summary: Alpha and Omega Semiconductor Ltd (AOSL) is expected to become a new supplier for NVIDIA's GB200 AI servers, benefiting from NVIDIA's accelerated supplier certification process. This change allows suppliers with existing HGX/DGX H100 certifications to more easily qualify for GB200, leading AOSL to potentially gain market share from competitors by the end of 2024. Analyst Ming-Chi Kuo highlights this as a significant investment focus for the second half of the year.

title: What’s new in Azure app service at Build 2024: Key announcements and features
summary: Microsoft's Build 2024 event brought significant updates to Azure App Service, focusing on enhancing web development and AI capabilities. Key announcements include automatic scaling for web applications, sidecar support for easier app modification, and tighter integration with Visual Studio for streamlined development and deployment. Furthermore, Microsoft announced strategic partnerships with Nvidia and AMD to bolster AI performance with new GPU offerings, and introduced Cobalt-based VMs for optimized performance and energy efficiency.

title: Here Are 5 Things Smart Investors Should Know About Broadcom's Upcoming 10-for-1 Stock Split
summary: Broadcom recently announced a 10-for-1 stock split, joining other chip companies like Nvidia. This article explains how stock splits work, why Broadcom might be pursuing one given its high stock price and AI-driven growth, and how investors' holdings will be adjusted. It also discusses Broadcom's stock split history and offers a perspective on whether the stock is a buy right now, emphasizing that a split does not fundamentally change a company's valuation.

title: What Exactly Does NVIDIA Do, and Why Should You Care?
summary: This article provides an introduction to NVIDIA, highlighting its transformation from a GPU manufacturer to a systems company offering hardware and an ecosystem for developers. It delves into the factors driving NVIDIA's success, its significant impact on the customer experience market through AI, and key partnerships with companies like Cisco, SAP, and ServiceNow. The piece emphasizes NVIDIA's role in advancing AI for personalized and intelligent customer interactions across various sectors.

title: Intel Demonstrates First Fully Integrated Optical I/O Chiplet
summary: Intel Corporation's Integrated Photonics Solutions (IPS) Group has demonstrated the industry's first fully integrated bidirectional optical compute interconnect (OCI) chiplet, co-packaged with an Intel CPU and running live data. This OCI chiplet is designed to enhance AI infrastructure for data centers and high-performance computing by providing higher bandwidth, lower power consumption, and longer reach than traditional electrical or pluggable optical solutions. This breakthrough is expected to revolutionize high-speed data processing for AI, enabling greater scalability and more efficient resource utilization.

title: Microchip Rad-hard MCU Targeted at Aerospace and Defense Market
summary: Microchip Technology has launched the SAMD21RT, a radiation-tolerant Arm Cortex-M0+ based 32-bit microcontroller, for the aerospace and defense market. This MCU is designed for space-constrained applications, offering high performance in harsh environments with radiation tolerance up to 50krad TID and SEL immunity up to 78MeV.cm²/mg. Building on existing SAMD21 MCUs, it simplifies design for radiation-tolerant applications while supporting a wide temperature range and integrating essential analog and digital peripherals.

title: Spear Alpha (SPRX) 1Q24 Quarterly Letter
summary: The Spear Alpha ETF (SPRX) experienced an underperformance in 1Q24 compared to the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite, posting a 4.22% gain versus 10.56% and 9.31% respectively, primarily due to rising interest rates and investor shift towards defensive mega-caps. The fund is adjusting its strategy by increasing idea velocity and reallocating investments in the data center value chain, particularly in materials and power generation, while maintaining a balanced approach between software and hardware to capitalize on future opportunities in the evolving technology cycle amplified by AI. Despite short-term challenges, the management expresses confidence for the rest of the year driven by increased capex spending in hyperscalers and broadening data center investments.

title: Joe Costello sells Metrics, his latest EDA startup
summary: Joe Costello, former Cadence CEO, has sold his latest EDA startup, Metrics Design Automation, to Altair. Metrics, founded in 2017, developed a free desktop digital SystemVerilog and VHDL simulator, generating revenue from its cloud-based "simulation as a service" offering. Altair plans to integrate Metrics' DSim simulator with its Silicon Debug Tools to provide more affordable and accessible EDA design tools, enhancing design verification for complex chip designs.

title: Better Chip Stock: Arm Holdings vs. Intel
summary: This article compares Arm Holdings and Intel, two major chipmakers. Arm, dominating mobile CPU design, has seen significant stock growth due to its flexible approach and expansion into cloud and auto markets, despite a high valuation. Intel, a major PC and server CPU producer, has struggled with manufacturing delays and competition, leading to a stock decline, though analysts expect some recovery. The author concludes that Arm is the better buy due to its faster growth, wider market moat, and higher-margin business.

title: Nokia sees South Korea as pioneer country in the 6G field
summary: Nokia views South Korea as a key player in the commercialization of 6G wireless networks by 2029, with plans to collaborate with major mobile carriers and focus on extended reality (XR) for monetization. South Korea is actively investing in 6G research and development, aiming to establish international standards and showcase network advancements by 2026. The country's K-Network 2030 strategy emphasizes public-private cooperation to accelerate 6G technology development and strengthen the network supply chain.

title: Applied Materials reveals chip wiring innovations for energy-efficient computing
summary: Applied Materials has introduced new chip wiring innovations designed to enhance energy efficiency in computing, particularly for 2-nanometer node manufacturing and beyond. These advancements utilize new materials like Ruthenium Cobalt liners and enhanced low-k dielectrics, which reduce electrical resistance by up to 25% and capacitance by up to 3%, ultimately supporting the development of trillion-transistor chips for AI and graphics processing. The company's solutions aim to improve performance-per-watt, strengthen chips for 3D stacking, and address challenges in scaling Moore's Law, with adoption already seen by leading logic and memory chipmakers including Samsung and TSMC.

title: Corsair Unveils Stunning 3500X Case For Gaming PCs And It's Surprisingly Affordable
summary: Corsair has introduced its new 3500X mid-tower chassis, featuring wraparound tempered glass panels and robust form factor support for various motherboards, including those with reverse connectors. This case prioritizes cooling with strategically placed fan mounting points and accommodates large components like 410mm graphics cards and 360mm liquid cooling radiators. Available in three versions ranging from $89.99 to $149.99, the 3500X offers gamers an affordable and visually appealing option for their PC builds.

title: Resonac Announces New US-JOINT Consortium; Ten Partners to Collaborate on Next-Generation Semiconductor Packaging in Silicon Valley
summary: Resonac has launched US-JOINT, a new consortium of ten American and Japanese companies, to advance semiconductor back-end process R&D in Silicon Valley. The partners, including KLA and ULVAC, will collaborate at a new R&D center in Union City, California, aiming to accelerate the development of advanced packaging technologies for applications like AI and autonomous driving. This initiative will bring packaging R&D closer to major semiconductor device makers, fostering faster evaluation and development.

title: AMD to Acquire Silo AI to Expand Enterprise AI Solutions Globally
summary: AMD announced its intent to acquire Silo AI, Europe's largest private AI lab, in an all-cash deal valued at approximately $665 million. This acquisition is a strategic move to enhance AMD's end-to-end AI solutions, focusing on open standards and accelerating the development and deployment of AMD-powered AI models and software globally. Silo AI's expertise in tailored AI models, platforms, and open-source multilingual large language models will bolster AMD's AI strategy and address complex challenges in deploying AI at scale for enterprise customers.

title: AMD to Buy European AI Lab Silo in Race Against Nvidia
summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. has agreed to acquire Helsinki-based Silo AI for $665 million in cash to enhance its artificial intelligence capabilities. This acquisition aims to help AMD narrow the competitive gap with Nvidia Corp. Silo AI, Europe's largest private AI lab, will continue to be led by its co-founder and CEO Peter Sarlin, becoming part of AMD's Artificial Intelligence Group.

title: ASE launches new testing facility in San Jose on AI demand
summary: ASE Technology Holding Co (ASE) has opened a second testing facility in San Jose, California, through its subsidiary ISE Labs Inc, to meet the increasing demand for advanced chip testing in AI and high-performance computing. This expansion doubles ISE Labs’ R&D and business space, aiming to strengthen the US semiconductor supply chain. ASE plans to invest about US$200 million in the new facility and has increased its overall capital spending to cope with growing demand.

title: Should I Buy Intel's Shares After Their Big Sell-Off?
summary: Intel's stock has significantly underperformed its semiconductor peers despite its historical dominance, largely due to struggles in competing with AI-focused chipmakers like Nvidia and losing market share in its core PC business. While Intel faces high uncertainty and financial challenges, Morningstar analysts consider its stock fairly valued at $30 per share, with potential upside dependent on its successful execution of an aggressive advanced manufacturing roadmap. The company needs to overcome manufacturing delays and increasing competition to regain its competitive edge.

title: SMH ETF: Question & Answer
summary: This article discusses the investment opportunities in the semiconductor industry, driven by digital transformation and AI. It highlights the benefits of investing in the VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH) for broad exposure to top companies in the sector. title: Opinion: AMD’s AI event lacked a wow factor, but here’s one reason investors should be excited
summary: AMD's recent AI event, while lacking new customer announcements, gave investors a key reason for optimism: CEO Lisa Su's updated forecast for the AI accelerator chip market. Su now projects the market to reach over $500 billion by 2028, a significant increase from her previous forecast and representing an approximately 60% compounded annual growth rate from last year's $45 billion market. This revised outlook indicates strong anticipated growth in the AI sector.

title: AMD: We Maintain Fair Value Estimate as ‘Advancing AI’ Event Highlights Strong Road Map
summary: Morningstar maintains its fair value estimate of $145 per share for AMD, viewing its shares as modestly overvalued despite progress in AI. This stance follows AMD's "Advancing AI" event, which highlighted significant advancements across processors, software, and networking hardware, and an increased forecast for the AI accelerator industry to $500 billion in revenue by 2028. The company also reported gains in server processor market share against Intel, suggesting continued growth in this segment.

title: After AMD’s AI event, this analyst says Nvidia’s stock is the better play
summary: Despite Advanced Micro Devices Inc.'s stock underperforming Nvidia Corp.'s shares significantly this year, a Bernstein analyst suggests Nvidia remains the better investment. This assessment comes after AMD's Advancing AI event, where analysts were reportedly more impressed with AMD's CPU positioning than its new AI accelerator announcements. The analyst prefers to anticipate Nvidia's Blackwell launch over AMD's recent developments.

title: How HPE’s AI strategy applies to telecom
summary: HPE is focusing its AI strategy on the telecommunications sector, viewing it as a significant market given that a large percentage of AI workloads are based on customer premises. Martin Halstead, Senior Distinguished Technologist at HPE, outlines three key AI use cases for telcos: customer/resource-facing applications, network planning/dimensioning using digital twins, and enhancing network performance and user experience through machine learning and generative AI. HPE leverages its supercomputing platforms and ProLiant servers, offering specialized AI infrastructure and a full-stack solution, including GreenLake for AI consumption, to help telcos adopt AI despite their risk aversion.

title: Jabil Introduces New High-Performance, Purpose-Built AMD and Intel Servers Optimized for AI, FinTech, and Cloud Applications
summary: Jabil Inc. has expanded its server portfolio with the introduction of the J421E-S and J422-S servers, featuring AMD 5th Generation EPYC™ and Intel® Xeon® 6 processors. These new servers are designed for scalability and customization across various cloud data center applications, including AI, HPC, and FinTech. Jabil emphasizes its collaborative approach with customers to develop tailored solutions, including Precision Time Measurement optimized motherboards, and will showcase these products at the 2024 OCP Global Summit.

title: Intel and AMD Form x86 Ecosystem Advisory Group to Accelerate Innovation for Developers and Customers
summary: Intel and AMD have announced the formation of an x86 Ecosystem Advisory Group to drive innovation and simplify software development across the x86 architecture. This group, including major tech companies like Google, Microsoft, and Dell, aims to enhance compatibility and consistency for developers and customers. The initiative seeks to ensure the x86 architecture evolves to meet emerging needs, particularly in areas like AI workloads and custom chiplets.

title: Jabil Introduces J421E-S, J422-S Purpose-Built AMD, Intel Servers
summary: Jabil Inc. has expanded its server portfolio by introducing the J421E-S and J422-S servers, powered by AMD 5th Generation EPYC and Intel Xeon 6 processors, respectively. These new servers are designed for scalability across various cloud data center applications, including AI, HPC, and fintech, offering customization options for performance, data management, and security. Jabil is showcasing these server platforms at the 2024 OCP Global Summit and plans for production by the first half of 2025.

title: Advanced Micro Devices Stock Overdue for a Bounce
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock is poised for a rebound after pulling back from March highs. Trading near a historically bullish trendline and with its RSI close to 'oversold' territory, analysis suggests AMD could see significant gains of over 18% in the next month, potentially reaching levels not seen since July.

title: What to Expect From Advanced Micro Devices' Q3 2024 Earnings Report
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is set to release its Q3 2024 earnings, with analysts expecting a 34% increase in profit per share year-over-year. The company has consistently beaten Wall Street estimates, driven by strong sales of its AI chips and significant growth in its Data Center unit. Analysts maintain a "Strong Buy" rating for AMD, anticipating continued strong growth in EPS for fiscal 2024 and 2025.

title: Invesco’s Semiconductor ETF Rallies on Strong TSMC Earnings
