summary: Invesco’s PHLX Semiconductor ETF (SOXQ) rallied after Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSM) reported strong third-quarter results and raised its annual growth expectations. TSMC is a top holding in SOXQ, which also includes Nvidia, Broadcom, and AMD. The ETF has performed well year-to-date, attracting significant net flows as investors seek exposure to the semiconductor sector.

title: Invesco’s Semiconductor ETF Rallies on Strong TSMC Earnings
summary: Invesco's PHLX Semiconductor ETF (SOXQ) rallied by 2.5% following strong third-quarter results from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC), a top holding in the ETF. TSMC's positive sales outlook and raised annual growth expectations boosted semiconductor stocks. SOXQ has shown significant year-to-date and one-year gains, with substantial asset growth.

title: Analog Devices sees India as key R&D hub: Company's India head
summary: Analog Devices (ADI) considers India a pivotal hub for its research and development efforts, recognizing the country's engineering talent and burgeoning market opportunities. With its Bengaluru R&D center being one of the largest globally, ADI aims to significantly contribute to the semiconductor industry's growth, which is projected to double to over a trillion dollars in seven years. ADI has also signed an MoU with Tata Electronics, Tata Motors, and Tejas Networks to explore semiconductor manufacturing in India and integrate ADI's products into Tata applications like electric vehicles.

title: Should you buy Nvidia stock now? It may be a once-in-a-generation opportunity
summary: Bank of America analysts have reiterated a "buy" rating on Nvidia stock, raising their price target to $190 from $165, suggesting a 38% upside. They view Nvidia as a "generational opportunity" due to the expanding AI market, robust demand for its next-generation AI chips like Blackwell, and strong enterprise partnerships. Nvidia's software ecosystem and potential for over $200 billion in free cash flow over the next two years further support BofA's bullish outlook.

title: Amplify ETFs Announces the Amplify Bloomberg AI Value Chain ETF (NYSE: AIVC)
summary: Amplify ETFs has launched the Amplify Bloomberg AI Value Chain ETF (AIVC), providing equal-weighted global exposure to companies crucial for the AI ecosystem. The ETF invests in semiconductor, cloud/software, and hardware companies identified by the Bloomberg AI Value Chain Index. AIVC aims to capitalize on the projected growth of the global AI market, which is expected to reach $3.6 trillion by 2034.

title: Shaping Our Future with AI: DuPont Reveals Next-Generation Solutions at 2024 TPCA Show
summary: DuPont announced it will showcase advanced circuit materials and solutions at the 2024 TPCA Show in Taipei, focusing on AI applications. The company will participate in the IMPACT Conference session "Shaping Our Future with AI by Next-Gen Substrate and Packaging" alongside industry leaders. DuPont will highlight its latest developments in bump plating technologies for HBM and advanced packaging, and a novel seed layer method for glass substrates.

title: AIVC Stock Fund Price and Chart — AMEX:AIVC
summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the Amplify Bloomberg AI Value Chain ETF (AIVC), including its current price, performance metrics, key stats like AUM and expense ratio, and investment strategy. It covers the fund's historical restructuring, its focus on cloud computing and AI ecosystems, and technical analysis indicators. The article also features a comprehensive FAQ section addressing common questions about ETFs and AIVC's specifics.

title: BlackRock Launches ETFs Focused On A.I.
summary: BlackRock (BLK) is introducing two new exchange-traded funds (ETFs) to provide investors with exposure to artificial intelligence (A.I.), building on the strong demand seen for A.I.-focused stocks. These ETFs, the iShares AI Innovation and Tech Active ETF and iShares Technology Opportunities Active ETF, will invest in companies driving A.I. technologies, including semiconductors, software, and hardware. This move follows BlackRock's earlier success with ETFs tracking spot prices of cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin and Ethereum, demonstrating their responsiveness to investor interests.

title: Core Scientific shares target raised by Canaccord on data center power
summary: Canaccord Genuity has raised its price target for Core Scientific Inc. (NASDAQ: CORZ) to $17.00 from $16.00, maintaining a Buy rating. This adjustment follows CoreWeave's exercise of an option to secure an additional 118MW of data center power for AI hosting, bringing their total contracted power with Core Scientific to 500MW for critical load and 200MW for supportive functions. Core Scientific expects an 80% contribution-like margin from this expanded contract, with CoreWeave funding the $180 million in capital expenditures for infrastructure upgrades. The analyst also highlighted the potential for Core Scientific to further expand its power portfolio by repurposing brownfield assets for AI hosting.

title: BlackRock unveils new funds focused on AI growth
summary: BlackRock, the world's largest asset manager, has launched two new exchange-traded funds (ETFs) to provide investors with exposure to the rapidly growing market for AI. These funds, the iShares A.I. Innovation and Tech Active ETF and the iShares Technology Opportunities Active ETF, target global AI and technology stocks, including semiconductors, software, and hardware. BlackRock sees AI as a major force driving long-term investment opportunities and a key shaper of future industries.

title: AMD Tumbles After AI Chip Growth Fails to Impress Investors
summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. experienced its worst rout in two years after its revenue outlook for the fourth quarter fell short of Wall Street expectations, indicating slower-than-anticipated growth in its artificial intelligence sales. Despite an increased forecast for AI accelerator sales to over $5 billion this year, investors were looking for a more significant bump, leading to an 11% slide in the company's stock. AMD's gaming revenue also came in below analysts' estimates.

title: Opinion: AMD could still be a sleeper against Nvidia, but Wall Street isn’t yet convinced
summary: AMD executives made bold statements about their AI accelerator chip and provided an updated AI chip-revenue forecast of $5 billion, which merely met analysts' expectations. This leaves investors wondering if the forecast is a concern or if AMD could become a strong competitor against Nvidia despite Wall Street's current skepticism.

title: Advanced Micro Devices Stock Sinks as Q4 Guidance Disappoints. What to Know
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock fell after its Q4 guidance disappointed Wall Street, despite meeting Q3 expectations. Analysts are mostly bullish, with an average target price suggesting upside, but some like Oppenheimer maintain a Perform rating due to competition and valuation concerns.

title: AMD’s stock is getting hurt by this ‘unpalatable’ reality
summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) shares are underperforming compared to other AI stocks in 2024, despite boosting its full-year AI revenue forecast. The reason for the muted performance is AMD's overall revenue outlook for the fourth quarter, which fell short of analyst expectations. This "unpalatable" reality contrasts with the strong growth seen in other AI-focused companies, according to one analyst.

title: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. Just Beat Analyst Forecasts, And Analysts Have Been Updating Their Predictions
summary: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD) recently exceeded analyst forecasts with better-than-expected EPS, despite revenues largely meeting expectations. Following these results, analysts slightly lowered their earnings per share estimates for 2025 but maintained their consensus price target at US$187. AMD is still projected to grow significantly faster than the broader semiconductor industry.

title: Is Celestica Inc. (CLS) The Best Electronic Stock To Buy According to Hedge Funds?
summary: Celestica Inc. (NYSE:CLS) is identified as a strong electronic stock, ranking 5th among the top 8 electronic stocks favored by hedge funds due to its robust performance, particularly in AI data center connectivity products. The article highlights the overall growth trajectory of the electronics sector, driven by technological advancements, IoT expansion, and semiconductor innovation, despite recent slowdowns in chip demand. Celestica's 23.33% year-over-year revenue increase in Q2 2024 and its new SC6100 storage controller showcase its potential for continued growth, though the article also suggests exploring AI stocks for potentially higher, quicker returns.

title: Fujitsu and AMD to begin strategic partnership to develop more sustainable computing infrastructure intended to accelerate open-source AI initiatives
summary: Fujitsu and AMD have announced a strategic partnership to develop more sustainable computing infrastructure for AI and high-performance computing (HPC). This collaboration will focus on creating open-source and energy-efficient platforms, combining Fujitsu's FUJITSU-MONAKA processors with AMD Instinct accelerators and ROCm software. The aim is to accelerate open-source AI initiatives and provide sustainable hardware and software options globally.

title: Goldman Sachs Initiates Coverage on Penguin Solutions, Inc. (NASDAQ:PENG) with ‘Buy’ Rating, Citing AI Growth Potential
summary: Goldman Sachs has initiated coverage on Penguin Solutions, Inc. (NASDAQ:PENG) with a ‘Buy’ rating and a $21 per share price target, emphasizing the company's strong position in the growing enterprise AI compute market. The report highlights AI's significant economic impact, with PwC projecting a $15.7 trillion contribution to the global economy by 2030, and notes that the increasing demand for AI necessitates a massive expansion of data centers. Despite cyclical drawbacks in Penguin Solutions' LED and memory businesses, Goldman Sachs believes the potential growth from generative AI will more than offset these declines, projecting a 13% 5-year CAGR for revenue.

title: VMware Cloud Foundation Delivers a Private Cloud Platform to Accelerate Customers’ AI, Sovereignty and Security Outcomes
summary: Broadcom's VMware Cloud Foundation (VCF) is enhancing its private cloud platform with new Advanced Services, ecosystem partnerships, and modernization programs to accelerate AI adoption, national digital sovereignty, and cybersecurity. These advancements aim to provide customers with a simple, secure, and cost-effective private cloud solution that supports generative AI application development, cyber resilience, and local data requirements. Key announcements include expanded data services, enhanced cyber recovery and security features, and new programs to facilitate private cloud transformation and certification.

title: Sprinklr Appoints Rory Read as Chief Executive Officer
summary: Sprinklr (NYSE: CXM) has appointed Rory Read as its new President & Chief Executive Officer and a member of its Board of Directors, effective November 5, 2024. Read succeeds Ragy Thomas, who will transition to an Advisor to the CEO role while retaining his position as Founder and Chairman of the Board. Read brings extensive leadership experience from major technology companies like Vonage, Dell, AMD, IBM, and Lenovo, and is expected to lead Sprinklr through its next phase of growth with a focus on its AI-powered platform.

title: Rackspace Technology launches GPU as a Service offering
summary: Rackspace Technology has launched a new GPU as a Service (GPUaaS) offering, leveraging Nvidia H100 processors, as an expansion of its Rackspace Spot platform. This service, housed in a new SJC3 data center in Silicon Valley, provides on-demand access to high-performance GPUs for AI, machine learning, and data analytics without significant upfront hardware investment. The expansion also adds the SJC3 data center as the seventh global location for the Rackspace Spot platform, improving latency and scalability for West Coast customers.

title: Big Changes Ahead For Analog Design
summary: The analog design landscape is undergoing significant changes due to challenges like heterogeneous integration, rapid foundry PDK evolution, and the increasing complexity of advanced nodes. Traditional in-house tools are struggling to keep up, pushing designers towards commercial EDA solutions despite initial resistance. The shift necessitates a new approach to design, focusing more on early post-layout simulation and continuous verification, while also highlighting a growing shortage of experienced analog engineers and the difficulties in transferring crucial design knowledge.

title: Bit Digital acquires Canada's Enovum as part of move into colocation
summary: Bit Digital has acquired Canadian data center operator Enovum for CA$62.8 million (US$46m) as part of its strategy to move into colocation and high-performance computing (HPC) services. This acquisition enables Bit Digital to offer colocation and on-demand computing, complementing its existing GPU offerings, and includes a 4MW facility in Montreal with expansion potential up to 24MW. The move reflects a broader trend among crypto firms pivoting to AI and HPC services, with Bit Digital also securing a GPU hosting contract with cloud gaming company Boosteroid.

title: Can Xpeng Motors Eat Tesla’s Lunch in Humanoid Robots the Way BYD Did in EVs?
summary: This article explores whether Xpeng Motors can challenge Tesla in humanoid robots, similar to how BYD became a major competitor in EVs. It highlights Xpeng's recent AI day announcements, including its "most human-like" IRON humanoid and robotaxi models, and draws parallels with past accusations of Xpeng copying Tesla. The author concludes that Xpeng is a strong contender in physical AI, potentially giving Tesla a "run for its money."

title: InnoPhase IoT Collaborates With TDK InvenSense to Deliver Sustainable Sensor to Cloud IoT Solutions With Exceptional User Experience
summary: InnoPhase IoT and TDK InvenSense have partnered to launch an end-to-end sensor-to-cloud platform. This solution integrates InnoPhase IoT's Talaria TWO Wi-Fi SoC with TDK InvenSense’s ICU-30201 ultrasonic sensor, aiming for ultra-low power consumption and extended battery life in IoT devices. The collaboration targets applications like smart locks and video cameras for Smart Homes, Building Automation, and Industrial IoT, promising sustainable and efficient user experiences.

title: Micron Introduces World’s Fastest, Most Energy Efficient 60TB SSD
summary: Micron Technology announced qualification for its 6550 ION NVMe SSD, which is the world’s fastest 60TB data center SSD and the industry’s first E3.S and PCIe Gen5 60TB SSD. This new SSD sets a new standard for performance, energy efficiency, endurance, security, and rack density for exascale data center deployments, excelling in high-capacity NVMe workloads. It delivers up to 179% faster sequential reads and 213% higher write bandwidth per watt compared to competing drives, while reducing rack storage needs by up to 67%.

title: Cathie Wood's ARK ETF adjusts portfolio with major buys in CRISPR, Aurora
summary: Cathie Wood's ARK ETF made significant adjustments to its portfolio on November 13, 2024, showing strong confidence in gene-editing and autonomous vehicle technologies. The ETF invested heavily in CRISPR Therapeutics AG and Aurora Innovation Inc, while also increasing its stake in Amazon.com Inc. Concurrently, ARK divested shares from companies like Unity Software Inc, Adaptive Biotechnologies Corp, Exact Sciences Corp, and Rocket Lab USA Inc, indicating a strategic shift towards innovative tech sectors and away from certain biotech and software holdings.

title: Sunwoda EVB inks MoU with STMicroelectronics in automotive electrification, intelligence fields
summary: Sunwoda EVB, through its automotive electronics division Sungiant, has signed an MoU with STMicroelectronics for strategic cooperation in automotive electrification and intelligent solutions, targeting Chinese and European markets. The partnership will leverage STMicroelectronics' semiconductor lineup for advanced battery management systems (BMS) and other applications, including co-developing BMS, VDC systems, and ZCU for NEVs. They also plan to establish a joint laboratory to foster innovation in chip design, testing, and materials research.

title: Huge layoff by Nvidia rival includes dozens at Bay Area headquarters
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is laying off 4% of its global workforce, impacting at least 129 employees in California, including dozens at its Bay Area headquarters. The layoffs, confirmed by AMD, are part of a strategy to align resources with "largest growth opportunities," particularly in artificial intelligence, where AMD is trying to catch up to competitors like Nvidia.

title: Big chipmaker to slash 1,000 jobs; $100M Big Sur home for sale
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is set to reduce its global workforce by 1,000 jobs. This news comes as a $100M Big Sur home is up for sale, alongside other Bay Area developments including calls for increased CHP presence on BART and a look into Santa Clara County's tax appeals. The article highlights significant regional news, including a major tech company's layoffs and high-value real estate.

title: Should You Investigate Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (NASDAQ:AMD) At US$139?
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) has seen significant price movements, with its stock currently trading at US$139 after reaching US$173 and dropping to US$129. Simply Wall St's valuation suggests an intrinsic value of $213.12, indicating the stock is undervalued, despite its volatility. With expected profit growth doubling over the next few years, the future outlook for AMD appears bright, presenting a potential buying opportunity for investors.

title: AGNC Investment Stock: A Top 15% Yielder To Buy For 2025 (Rating Upgrade) (NASDAQ:AGNC)
summary: AGNC Investment is poised to benefit from recent Federal Reserve rate cuts, which are expected to reduce interest costs and enhance profitability. This could lead to an upward repricing of its book value and a higher valuation. Despite potential risks related to inflation or stronger economic growth affecting future rate decisions, AGNC's attractive 15% yield and low price-to-book ratio make it a promising investment for 2025.

title: Biden locks in $6.6B for TSMC chip factories, ensuring Trump can’t rescind CHIPS Act deal
summary: The Biden administration has finalized a $6.6 billion award with Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) for its chip factories in Phoenix, along with up to $5 billion in loans, largely to secure the deal against potential changes by President-elect Donald Trump. This investment, part of the CHIPS and Science Act, aims to boost domestic semiconductor manufacturing and create thousands of jobs in Arizona. Despite Trump's previous opposition to similar deals, the finalized contract is binding, ensuring the continuation of a project critical for U.S. economic and national security.

title: Applied Materials Announces New Collaboration Model for Advanced Packaging at Summit on Energy-Efficient Computing
summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) has announced an expansion of its global EPIC innovation platform to accelerate the commercialization of advanced chip packaging technologies, specifically targeting energy-efficient computing for AI. The company convened over two dozen semiconductor R&D leaders in Singapore to foster collaboration across the industry. This initiative, EPIC Advanced Packaging, aims to address challenges in developing next-generation packaging technologies by facilitating co-innovation and providing early access to new equipment and expertise.

title: Penguin Solutions OriginAI Infrastructure Now Available with Additional GPUs and Enhanced Cluster Management Capabilities
summary: Penguin Solutions has expanded its OriginAI infrastructure solution to include the latest NVIDIA and AMD GPUs, as well as Dell Technologies AI-optimized hardware. This enhanced offering provides organizations with more options for tailoring AI infrastructure to specific workloads, supported by Penguin's ClusterWare software and end-to-end services. The updates aim to help companies rapidly deploy AI at scale and achieve faster ROI and competitive benefits.

title: To understand Nvidia, take a look at its CEO and culture
summary: A new book, "The Nvidia Way," by Tae Kim, explores the critical role of CEO Jensen Huang and the company's unique work culture in its significant success, particularly in the AI industry. The excerpt highlights Huang's intense work ethic, his use of "fear and anxiety" as motivational tools, and the demanding environment that shaped Nvidia's early years, including a fierce competitive challenge from Intel. The article emphasizes that beyond technology, the distinct culture fostered by Huang has been a key driver of Nvidia's dominance.

title: Interim chief at Wolfspeed poised to make $375K in monthly compensation
summary: Thomas H. Werner has been appointed interim executive chairman of Wolfspeed following the termination of previous CEO Gregg Lowe. Werner will receive $375,000 in monthly compensation, consisting of a $150,000 base salary and $225,000 in restricted stock, while the Durham chipmaker searches for a permanent CEO. The company's shares have significantly declined over the past year amidst decreased demand and a complex strategic transition.

title: Wells Fargo Sticks to Their Buy Rating for CoreWeave (CRWV)
summary: Wells Fargo analyst Michael Turrin maintained a Buy rating for CoreWeave (CRWV) with a price target of $150.00, despite negative corporate insider sentiment surrounding the stock. CoreWeave reported quarterly revenue of $1.36 billion and a GAAP net loss of $110.12 million for the quarter ending September 30. Other analysts have varying ratings, including a Buy from Compass Point and a Hold from TR | OpenAI – 4o.

title: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Shifts to Unified UDNA Tech: AI Capabilities for Future Gaming GPUs
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is reportedly shifting its graphics architecture strategy to unify its RDNA and CDNA technologies into a new "UDNA" tech. This move aims to integrate AI capabilities into AMD's gaming GPUs sooner. The article highlights the growing influence of AI on market trends and AMD's position within this landscape.

title: GlobalFoundries fined $500K over export violations
summary: GlobalFoundries has been fined $500,000 by the Department of Commerce for violating federal export restrictions by shipping $17.1 million worth of semiconductor wafers to China-based SJ Semiconductor. SJ Semiconductor is on the U.S. entity list, and GlobalFoundries failed to obtain the necessary licenses due to a data entry error. The company voluntarily disclosed the transactions and cooperated with the investigation, leading to a reduced penalty.

title: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD): Opens $400M R&D Center in India, Builds Supercomputers for Weather Forecasting
summary: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD) has inaugurated a $400 million research and development center in India and is building supercomputers for weather forecasting in the country. The company's CEO, Lisa Su, affirmed this investment, highlighting India's importance to AMD. AMD is also noted as a significant player among AI stocks, with a large number of hedge fund holders.

title: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Faces Competition as Chinese Chips from Zhaoxin Close the Gap in AI Race
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is facing increasing competition in the AI chip market from Chinese manufacturers like Zhaoxin, despite US export restrictions. While Chinese chips are improving, they still lag behind American technology. The article notes that AMD ranks 6th on a list of AI stocks, but suggests other AI stocks may offer higher returns.

title: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and Apple to Lead with TSM’s Advanced 2nm AI Chips in 2025
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and Apple are expected to be the first customers for TSMC's advanced 2nm AI chips, with mass production scheduled to begin in 2025. This development highlights the increasing importance of advanced semiconductor technology in the AI sector and positions AMD as a key player among trending AI stocks. The article also touches on significant investments in AI startups and the overall boom in the AI market.

title: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Unveils Alveo V80 Accelerator to Boost AI and High-Performance Computing
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) has launched its Alveo V80 compute accelerator, designed to optimize memory-bound computing tasks for high-performance computing and AI applications. This new offering aims to address the growing demands of large dataset workloads by providing significant advancements in performance and scalability. Despite Amazon's internal AI integration, AMD is ranked 6th among AI stocks on Wall Street's radar by the article's compiled list.

title: What Wall Street Analysts Think of HPE's Stock Ahead of Earnings
summary: Ahead of its fiscal fourth-quarter earnings report, Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) is receiving softly bullish sentiment from Wall Street analysts. The majority of tracked analysts maintain a "hold" rating, though Bank of America has a "buy" rating and raised its price target to $26, citing optimism for the AI server market in 2025. HPE is expected to report increased revenue and net income for the quarter.

title: Bitcoin ETFs Showdown: BTCW Falls Short Against IBIT
summary: The WisdomTree Bitcoin Fund ETF (BTCW) is compared to the iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF (IBIT), highlighting BTCW's shortcomings despite its temporary 0% expense ratio. While BTCW tracks the same index, it suffers from illiquidity and higher bid/ask spreads, with declining trading volume suggesting a shift towards long-term holding. IBIT is deemed superior due to its higher liquidity, lower transaction costs, and additional features like options availability, appealing more to active traders.

title: Silvaco Joins SMART USA Institute to Propel Digital Twin Innovations Under CHIPS Manufacturing USA Program
summary: Silvaco Group, Inc. has announced its participation in the Semiconductor Manufacturing and Advanced Research with Twins (SMART) USA Institute, an initiative under the CHIPS Manufacturing USA program. This collaboration aims to advance semiconductor design, manufacturing, and packaging through digital twin technologies. Silvaco will contribute its TCAD and EDA software expertise, including its FTCO™ solution, to optimize semiconductor processes and accelerate the integration of digital twin capabilities into the U.S. semiconductor ecosystem.

title: REX Crypto Equity Premium Income ETF Holdings
summary: This article details the holdings of the REX Crypto Equity Premium Income ETF (CEPI), which was created on December 4, 2024, by REX Shares. The ETF has $78.72 million in assets under management with 28 holdings, focusing on crypto-related and digital payment solutions stocks, and aims to generate income by writing covered call options. The list includes top holdings such as Micron Technology, Applied Digital Corp, and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co Ltd ADR, along with various options contracts.

title: Broadcom Inc. Launches 3.5D eXtreme Dimension System in Package (XDSiP) Platform for Custom AI Accelerators
summary: Broadcom Inc. has launched its 3.5D eXtreme Dimension System in Package (XDSiP) technology, a new platform for creating custom AI accelerators (XPUs). This platform integrates over 6000 mm² of silicon and up to 12 high-bandwidth memory stacks, significantly enhancing performance, power efficiency, and integration capabilities for AI applications, including generative AI models. Broadcom, in collaboration with TSMC and Fujitsu, anticipates production shipments to begin in February 2026, marking a crucial advancement in high-performance computing.

title: Intel Shores Up Board With Microchip CEO, Former ASML Chief
summary: Intel (INTC) has appointed former ASML Holding NV CEO Eric Meurice and Microchip Technology Inc. head Steve Sanghi to its board of directors. This move aims to bring significant semiconductor industry experience and operational vigor to the embattled chipmaker. The appointments come as Intel seeks a new chief executive officer and attempts a significant comeback.

title: Is AMD Stock's 7.19X P/S Still Worth it? Buy, Sell or Hold?
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) shares are trading at a stretched valuation with a 7.19X P/S ratio, but strong demand for data center chips and strategic acquisitions are boosting its prospects. The company faces challenges from weakness in its Embedded segment and intense competition from NVIDIA, maintaining a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) despite optimistic earnings estimates.

title: Soluna Holdings (SLNH) Partners with SF Compute to Power AI with NVIDIA H100 GPUs
summary: Soluna Holdings (SLNH) has partnered with SF Compute to provide NVIDIA H100 GPUs for AI applications, starting with 80 units and potentially scaling to 120. This collaboration aims to leverage Soluna's AI cloud infrastructure for advanced computational capabilities, placing Soluna Holdings 14th on a list of AI news and ratings watched by Wall Street. The article also touches upon recent US government curbs on the chip industry in China, impacting advanced memory chips and tools critical for AI development.

title: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Challenges NVIDIA with Faster, Cheaper AI Chip Alternatives
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is emerging as a credible alternative to NVIDIA in the AI chip market, offering faster speeds at lower prices, according to industry analysts. This development comes amid new U.S. government curbs on chip exports to China, aiming to limit Chinese access to advanced AI technology. While AMD shows promise, the article suggests other AI stocks might offer higher returns in a shorter timeframe.

title: AI's growth is just getting started, BlackRock's thematic ETF head says
summary: BlackRock's U.S. head of thematic and active ETFs, Jay Jacobs, believes the AI boom is still in its early stages, highlighting infrastructure and cybersecurity as key areas for growth in 2025. He emphasizes that the theme extends beyond megacap tech to include various semiconductor, data center, and software companies. Jacobs also points out the need for physical infrastructure, including energy, materials, and real estate, to support AI's expansion.

title: The next wave of AI: Demand and adoption
summary: Barclays Research analysts predict that AI is entering a new era, moving beyond text-based chatbots to "Agent Era" systems capable of autonomous, end-to-end tasks by 2025-2026. This shift is expected to drive significant enterprise and consumer adoption, with consumer AI potentially reaching one billion daily active users by 2026. Despite immense capital investment requirements for computing resources like GPUs and ASICs, rapidly falling inference costs are projected to make AI systems profitable sooner than anticipated, with demand for compute power significantly exceeding current forecasts.

title: AMD Redefines Business Productivity with Third-Gen Ryzen™ AI PRO Processors: Powering the Future of Advanced AI Computing
summary: AMD has launched its third-generation Ryzen™ AI PRO 300 Series mobile processors, designed to enhance business productivity with advanced AI computing capabilities. These new processors feature the XDNA™ 2 architecture, delivering over 50 TOPS of AI processing power and are equipped with AMD PRO Technologies for enhanced security and IT management. The processors will power upcoming business laptops like the Lenovo ThinkPad T14s Gen 6 and the HP EliteBook X Next-Gen AI PC.

title: Nvidia slapped with a Chinese antitrust probe as Beijing collects ‘bargaining chips’ ahead of Trump’s return
summary: China's State Administration for Market Regulation (SAMR) has launched an antitrust investigation into Nvidia regarding its 2020 acquisition of Mellanox Technologies, alleging potential violations of monopoly regulations. This move is seen as Beijing's effort to gain leverage ahead of Donald Trump's potential return to the White House, which is expected to intensify U.S. tech export controls against China. Despite U.S. restrictions, China remains a significant market for Nvidia and other chipmakers, making the company vulnerable to pressures from both the U.S. and China.

title: Monolithic Power Systems: Big Growth At A Reasonable Price (NASDAQ:MPWR)
summary: This article advocates for a risk-neutral portfolio strategy comprising short-term U.S. Treasuries, gold, and low-risk dividend equities, suitable for wealth preservation. Monolithic Power Systems (MPWR) is presented as a growth opportunity with an estimated 15% CAGR over five years, driven by its robust fabless model and strong market presence, despite potential risks from its reliance on TSMC and Nvidia. A valuation model shows a 7% margin of safety and a December 2029 EV estimate of $54.34B for MPWR, positioning it as a viable addition to growth-oriented portfolios.

title: Vultr Collaborates with AMD, Broadcom and Juniper Networks to Pioneer New GPU Data Center Architecture
summary: Vultr has announced a strategic four-way collaboration with AMD, Broadcom, and Juniper Networks to create a new GPU data center architecture. This initiative includes expanding Vultr's Chicago data center with an AMD GPU supercompute cluster powered by ROCm™ open software. The collaboration aims to provide enterprises and AI innovators with high-performance, scalable, and secure cloud GPU infrastructure for global AI training and inference workloads

title: Vultr, AMD, Broadcom, Juniper Team for ‘AI Innovation’
summary: Vultr is collaborating with AMD, Broadcom, and Juniper Networks to create an open ecosystem for artificial intelligence, aiming to accelerate AI deployment for enterprises. This partnership brings together essential components for cloud-fueled AI, benefiting Vultr's MSPs and VARs by enabling GPU-accelerated workloads. The new alliance involves AMD supplying Instinct MI300X GPUs and ROCm software, Broadcom providing Ethernet network adapters, and Juniper contributing AI-optimized Ethernet switches, with Vultr expanding its Chicago data center to support this initiative.

title: 1 Spectacular Artificial Intelligence (AI) ETF to Buy With $40 During the S&P 500 Bull Market
summary: The article highlights the iShares Future AI and Tech ETF (ARTY) as a promising investment for exposure to the booming AI market, especially given the S&P 500's strong performance driven by technology stocks. It details the ETF's highly concentrated portfolio, featuring top AI companies like Nvidia and Palantir, and suggests it could be a valuable addition to a balanced investment portfolio despite its short track record.

title: Broadcom's AI Chip Leadership Puts Pressure on Nvidia and Marvell, Analysts Say
summary: Broadcom's stock surged after reporting strong Q4 adjusted EPS results and raising its dividend, driven by its leadership in AI custom Application-Specific Integrated Circuits (ASICs). Analysts upgraded Broadcom's price targets, highlighting its expanding AI opportunities and advanced 3nm silicon technology, which puts it ahead of competitors like Nvidia and Marvell. The company anticipates significant growth in its AI revenues, expecting to reach $30 billion by calendar 2027 based on strong demand from hyperscalers.

title: A decade ago, she took over a struggling chipmaker—now she's worth $1 billion: 'Everything takes time'
summary: Lisa Su took over Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) a decade ago when the company was struggling, with its stock at $3 per share and significant staff cuts. Under her leadership, AMD's market cap has grown to over $205 billion and her net worth has reached $1.3 billion. Su, named Time's CEO of the Year for 2024, attributes her success to a long-term strategy focused on high-quality products, customer trust, and simplified operations, emphasizing that "everything takes time."

title: First Trust Debuts Defense, Semiconductor ETFs
summary: First Trust has launched two new UCITS ETFs: the First Trust Indxx Global Aerospace & Defense UCITS ETF (MISL) and the First Trust Bloomberg Global Semiconductor Supply Chain UCITS ETF (CHPS). These ETFs are listed on the London Stock Exchange with TERs of 0.65% and 0.60% respectively, expanding First Trust's UCITS ETF range to 36. MISL focuses on companies deriving at least 50% of revenues from aerospace or defense sectors, while CHPS allocates 70% to semiconductor manufacturing and 30% to semiconductor device companies.

title: Channel Brief: Onapsis Plans to Double Revenue With New CRO
summary: Onapsis aims to double its revenue by 2026, driven by its new Chief Revenue Officer, Paul Kleinschnitz, focusing on product roadmap execution and partnerships. Other tech news includes Grip Security partnering with SailPoint, Tim Wintrip becoming CEO of Lemongrass, Vultr raising $333 million, and the U.S. considering a ban on TP-Link routers due to national security concerns. The article also touches on the Biden administration's overhaul of the H1-B visa program.

title: YieldMax Dorsey Wright Hybrid 5 Income ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:FIVY)
summary: This article provides detailed information on the YieldMax Dorsey Wright Hybrid 5 Income ETF (FIVY), including its current price, and key statistics like market capitalization, assets under management, and dividend yield. It outlines the ETF's investment strategy, which involves tracking an index of five stocks and five affiliated ETFs using synthetic covered call strategies. The article also lists recent dividend declarations and its top holdings, such as YieldMax options income ETFs for GOOGL, AMD, PLTR, MRNA, and RDDT.

title: Is It Worth Investing in Advanced Micro (AMD) Based on Wall Street's Bullish Views?
summary: This article examines the reliability of Wall Street analyst recommendations for Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), highlighting that while AMD has a strong "Buy" average brokerage recommendation (ABR), investors should be cautious due to potential analyst biases. It contrasts the ABR with the Zacks Rank, a proprietary stock rating tool, suggesting that the Zacks Rank offers a more objective and timely indicator for investment decisions based on earnings estimate revisions. The article concludes by recommending that investors use the Zacks Rank to validate their research, noting AMD's current Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold).

title: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Co-Leads $3.5 Billion Financing Round for Vultr to Boost AI and Cloud Infrastructure
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) co-led a $3.5 billion growth financing round for Vultr, a leading provider of cloud infrastructure and AI solutions. This investment aims to accelerate Vultr's global expansion and AI infrastructure capabilities, utilizing AMD Instinct accelerators. The move highlights AMD's strategic role in integrating AI into its semiconductor solutions and supporting high-performance computing for AI development and deployment.

title: Advanced Micro Devices Stock Pops on Bullish Coverage
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock surged by 4.6% after Rosenblatt initiated bullish coverage with a "buy" rating and a $250 price target, citing expanding market share in CPUs and GPUs. Despite the rally, AMD is still down 15.3% this year and faces resistance at its 20-day moving average and the $130 level. Options traders remain optimistic, with a high call/put volume ratio indicating strong investor confidence in the semiconductor giant.

title: 2025 Crypto Outlook: Eight Themes To Watch
summary: Alex Tapscott, managing director of the Digital Asset Group, shares his 2025 crypto and AI outlook. He highlights the "Trump trade" benefiting crypto, anticipating regulatory clarity and institutional adoption under a pro-crypto administration. The report also discusses Ethereum's overlooked strength in enterprise adoption and Nvidia's dominance in the booming AI chip market, predicting significant disruptions across various sectors due to AI.

title: Cipher Mining Stock: Capitalizing On Bitcoin, AI, And HPC Trends (Upgrade) (NASDAQ:CIFR)
summary: Cipher Mining Inc. (CIFR) is strategically expanding its Bitcoin mining and high-performance computing (HPC) infrastructure, aiming for 23.5 EH/s by 2025 by leveraging low-cost energy and operational efficiencies. The company's growth plan includes significant capacity increases in Texas with new facilities like Black Pearl and Stingray, which are expected to boost mining efficiency and computational power. Despite potential cash burn, CIFR's competitive production costs and high gross margins position it favorably to benefit from the current cryptocurrency bull market, leading to an upgraded "Buy" rating, though investors should prepare for high volatility.

title: Nvidia Supplier Ibiden Weighs Faster Expansion for AI Demand
summary: Ibiden Co., a key supplier of chip package substrates for Nvidia, is considering accelerating its production capacity expansion due to robust demand from the AI sector. CEO Koji Kawashima indicated that current expansion plans might not be sufficient to meet customer needs, prompting discussions for further investment. This expansion comes as Ibiden's shares rose and despite past reliance on Intel, the company is seeing increased demand driven by AI chips used by Nvidia and potentially other chipmakers.

title: Returns On Capital Signal Tricky Times Ahead For Intchains Group (NASDAQ:ICG)
summary: Intchains Group (NASDAQ:ICG) shows a declining Return on Capital Employed (ROCE), currently at 3.8% which is below the Semiconductor industry average. While the company's revenue and assets are growing, suggesting reinvestment for future growth, the ROCE has fallen significantly from 17% four years ago. The stock has also decreased by 25% over the last year, indicating potential opportunities for astute investors despite the challenging ROCE trend.

title: Jim Cramer Says ‘Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) Is A Buy, And I Think It Will Continue To Perform Well’
summary: Jim Cramer has endorsed Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) as a strong buy, citing its performance and strategic positioning in AI. The article highlights Amazon's role across the AI stack, its partnership with Anthropic, and discusses Cramer's investment tips for 2025 including avoiding "pigs get slaughtered" mentality, holding through tough markets, and staging buys.

title: IBM, GlobalFoundries settle legal dispute over chip technology
summary: IBM Corp. and GlobalFoundries Inc. have resolved a multi-year legal dispute concerning chip technology and manufacturing agreements. The settlement opens doors for potential future collaborations between the two companies. The dispute stemmed from IBM's accusation of breach of contract by GlobalFoundries and GlobalFoundries' counterclaim of trade secret sharing by IBM.

title: CES 2025: HP’s OMEN AI Casts a Spell on Gaming, Optimizing Performance with a Single Click
summary: HP announced new OMEN and HyperX gaming products at CES 2025, including the OMEN AI, the world's first AI-driven 1-click performance optimization tool. The OMEN MAX 16 is introduced as HP's most powerful gaming laptop, featuring NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs and enhanced thermal management. Additionally, HP unveiled the OMEN 32x Smart Gaming Monitor with built-in Google TV and the customizable HyperX Pulsefire Saga mice series.

title: NVIDIA Blackwell GeForce RTX 50 Series Opens New World of AI Computer Graphics
summary: NVIDIA has announced its new GeForce RTX 50 Series Desktop and Laptop GPUs, powered by the Blackwell architecture, which aim to revolutionize AI computer graphics. These GPUs offer significant performance increases, up to 2x over the previous generation, through advancements in neural shaders, DLSS 4 with Multi Frame Generation, and AI-driven rendering. The new series also introduces AI-powered tools for creators, autonomous game characters, and strengthens AI capabilities for PCs.

title: UniFirst Shares Skyrocket As Cintas Reveals $275 Per Share Acquisition Offer
summary: Cintas Corporation has made a public offer to acquire UniFirst Corporation for $275 per share in cash, valuing UniFirst at approximately $5.3 billion. This offer represents a significant premium to UniFirst's recent closing prices. Despite Cintas emphasizing the strategic fit and operational synergies, UniFirst's board has repeatedly declined to engage in discussions, prompting Cintas to publicize its proposal to UniFirst shareholders.

title: Absci and AMD Team Up to Revolutionize Drug Discovery with AI
summary: Absci and AMD have announced a pivotal collaboration to revolutionize drug discovery using AI. AMD is investing $20 million in Absci, leveraging AMD Instinct™ accelerators and ROCm™ software to power Absci's AI-driven drug discovery processes and aiming to accelerate the creation of biologics through high-performance computing. This partnership is expected to reduce infrastructure costs, speed up innovation cycles, and enhance the performance of Absci's proprietary models, such as IgDesign1.

title: Dell picks AMD for business PCs
summary: Dell Technologies will start using Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) chips for its business PCs for the first time, a move announced at CES in Las Vegas. This decision is seen as a blow to Intel's dominance in the PC chip market, especially in the more profitable commercial sector. AMD unveiled new Ryzen AI Max series processors and 9000 series desktop processors, claiming significant performance improvements for AI workloads, while competitors Intel and Qualcomm also showcased their AI-focused chips.

title: Delta (DAL), AMD, Walgreens (WBA) ou Constellation Energy (CEG): qual ação comprar e qual pular
summary: This article analyzes four major companies—Delta Air Lines (DAL), AMD, Walgreens (WBA), and Constellation Energy Corporation (CEG)—to guide investors on which stocks to buy and which to avoid. It recommends buying Delta and Constellation Energy due to strong financial performance and stable market positions, while advising to skip Walgreens and AMD due to ongoing restructuring challenges and competitive pressures, respectively. The analysis emphasizes considering financial health, growth potential, and individual risk tolerance before making investment decisions.

title: S&P Global Upgrades Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD) to ‘A’ Rating: AI Growth and Strong Market Share Propel Outlook
summary: S&P Global has upgraded Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) to an ‘A’ credit rating from ‘A-’, citing strong growth prospects driven by AI-accelerated computing chip sales and gains in the x86 product segment. The outlook remains stable, with projections for 13% revenue growth in 2024 and 24% in 2025, primarily from its data center segment. This upgrade reflects AMD's solid financial health and strategic positioning within the expanding AI market.

title: Illumina and NVIDIA collaborate to decode biology and propel precision health
summary: Illumina and NVIDIA are partnering to advance technology platforms for multiomic data analysis and interpretation. This collaboration aims to accelerate drug discovery and clinical development by combining Illumina's sequencing technologies and software with NVIDIA's AI tools, including foundation models and accelerated computing. The initial phase will focus on enabling Illumina's DRAGEN algorithms on NVIDIA GPUs and integrating NVIDIA's image processing tools into Illumina's Connected Software.

title: AMD: Trading at a 20% Discount, This Misunderstood Stock Is a Buy | Morningstar UK
summary: Morningstar believes Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is undervalued, trading at a 20% discount to its fair value estimate despite a challenging 2024. The company is well-positioned in the lucrative AI chip market, second only to Nvidia, and its strong data center business is expected to drive significant growth. Morningstar maintains a "narrow economic moat" rating for AMD, citing its chip design expertise and its unique x86 instruction set architecture license.

title: Supermicro Empowers AI-driven Capabilities for Enterprise, Retail, and Edge Server Solutions
summary: Supermicro is showcasing its latest AI server, storage, and edge computing solutions for the retail industry at the National Retail Federation (NRF) annual show, in collaboration with NVIDIA. These solutions aim to enhance retail operations, security, and efficiency by leveraging generative AI for personalized shopping, logistical automation, and shrinkage prevention. Supermicro highlights its broad portfolio of edge AI solutions and various server systems designed to deliver high-end performance at retail locations, demonstrating use cases like digital human customer service, AI shopping assistants, and video search for loss prevention.

title: Tech Investing in 2025: Emerging Trends and Market Opportunities
summary: In 2025, technology continues to fuel innovation and economic expansion, with AI, semiconductors, cloud computing, cybersecurity, and renewable energy offering significant investment opportunities. A diversified investment strategy is crucial to navigate this dynamic environment, as the global economy and supportive policies are expected to provide strong tailwinds for the tech sector. The article highlights key areas like semiconductors, nuclear energy, and cloud computing, suggesting thematic ETFs (like SMH and NUCL) as practical solutions for broad exposure while mitigating risk.

title: TSMC has begun chip production in Arizona, company confirms
summary: TSMC has confirmed it has begun producing advanced manufacturing chips in Phoenix, Arizona. This marks the first time 4-nanometer chips have been produced on American soil, a significant development for the U.S. semiconductor industry supported by the CHIPS Act. Despite potential political shifts, the company's grant under the Act has been finalized, and two more fabs are under construction.

title: Should You Steer Clear of Intel and Enphase Stocks in 2025?
summary: This article examines whether Intel (INTC) and Enphase Energy (ENPH) stocks, both of which have seen significant declines, represent a buying opportunity or should be avoided in 2025. Intel faces challenges from strong competition and slow AI adoption, while Enphase struggles with high interest rates and European market weakness. Both companies have strategies for recovery, but face considerable headwinds and have received cautious ratings from Wall Street analysts.

title: Governor Hochul Announces Corning to Invest More Than $315 Million and Create up to 300 Jobs at Semiconductor Glass Manufacturing Facility in North Country
summary: Governor Kathy Hochul announced that Corning Incorporated will invest up to $315 million in its Canton, St. Lawrence County facility, creating up to 130 new manufacturing jobs and 175 construction jobs. This investment will increase the production of High Purity Fused Silica and EXTREME Ultra Low Expansion glass, critical for advanced semiconductor manufacturing, and supports New York State's efforts to grow the semiconductor industry. Empire State Development is providing up to $7 million in Excelsior Jobs Program tax credits to support Corning's expansion.

title: Advanced Semiconductor Packaging Market to Generate US$ 40.3 Billion in Revenue by 2031, Driven by the Surge in Demand for Miniaturized Electronics | Latest Report by TMR
summary: The advanced semiconductor packaging market, valued at US$ 30.1 billion in 2022, is projected to reach US$ 40.3 billion by 2031, growing at a CAGR of 5.2%. This growth is fueled by increasing demand for compact, high-performance electronic devices, driven by innovations in packaging technologies like 3D and wafer-level packaging, and the rising adoption of 5G, IoT, and AI applications. Key drivers include miniaturization trends, 5G technology, IoT and edge computing, automotive electronics, and AI/high-performance computing, with major companies investing in R&D and strategic partnerships.

title: Tesla Price Target Bumped To $500 By Bank With 120x P/E Multiple Stemming From “Nebulous” Business
summary: Investment bank Piper Sandler has raised its price target for Tesla shares to $500, citing the company's potential in "real-world" AI and future opportunities like Optimus robots, despite a mixed performance in 2024. The bank acknowledges that immediate financial estimates for Tesla are highly uncertain due to the nascent nature of these business segments, leading them to use a 120x fiscal year 2026 P/E valuation.

title: SMART Modular Add-In Cards Now Listed on CXL Consortium Integrators' List
summary: SMART Modular Technologies announced its 4-DIMM and 8-DIMM CXL memory Add-in Cards (AICs) have successfully passed CXL 2.0 compliance testing and are now listed on the CXL Consortium's Integrators' List. This achievement highlights SMART Modular's dedication to industry standards and its role in advancing integrated memory technology to meet the growing demand for high-speed data processing. The company aims to provide cutting-edge solutions with enhanced performance and reliability.

title: The Best Tech Stocks to Buy
summary: This article identifies the 10 best tech stocks to buy now, according to Morningstar analysts, highlighting companies that are undervalued in a largely overvalued sector. These firms, including Sensata Technologies, STMicroelectronics, and Adobe, possess narrow or wide economic moats and are trading significantly below their fair value estimates. The analysis provides in-depth commentary on each company's competitive advantages, growth drivers, and market position.

title: Baya Systems raises $36M to transform AI chiplet design
summary: Baya Systems, a system-on-chip design startup, has secured $36 million in Series B funding to advance its software for the emerging "chiplet" economy. The company aims to address challenges in AI chiplet design by developing a "revolutionary" chiplet-optimized network-on-chip (NoC) interconnect solution. This technology creates a unified fabric for NoC-to-NoC communications, improving efficiency and flexibility for complex multi-die designs required by AI.

title: NYSEARCA:NVDY: High-Yield Income ETF or Missed NVIDIA Gains?
summary: The YieldMax NVDA Option Income Strategy ETF (NYSEARCA:NVDY) offers an attractive 84% dividend yield through a covered call strategy on NVIDIA stock. While it provides high monthly income, this approach caps upside potential, leading to significant underperformance compared to NVIDIA's direct stock appreciation. Investors must weigh NVDY's income generation against its limited total return, dividend volatility, and specific risks like single-stock dependence and tax inefficiency.

title: AI stock rout: The US selloff in 4 charts
summary: US stocks experienced a selloff, particularly in large-cap growth, following news that Chinese AI startup DeepSeek developed an advanced AI model that requires less sophisticated semiconductor chips. This development triggered concerns about the necessity of significant capital expenditures by large US tech companies in generative AI, leading to a 17% plunge in Nvidia stock and declines in other chipmakers and utility stocks. However, some tech giants like Apple and Meta Platforms saw gains, and Morningstar analysts maintain their fair value estimates for Microsoft, Amazon, and Alphabet, expecting long-term benefits from reduced AI model pricing.

title: Mawson Infrastructure Group signs 20MW lease with AI customer
summary: Mawson Infrastructure Group has secured a 20MW lease with an undisclosed AI customer for deploying Nvidia GPUs, with a Letter of Intent for a potential expansion to 144MW. The company is also planning to increase its total capacity to 153MW through an expansion in Ohio. This move signifies Mawson's strategic shift towards AI and high-performance computing markets, building on its growing digital colocation business.

title: Chipotle (CMG) To Report Earnings Tomorrow: Here Is What To Expect
summary: Chipotle (CMG) is expected to report its earnings tomorrow, with analysts projecting a 13.3% year-on-year revenue growth to $2.85 billion and adjusted earnings of $0.25 per share. The company previously missed revenue expectations by 0.8% last quarter, despite a narrow beat on EBITDA. Sentiment among restaurant sector investors is positive, though Chipotle's stock has seen a slight decline recently.

title: Intel Announces Another 58 Layoffs For Folsom Campus
summary: Intel has announced an additional 58 layoffs at its Folsom campus, continuing its cost-cutting measures due to stagnant sales and increased competition. This new round of job cuts, expected by March 31, follows previous significant reductions, with the Folsom workforce decreasing from over 6,000 in 2018 to under 4,000. The company is also planning to sell its 150-acre Folsom campus in a sale-leaseback deal as part of its broader restructuring efforts and aims to cut approximately 15,000 jobs globally.

title: AMD shares drop more than 6%, close at lowest since 2023 on disappointing data center revenue
summary: AMD's shares dropped over 6% after the company's data center business underperformed Wall Street's revenue estimates, despite overall better-than-expected top and bottom-line results. The chipmaker reported data center sales of $3.86 billion, falling short of the $4.14 billion analysts anticipated. Following this, several Wall Street firms, including Citi and JPMorgan, trimmed their price targets on AMD, citing concerns about competition from Nvidia and potential first-half weakness.

title: VEA vs VEU: Which International Vanguard ETF Is the Better Buy Today?
summary: This article compares two international Vanguard ETFs, VEA (Vanguard FTSE Developed Markets ETF) and VEU (Vanguard FTSE All-World ex-US ETF), to help investors decide which is a better buy. It details their holdings, sector weightings, and financial metrics, highlighting key differences such as VEU's larger allocation to emerging markets and significant positions in Taiwan Semiconductor and Tencent Holdings, which could offer a growth advantage over VEA.

title: Building tiny chips that can handle enormous data
summary: This article explores the advancements in computing, focusing on quantum computing and nanotechnology, to handle the vast data demands of AI. It highlights IBM's research into developing powerful yet energy-efficient chips that move beyond traditional binary systems, aiming for significantly increased processing power and efficiency in the near future. The piece also corrects some initial misinterpretations of IBM's complex technology, clarifying the distinct but potentially integrated roles of quantum computing and AI.

title: Amkor Technology Reports Financial Results for the Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024
summary: Amkor Technology, Inc. announced its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2024. For the fourth quarter, net sales were $1.63 billion and net income was $106 million, while full-year net sales reached $6.32 billion with a net income of $354 million. The company highlighted record revenue in its computing end market despite weakness in automotive, industrial, and communications sectors, and provided guidance for the first quarter of 2025.

title: Digihost to Develop High-Performance Computing and AI-Tier
summary: Digihost Technology Inc. announced the formation of US Data Centers, Inc., a new subsidiary dedicated to developing high-performance computing (HPC) and artificial intelligence (AI)-focused data centers. Their first project involves transforming an existing site in Columbiana, Alabama, into a 55 MW Tier 3 data center with an estimated total capital expenditure of $440 million, planned for completion by Q1 2027. This strategic investment aims to meet the escalating demand for AI-driven computing power and cloud services.

title: GlobalFoundries Reports Fourth Quarter 2024 and Fiscal Year 2024 Financial Results
summary: GlobalFoundries (GF) announced its preliminary financial results for Q4 2024 and the full fiscal year 2024, exceeding guidance for the quarter despite industry challenges. The company reported over $1 billion in Non-IFRS adjusted free cash flow for 2024 and highlighted strong design win momentum for 2025. GF also detailed a significant impairment charge related to legacy assets in Malta, New York, and shared recent business milestones including new partnerships and advanced packaging developments.

title: New ETFs From Direxion Focus on Daily AMD Results
summary: Direxion has launched two new single-stock ETFs focusing on Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), offering distinct leveraged and inverse strategies. These actively managed funds, with an expense ratio of 97 basis points, aim to capture investor interest in AMD's daily performance, especially given the company's role in the AI race. They are designed for traders and investors who understand the high-risk, high-reward nature of daily leveraged and inverse products.

title: Direxion Launches 2 Leveraged AMD ETFs Amid AI Chip Race
summary: Direxion has launched two new leveraged ETFs, Direxion Daily AMD Bull 2X Shares (AMUU) and Direxion Daily AMD Bear 1X Shares (AMDD), offering traders concentrated exposure to Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock. This move comes as AMD expands its presence in the artificial intelligence and data center markets, aiming to challenge Nvidia's dominance. The ETFs are designed for short-term trading due to their high-risk nature.

title: Report: Arm to build its own chips, and Meta is first in line to buy them
summary: Arm Holdings Plc. reportedly plans to design and build its own server CPUs, with Meta Platforms Inc. lined up as a first customer, marking a significant shift from its traditional licensing model. This move, driven by the lucrative AI chip market, could put Arm in direct competition with its existing licensees, potentially alienating customers but offering substantial new revenue streams. Analysts highlight both the immense opportunity in AI and the risks of disrupting established customer relationships.

title: After Earnings, Is Advanced Micro Devices Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) reported strong fourth-quarter results with growth in client and data center revenue, though its AI GPU growth forecast was disappointing compared to competitor Nvidia. Morningstar has cut its fair value estimate for AMD to $140 per share from $160 due to less optimism about AI GPU growth, but still considers the stock undervalued. The company holds a narrow economic moat based on intangible assets in chip design and expects significant long-term growth, particularly in the data center segment for AI applications.

title: GTA VI PC Might Launch Early Next Year, According to Corsair
summary: Corsair Gaming CEO Andrew Paul hinted that the PC version of Grand Theft Auto VI (GTA VI) might launch in early 2026, much sooner than PC fans typically expect for Rockstar titles following console releases. While not official confirmation, this suggests a potentially shorter waiting period—around six months—compared to previous Grand Theft Auto games. Console versions of GTA VI are still expected in Fall 2025.

title: Direxion每日一倍放空AMD ETF-AMDD-ETF報酬分析
summary: This article analyzes the performance of the Direxion Daily 1X Short AMD ETF (AMDD), providing detailed return data across various timeframes, including daily, weekly, monthly, and yearly performance. It also lists key metrics such as Alpha, Beta, and R-squared, although these are currently unavailable for this ETF. The data presented aims to help investors assess the past performance and risk characteristics of AMDD.

title: Micron Redefines Performance for AI PCs, Gamers and Professionals
summary: Micron Technology announced the new Micron 4600 PCIe Gen5 NVMe SSD, designed to significantly boost performance for AI PCs, gamers, creators, and professionals. Leveraging Micron G9 TLC NAND, this SSD delivers sequential read speeds of 14.5 GB/s and write speeds of 12.0 GB/s, effectively halving AI model loading times compared to Gen4 SSDs and offering improved energy efficiency. The 4600 SSD is compatible with leading processors from AMD and Intel and integrates advanced security features.

title: Micron Stock Soars 26% YTD: Should You Buy More or Book Profits?
summary: Micron Technology's stock has surged 26.5% year-to-date in 2025, driven by strong AI demand for high-bandwidth memory (HBM) and strategic partnerships, including with NVIDIA. However, the article suggests short-term risks such as margin contraction, weak consumer demand in PC and smartphone markets, and geopolitical tensions with China, making it potentially prudent to book profits. Despite long-term AI growth potential, near-term challenges and profitability concerns are highlighted.

title: Arteris Revolutionizes Semiconductor Design with FlexGen – Smart Network-on-Chip IP Delivering Unprecedented Productivity Improvements and Quality of Results
summary: Arteris has introduced FlexGen, a smart network-on-chip (NoC) interconnect IP designed to significantly accelerate chip development and optimize performance efficiency. This technology offers up to a 10x productivity boost, a 30% reduction in wire length, and improved latency, addressing the growing demand for faster, more sustainable innovation across various industries. FlexGen's AI-driven automation reduces manual adjustments by over 90%, enabling rapid generation of optimized NoC topologies and maintaining high quality for complex semiconductor designs.

title: Complex AI chips will drive demand for Lam Research chipmaking tools, CEO says
summary: Lam Research CEO Tim Archer predicts that growing demand for complex AI silicon will significantly increase orders from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) for their chipmaking tools over the next three years. The company projects revenue between US$25 billion and US$28 billion in 2028, up from US$16.2 billion in 2024, and announced two new groundbreaking tools: one that uses molybdenum instead of tungsten for material deposition and another for more precise etching. These developments underscore the semiconductor industry's reliance on companies like Lam Research for advanced manufacturing capabilities.

title: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD): Among the Best Long-Term Stocks to Buy According to Ken Fisher
summary: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD) is listed among Ken Fisher's best long-term stocks to buy, as identified from Fisher Asset Management's 13F portfolio. Fisher, a renowned investment analyst and founder of Fisher Investments, maintains a significant stake in AMD. Despite AMD's promising outlook and strong 2024 financial performance, the article suggests other stocks might offer higher returns in a shorter timeframe.

title: Should You Pick Unity Stock At $28?
summary: Unity Software (NYSE: U) released Q4 results showing a loss of $0.30 per share on $457 million in sales, beating analyst estimates. Despite a 25% year-over-year revenue decline, driven by drops in its Create Solutions and Grow Solutions segments, Unity stock jumped 30% to $28 after the company announced its new AI-powered advertising network, Vector. The article suggests that while the stock appears fully priced at 6x price-to-sales and faces near-term headwinds, the new AI platform offers future growth potential.

title: Apple’s $500B US expansion includes chips from TSMC Phoenix
summary: Apple pledged $500 billion for US expansion, including sourcing chips from TSMC's Phoenix factory, where mass production began recently. This investment is set to create 20,000 new US jobs and expand manufacturing and data centers across several states. Former President Trump and the Biden administration both claim credit for stimulating domestic chip production, with Trump citing tariffs and Biden highlighting the CHIPS and Science Act.

title: Micron starts shipping 1γ DRAMs ...
summary: Micron Technology has announced it has begun shipping 1γ (1-gamma) 16Gbit DDR5 DRAMs to partners and customers, claiming to be the first to reach this sixth generation of DRAM process technology. This new memory offers up to a 15 percent speed increase and more than 20 percent power reduction compared to its predecessor, with a 30 percent increase in bits-per-wafer, though it requires EUV lithography. Micron plans to integrate 1γ across its DRAM range for various applications, including data centers, AI-PCs, mobile products, and automotive.

title: Nvidia to report earnings amid infrastructure spending, DeepSeek concerns
summary: Nvidia is set to release its fourth-quarter earnings, capping off a remarkable year of growth driven by demand for its data center GPUs essential for AI. Despite its success, investors are cautious due to concerns about major customers potentially tightening capital expenditure and the emergence of more efficient AI models from startups like DeepSeek, which could reduce the need for extensive GPU infrastructure. CEO Jensen Huang is expected to address these concerns, emphasizing the continued need for GPUs in AI development and deployment, particularly for the inference phase.

title: Navitas Semiconductor Full Year 2024 Earnings: Misses Expectations
summary: Navitas Semiconductor (NASDAQ:NVTS) reported its Full Year 2024 earnings, showing a revenue of US$83.3m, an increase of 4.8% from FY 2023, and a net loss of US$84.6m, a 42% reduction from the previous year. Both revenue and earnings per share missed analyst estimates by 1.3% and 17% respectively. The company's shares dropped 28% in the past week, and analysts forecast a 21% annual revenue growth for the next three years.

title: Top Distribution Execs Are Grappling With Financial Impact Of Tariffs
summary: Top executives from leading technology distributors like Ingram Micro, Arrow Electronics, TD Synnex, and D&H Distributing are preparing for the financial impact of new tariffs imposed by President Donald Trump. While they anticipate increased costs will be passed down to end-users, questions remain about price elasticity and potential delays in purchases. Distributors are leveraging past experiences, adjusting supply chains, and focusing on transparent communication with partners to navigate the evolving situation.

title: Focus on Execution as Synopsys Reports Solid 1Q FY 2025 Financial Results
summary: Synopsys reported solid 1Q FY 2025 financial results, with revenue of $1.455 billion andGAAP EPS of $1.89, demonstrating strong performance driven by design activity and its قیادت in various technology sectors. The company's focus on hardware-assisted verification, EDA software, and AI innovations, particularly for 2nm projects, positions it for continued growth. Synopsys expects 2Q FY 2025 revenue between $1.585 billion and $1.615 billion, anticipating a 10.0% year-over-year growth.

title: Sanan-STMicro JV Finishes Building USD3 Billion SiC Wafer Factory in Chongqing
summary: A joint venture between Chinese semiconductor firm Sanan Optoelectronics and Swiss company STMicroelectronics has completed construction of a CNY23 billion (USD3.2 billion) silicon carbide (SiC) wafer factory in Chongqing. The factory, China's first of its kind, is set to begin mass production of 8-inch automotive-grade SiC electronic control chips in Q4, with an expected annual output of 480,000 units. This initiative aims to localize SiC chip production for new energy vehicles in China, anticipating a significant reduction in costs and market growth to USD6.1 billion by 2030.

title: Is Advanced Micro Devices Stock Underperforming the Nasdaq?
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock has significantly underperformed the Nasdaq Composite, falling 26.7% over the past three months and 43.4% over 52 weeks. This underperformance is attributed to competition, particularly from Chinese AI models and NVIDIA, and concerns about its AI market share. Despite beating Q4 revenue and EPS estimates, analysts are moderately bullish with a "Moderate Buy" rating, projecting a 47.8% potential upside.

title: BioXcel Therapeutics Soars: Analyzing Sustained Gains
summary: BioXcel Therapeutics saw its stock soar by over 108% following its regaining compliance with Nasdaq's minimum closing bid price rule. This compliance has significantly boosted investor confidence, despite the company's financial results showing substantial operating losses and negative equity. While the market response is optimistic, the article highlights the importance of strategic improvements and operational efficiency to translate this momentum into sustainable financial viability.

title: Microchip Technology to cut 2,000 chip jobs, will close Arizona fab earlier than planned
summary: Microchip Technology is laying off around 2,000 employees and closing its Arizona chip fab early as part of a cost-cutting and restructuring effort. The company expects to incur $30 million to $40 million in restructuring costs but anticipates annual savings of $90 million to $100 million. This move follows five successive quarters of revenue decline and a pause in its CHIPS Act funding application.

title: Malaysia-Arm Deal To Drive Semiconductor Growth
summary: Malaysia's partnership with United Kingdom-based Arm Holdings Plc (ARM) involves a US$250 million investment to develop proprietary intellectual property (IP) for integrated circuit (IC) design, aiming to elevate Malaysia into a high-value economy and foster local semiconductor firm growth. This collaboration, believed to be the world's first between a nation and a technology giant for IP development, is expected to accelerate the formation of front-end semiconductor firms and help achieve a semiconductor export target of RM1.2 trillion by 2030. Industry analysts have offered mixed outlooks, with some maintaining 'neutral' stances due to current market conditions but acknowledging long-term positive spillover effects for the technology sector.

title: Chipmakers are finding that good earnings aren't enough to please Wall Street
summary: Despite the ongoing AI boom, chipmakers like Marvell Technology, Nvidia, and Advanced Micro Devices are struggling to impress Wall Street, as investor expectations have reached historically high levels. Even with strong earnings and revenue, many stocks have plunged because guidance or performance fell short of optimistic projections. This trend highlights the immense pressure on semiconductor companies amidst the AI buildout and increasing scrutiny from investors.

title: Synopsys Introduces Virtualizer Native Execution on Arm Hardware to Accelerate Software-defined Product Development
summary: Synopsys has launched Virtualizer Native Execution on Arm-based hardware to speed up software development for edge devices across automotive, high-performance computing, and IoT sectors. This innovation enables accelerated virtual prototype execution by leveraging common instruction set architectures between Arm servers and edge devices, integrating into CI/CD pipelines, and supporting hybrid prototyping for comprehensive system-level validation. The solution promises faster validation, earlier software development, and improved performance analysis for software-defined products.

title: embedded world 2025 Best-in-Show: Winners
summary: This article announces the winners of the embedded world 2025 Best-in-Show awards, recognizing excellence in design, performance, and market impact across various technology categories. Awarded products span AI & Machine Learning, Analog, Power & Related Components, Computer Boards, Systems, Components & Peripherals, Dev Tools, Software & OS, IoT & Connectivity, Memory & Storage, MEMS & Sensors, Microcontrollers, Microprocessors & IP, Security, and Wireless. The judging process involved a 15-point rubric assessed by the ECD Content Team.

title: AMD FSR 3 Frame Generation Slow Level Loading Fix _Marvel Rivals - The Super Hero Team-based PVP Shooter
summary: Marvel Rivals has identified an issue where AMD FSR 3 Frame Generation causes slow level loading times on devices with fewer CPU threads. A fix for this problem is scheduled to be deployed in an upcoming update on March 13. The company reassures players of their commitment to an epic gaming experience and provides support contact information.

title: SanDisk to Hike NAND Prices by Over 10% as US Memory Giant Expects Supply to Tighten
summary: SanDisk, a leading memory chip maker, plans to increase its NAND flash memory prices by over 10% from April 1st in anticipation of a supply shortage. This decision follows production cuts by major OEMs due to a previous surplus, and while it signals a potential market recovery, its acceptance by clients and the impact of weak demand in consumer electronics remain uncertain. The long-term outlook depends on the recovery of PC and smartphone demand and the adoption of AI features in gadgets.

title: Intel appoints ex-Cadence CEO amid turnaround struggles
summary: Intel has appointed Lip-Bu Tan, former CEO of Cadence Design Systems, as its new CEO to lead the company's turnaround efforts, succeeding interim co-CEOs David Zinsner and Michelle Johnston Holthaus. Tan is tasked with revitalizing the struggling chipmaker and transforming it into a contract manufacturer, leveraging his extensive experience in the semiconductor industry. Analysts believe this appointment signals Intel's commitment to keeping its design and foundry businesses integrated, though challenges remain given Intel's recent struggles in market share and emerging tech trends.

title: Newegg Launches the World’s Most Advanced Gaming CPU: The AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D
summary: Newegg has launched the AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D and AMD Ryzen 9 9900X3D processors, which are now available for purchase. These CPUs are touted as the world's most advanced gaming desktop processors, featuring high core/thread counts, significant cache, and AMD's Zen 5 architecture. Newegg also offers compatible AM5 motherboards, pre-built gaming PCs with these processors, and an expanded CPU Trade-In Program to facilitate upgrades.

title: Analysts Offer Insights on Technology Companies: Intel (INTC), Adobe (ADBE) and SentinelOne (S)
summary: Analysts have provided new ratings for technology companies Intel (INTC), Adobe (ADBE), and SentinelOne (S). Intel received a "Hold" rating from TD Cowen and an upgrade to "Hold" from Bank of America Securities, with price targets indicating a modest upside. Adobe and SentinelOne both received "Buy" ratings from analysts at BMO Capital and Loop Capital Markets, respectively, with their price targets suggesting significant upside potential for investors.

title: Dell’s new RTX Pro AI PC boasts an ‘unlimited turbo’ mode
summary: Dell has announced its new "Pro Max" lineup of workstation desktops and laptops, featuring Nvidia's RTX Pro Blackwell GPUs and an innovative "unlimited turbo" mode for sustained high performance in its Tower T2 desktop. This series targets AI developers and power users, offering advanced cooling, large displays, and significant memory and storage capacities, including options with tandem OLED screens and modular USB-C ports. The range will include both Intel Core Ultra Series 2 and AMD Threadripper CPUs, with some models shipping in March and others in July.
