title: Outlook 2025: GenAI to Push Semiconductor Industry Towards a New Growth Phase
summary: Generative AI is expected to be the main growth driver for the semiconductor industry in 2025 by increasing demand for advanced computational power. This will lead to new market opportunities in AI-enabled devices across various sectors and drive in-house chip design and specialized, energy-efficient AI hardware for edge deployments. The industry faces challenges in energy consumption and model compatibility, requiring collaboration and standardized frameworks.

title: How Will FactSet Stock React To Its Upcoming Earnings?
summary: FactSet Research Systems (NYSE:FDS) is scheduled to report earnings, with analysts predicting a slight decline in EPS to $4.18 and a 4.5% revenue increase to $571 million. The article analyzes historical post-earnings stock reactions, showing positive one-day returns 50% of the time over the past five years, with a median positive return of 3.8% and a median negative return of -4.3%. It also explores correlations between short-term and medium-term returns and the influence of peer performance.

title: 4 Best Stocks To Buy Under $5
summary: This article highlights four promising stocks trading under $5: POET Technologies, TeraWulf, Ares Commercial Real Estate, and Regulus Therapeutics. Each company is presented with potential growth catalysts, including advancements in AI, a recovering Bitcoin market, strong real estate sectors, and positive clinical trial results and insider buying, respectively. The author emphasizes that low-priced stocks can offer significant returns if they have a compelling story or game-changing technology.

title: Should You Buy Vertex Pharmaceuticals Stock At $510?
summary: Vertex Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:VRTX) stock has seen a significant 27% surge year-to-date, contrasting sharply with the S&P 500's decline, driven by FDA approval of its new non-opioid pain reliever, Journavx. Despite its attractive historical performance and strong fundamentals across growth, financial stability, and downturn resilience, the stock is currently valued very highly, making it a volatile and tricky investment. The article suggests that while there's minimal cause for concern regarding its operating performance and financial health, its high valuation warrants cautious consideration.

title: Nvidia unveils next generation of DGX SuperPOD in partnership with Equinix
summary: Nvidia has unveiled its next generation of DGX SuperPOD, the DGX GB300 SuperPOD, designed to enhance AI inferencing with Grace Blackwell Architecture. This release comes as Nvidia partners with Equinix Inc., which will manage Nvidia's Instant AI Factory services, providing ready-made, AI-ready data centers globally. The collaboration aims to democratize AI deployment, enabling organizations to quickly scale and operate advanced AI infrastructure.

title: GCT Semiconductor Holding, Inc. Provides Business Update and Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Financial Results
summary: GCT Semiconductor Holding, Inc. announced its "202 5G CT" program, signaling the "Year of 5G" with forthcoming 5G chipset shipments in the first half of 2025. The company reported a net loss of $5.0 million for Q4 2024 and $12.4 million for the full year 2024, emphasizing a shift towards 5G chipset sales for future growth. GCT Semiconductor also highlighted significant debt reduction and ongoing discussions for capital funding.

title: Accelerating the Future of Transportation with SES AI’s NVIDIA-Powered Innovation for Electric Vehicles
summary: SES AI is utilizing NVIDIA hardware and software, including domain-adapted large language models (LLMs) and GPU-accelerated simulations, to rapidly discover new battery materials for electric vehicles. This collaboration has led to significant advancements, such as a 1.6Kx acceleration in simulation workflows, a 25% increase in LLM accuracy, and the identification of 17 candidate materials, resulting in a 20% improvement in battery cycle life. Their approach aims to reduce the time for battery electrolyte discovery from five years to six months, driving sustainable transportation.

title: Advanced Micro Devices Can Double in Price: Here’s Why
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is projected to double in price due to its increasing market share against NVIDIA in GPU and CPU markets, driven by lower-cost, better-performing products. Insider transactions, strong institutional buying, and an undervalued stock price relative to its growth potential support this optimistic outlook. The company is actively addressing its software infrastructure gap to further compete with NVIDIA.

title: Qualcomm Stock Is Coiling for a Breakout
summary: Qualcomm (NASDAQ: QCOM) is showing signs of an impending breakout, supported by robust technicals, an attractive valuation, and bullish analyst targets. The stock has consistently bounced off its key $150 support level, indicating strong buying interest after recent declines. With diversification efforts in automotive and AI chips paying off, Qualcomm offers significant value compared to its semiconductor peers.

title: Supermicro Ships Over 20 New Systems that Redefine Single-Socket Performance and Deliver Data Center Power, Space, and Cost Savings
summary: Supermicro announced the availability of over 20 new single-socket server systems supporting Intel Xeon 6 with P-Cores, designed to reduce data center power, space, and cost. These systems offer performance previously requiring dual-socket configurations, providing advantages like increased core counts, faster memory, and enhanced PCIe 5.0 lanes for diverse workloads. The new servers aim to optimize TCO and environmental impact across various applications from cloud computing to AI.

title: US firm Vishay Intertechnology confirms £250m investment at UK's biggest semiconductor facility
summary: US tech company Vishay Intertechnology has announced a £250m investment at its Newport foundry in South Wales, which will focus on producing advanced silicon carbide semiconductors for electric vehicles. This investment is part of Vishay's larger plan to inject £1bn into the facility by 2030, creating hundreds of new high-skilled jobs and potentially expanding into defense, aerospace, and renewable energy sectors. The UK Government is supporting this through its Automotive Transformation Fund, aiming to boost the economy and position the UK as a leader in advanced semiconductors.

title: SOXL stock: More chaos to come ahead of ‘Liberation Day’?
summary: The Direxion Daily Semiconductor Bull 3X Shares (SOXL) ETF has notably declined due to macroeconomic uncertainties and anxiety over President Trump’s tariff agenda. Investors are monitoring April 2, dubbed "Liberation Day," for new tariff announcements that could further impact semiconductor demand, even if the sector avoids direct tariffs, particularly through collateral damage to chip-consuming industries like automotive. The future of the CHIPS Act also remains a wildcard, despite bipartisan support.

title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD): Among Billionaire Ken Fisher’s Top Growth Stock Picks
summary: Billionaire Ken Fisher, through Fisher Investments, holds a significant stake in Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD), making it one of his top growth stock picks. AMD's strong performance is attributed to increasing demand for its CPUs and GPUs in data centers, especially within the booming AI segment. Fisher Asset Management's investment strategy focuses on growth stocks, and AMD's robust revenue and free cash flow growth in 2024 underscore its potential.

title: Game-Changing News for Advanced Micro Devices
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is integrating Rapt.AI into its MI300X, MI325X, and upcoming MI350 GPUs, which is expected to enhance its AI semiconductor capabilities and market share. The company also completed the acquisition of ZT Systems to offer end-to-end AI infrastructure solutions. Despite a recent stock price correction, analysts are still bullish on AMD, seeing it as a deep-value opportunity with significant growth catalysts.

title: Deciphering the shift from conversational to embodied AI
summary: The article discusses the evolution of AI from conversational models like ChatGPT to embodied intelligence, which involves AI agents interacting with the physical world through robotic bodies. It highlights the potential impact of embodied AI on various industries and daily life, as well as the challenges and advancements in China's efforts to develop this technology. Key points include the establishment of innovation centers and new platforms to accelerate research and application of embodied AI.

title: GlobalFoundries, Taiwan's UMC Rumored to Be Investigating a Merger to Build a US Chipmaker
summary: GlobalFoundries and Taiwan's UMC are reportedly considering a merger to create a US-based chipmaker. This move aims to navigate potential trade wars and rising tariffs, leveraging GlobalFoundries' US presence and "trusted foundry" status. The combined entity would operate fabs across the US, Asia, and Europe, addressing increasing demand for localized chip production and reducing reliance on foreign companies amidst a growing independent semiconductor industry in China.

title: ARTY ETF Holdings List — AMEX:ARTY
summary: The ARTY ETF (iShares Future AI & Tech ETF) holdings list, as of February 26, 2026, details its top investments. Key holdings include Micron Technology, Taiwan Semiconductor, SK Hynix, NAVER Corp., NVIDIA, and Advanced Micro Devices. The ETF focuses on companies involved in artificial intelligence and technology.

title: Taiwanese chipmaker UMC denies merger talks with GlobalFoundries
summary: Taiwanese chipmaker UMC has denied rumors of a potential merger with US-based GlobalFoundries. The company stated that the reports circulating in the media are entirely false.

title: Qualcomm seeks AI expansion with possible Alphawave buy
summary: Qualcomm is looking to expand its presence in the rapidly growing AI market by potentially acquiring UK-based semiconductor maker Alphawave IP Group and has already acquired MovianAI, VinAI's generative AI division. These moves aim to diversify Qualcomm's portfolio beyond mobile phone chipsets, whose revenue growth is slowing, and to strengthen its AI infrastructure offerings to compete with rivals like Nvidia, Intel, and AMD. The company faces urgency in diversification due to Apple phasing out Qualcomm modems in iPhones.

title: Newegg announces reverse stock split to maintain Nasdaq listing
summary: Newegg Commerce, Inc. announced a twenty-to-one reverse stock split, effective April 7, 2025, to meet Nasdaq's minimum price per share requirement, as its stock trades at $0.27 and has declined nearly 32% year-to-date. The company's financial health is rated as weak by InvestingPro, with concerns in profitability and price momentum, despite the company also recently expanding its product lineup with new AMD and NVIDIA GPUs. The split will proportionately increase the par value of shares and fractional shares will be exchanged for cash.

title: US Computer Vision in Healthcare Market worth $11.53 billion by 2029
summary: The US Computer Vision in Healthcare Market is projected to reach USD 11.53 billion by 2029, growing at a CAGR of 24.0% from 2024, driven by the need for efficient medical imaging analysis and AI-driven solutions. PC-based computer vision systems currently hold the largest market share due to their affordability and integration capabilities, while patient activity monitoring and fall prevention are the fastest-growing segments. Predictive maintenance applications also account for a significant share, supported by AI and machine learning advancements.

title: Mark Zuckerberg says Meta’s latest Llama models put open-source AI in the driving seat
summary: Meta Platforms Inc. has unveiled new additions to its Llama family of large language models, including Llama 4 Scout and Llama 4 Maverick, which Zuckerberg claims will put open-source AI in the driving seat. These models are available through Meta AI assistant and are designed for enhanced reasoning skills and various tasks. Meta is also working on a larger model, Llama 4 Behemoth, intended to be the "highest performing base model in the world."

title: Adeia to Release First Quarter 2025 Financial Results on May 5, 2025
summary: Adeia Inc. (Nasdaq: ADEA) announced it will release its first quarter 2025 financial results on Monday, May 5, 2025, after market close. The company will host an earnings conference call the same day at 2:00 p.m. Pacific Time. Details for accessing the call and a webcast replay are provided.

title: AMDG ETF Holdings List — NASDAQ:AMDG
summary: This article provides the current ETF holdings for the Leverage Shares 2X Long AMD Daily ETF (AMDG) as of February 26, 2026. The fund's composition includes U.S. Dollar at 83.43% and First American Funds Inc X Treasury Obligations Fund at 16.57%. This information details the specific weights and market values of each holding within the ETF.

title: The 25 Hottest AI Companies For Data Center And Edge: The 2025 CRN AI 100
summary: This article identifies the 25 hottest AI companies for data center and edge computing in 2025, according to CRN. These companies, ranging from tech giants like Cisco, Intel, Dell, and HPE to niche players and startups, are driving AI innovation with specialized hardware, software, and new AI agents. The piece highlights how these firms are transforming enterprise IT environments to handle AIOps and data management, and are poised to capitalize on the projected $749 billion spending on AI-supporting technologies by 2028.

title: Frontier sets fluid dynamics speed record, cutting simulation time from 38.5 to 1.5 hours on AMD GPUs
summary: Ansys, using its Fluent software on the Frontier supercomputer with AMD Instinct GPUs, has drastically reduced the simulation time for complex turbine systems. By leveraging 1,024 Instinct MI250X GPUs instead of over 3,700 CPU cores, the company cut a 2.2-billion-cell axial turbine stator simulation from 38.5 hours to just 1.5 hours. This breakthrough in computational fluid dynamics (CFD) offers significant advantages for industrial design, speeding up iterations and providing more accurate performance forecasts for various applications, including those for small and mid-sized businesses.

title: Semiconductors Leading Nasdaq's Bounce-Back: ETFs in Focus
summary: The Nasdaq saw a significant single-day surge led by semiconductor stocks after President Trump announced a temporary reduction in tariffs. This rebound occurred despite earlier market corrections triggered by initial tariff announcements. The article highlights the strong performance of semiconductor ETFs and specific chip stocks, attributing some of this strength to intensifying competition and advancements in AI models, while also acknowledging potential challenges for leading companies like NVIDIA.

title: Tigress Financial raises Intel stock price target to $52 on AI growth
summary: Tigress Financial Partners has increased its price target for Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) to $52, maintaining a Buy rating due to the company's AI innovation, strategic partnerships, and manufacturing expansion. Intel's return to profitability in Q3, driven by AI accelerator demand and an improved product mix, plus supportive U.S. policies, are expected to fuel multiyear growth. The stock has seen a 75.4% return over the past year and trades at a P/E ratio of 842.35, with 11 analysts revising earnings upwards for the upcoming period.

title: Earnings call transcript: AMD Q3 2025 beats forecasts, stock drops
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) reported record Q3 2025 revenue of $9.2 billion and an EPS of $1.20, significantly exceeding Wall Street forecasts. Despite strong financial results and optimistic Q4 guidance, the company's stock experienced a 3.67% drop in after-hours trading, signaling investor concerns about future growth prospects. AMD emphasized its strategic focus on AI and data center technologies, including major partnerships with OpenAI and Oracle for its next-gen MI450 series GPUs, which are expected to drive substantial revenue growth in the coming years.

title: NVIDIA to Manufacture American-Made AI Supercomputers in US for First Time
summary: NVIDIA is collaborating with manufacturing partners to produce AI supercomputers entirely within the U.S. for the first time. The company is commissioning over a million square feet of manufacturing space in Arizona and Texas to build and test NVIDIA Blackwell chips and AI supercomputers, aiming to complete up to half a trillion dollars of AI infrastructure in the US within four years. This initiative is expected to create hundreds of thousands of jobs and enhance supply chain resiliency while meeting the growing demand for AI technology.

title: Nvidia (NVDA) Says It Will Build Up to $500 Billion of AI Gear in US
summary: Nvidia plans to invest up to $500 billion in AI infrastructure in the US over the next four years through strategic manufacturing partnerships. This initiative includes producing Blackwell AI chips at TSMC's new Phoenix plant, building supercomputer manufacturing facilities in Texas with Foxconn and Wistron, and collaborating with Amkor Technology and Siliconware Precision Industries for packaging and testing in Arizona. The company aims to strengthen its dominant position in the AI chip market.

title: Is It Time To Book Profits In Royal Gold?
summary: Royal Gold (RGLD) has seen significant stock appreciation and revenue growth due to a gold rally, presenting a seemingly perfect financial picture. However, the article suggests it's time to consider booking profits, citing concerns about gold's sensitivity to macro factors, RGLD's inconsistent price action compared to gold, and a shrinking P/E ratio indicating limited future upside. Investors who have benefited from the rally are advised to consider selling or trimming their positions.

title: After Altera, is Intel preparing to sell Mobileye?
summary: Intel's recent sale of a 51% stake in its Altera unit for $4.46 billion under new CEO Lip-Bu Tan signals a broader strategy to shed non-core assets and refocus. This divestment, though a financial loss, provides crucial cash for Intel's recovery. Attention is now shifting to Mobileye, which analysts believe could be Intel's next divestment, allowing the company to concentrate on core operations like custom silicon, AI, and foundry services.

title: AMD: Lowering Our Fair Value Estimate Due to China Restrictions and PC Concerns
summary: Morningstar has lowered its fair value estimate for AMD to $120 per share from $140 due to new U.S. restrictions on AI chip exports to China and concerns about PC market performance. These factors led to reduced revenue estimates for AMD's AI GPU and PC CPU segments in 2025 and 2029, even though shares appear undervalued and AMD is expected to remain a winner in PC CPUs and gain some AI market share.

title: Taiwan firms bidding for AMD’s server assets
summary: Taiwanese firms Compal Electronics, Wiwynn Corp, and US company Jabil Inc are preparing to submit revised bids for AMD's AI server assembly plants, valued between US$3 billion and US$4 billion. These plants, inherited from AMD's acquisition of ZT Systems, are attractive due to their 1,500 skilled employees and align with the trend of Taiwanese companies building facilities in the US to mitigate potential tariffs. AMD aims to finalize the sale by the end of the second quarter, enabling it to avoid competing with its customers in manufacturing.

title: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. finishes 2nd fab
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) has completed construction of its second fab in North Phoenix ahead of schedule, driven by high demand for AI-related chips. The new facility will use 3-nanometer process technology, with production expected to begin sooner than the originally planned 2028. Additionally, TSMC plans to break ground on a third fab for 2-nanometer and A16 technologies later this year, pending permit approval.

title: 7 Best Meme Stocks to Buy Now
summary: This article identifies seven top meme stocks for 2025: Nvidia, SPDR S&P 500 ETF, enCore Energy, Tesla, AMD, Webull, and Point Bridge America First ETF. It explains that meme stocks are driven by social media hype and retail investor interest, leading to high volatility and potential for rapid gains or losses. The article emphasizes the importance of thorough research and risk management when investing in these stocks.

title: Marvell Technology: Shares Plunge, But Operating Momentum Accelerates (NASDAQ:MRVL)
summary: Marvell Technology's stock has significantly declined but the company's operating momentum is accelerating. While realistic earnings are modest due to stock-based compensation, the sale of its Automotive Ethernet business is expected to improve financial health. Despite improved valuations, caution is advised due to mixed business performance.

title: Command the data center lifecycle: maximize flexibility, uptime, and infrastructure investments in the AI expansion boom
summary: This article discusses the challenges and opportunities for data center operators in maximizing flexibility, uptime, and infrastructure investments amidst the AI-driven expansion boom. It emphasizes the need for strategic partnerships to navigate the complex landscape of technology, supply chain, and sustainability goals. Flex highlights its integrated approach, leveraging advanced manufacturing, power, cooling solutions, and lifecycle services, including the use of analytics and AI, to optimize data center operations and investments.

title: AMD Announces Press Conference at Computex 2025
summary: AMD announced it will host a press conference at Computex 2025 on Wednesday, May 21, 2025, at 11 a.m. UTC+8 in Taipei. The event will showcase advancements in AI across gaming, PCs, and professional workloads, featuring discussions from AMD senior vice president Jack Huynh and industry partners. A livestream will be available on their website starting May 20, with a replay afterwards.

title: ASE improves performance and reduces energy with AMD CPUs
summary: Advanced Semiconductor Engineering (ASE) significantly enhanced its performance and reduced energy consumption by adopting AMD EPYC and Ryzen CPUs. The company increased its data center compute resources by 2.5 times and improved client PC user productivity by 30%. This transition also led to a 30% reduction in total cost of ownership and a 6.5% decrease in energy consumption, while supporting smart factory automation and digital transformation efforts.

title: Shanghai chip firm doubles quarterly profit as China expands supply chain role
summary: Chinese semiconductor company Montage Technology reported a significant surge in revenue and profit for the March quarter, driven by strong demand from the AI industry for its DDR5 DRAM connector chips. This performance highlights China's growing influence in the global memory chip supply chain, despite existing US tech restrictions. Montage Technology is one of the world's leading suppliers of these critical connector chips.

title: Avnet AI Tech Days Empower Singapore's AI Ambitions
summary: Avnet Asia is hosting "Avnet AI Tech Days – Shaping the Future of AI" in Singapore on May 14, 2025, to support the nation's AI development efforts. The event will showcase cutting-edge AI solutions from Avnet and its partners, feature insights from leading AI experts, and address challenges such as security and data quality based on an Avnet Insights survey. This initiative aligns with Singapore's National AI Strategy, aiming to empower local businesses and accelerate AI integration across various sectors.

title: Sony May Spin-Off Its Image Sensor Business as a Separate Company
summary: Sony is reportedly considering spinning off its semiconductor business, Sony Semiconductor Solutions (SSS), into an independent entity, potentially valuing it at $49.2 billion. While a Sony spokesperson dismissed the report as speculation, the move aligns with Sony Group's efforts to streamline operations and focus on entertainment, given SSS's plateauing growth and shrinking margins. The semiconductor division recently realigned its executive structure, emphasizing a new leadership focused on navigating industry transformations and expanding beyond traditional boundaries.

title: SOXL Set to Ride AI Boom with Projected Growth in Semiconductor Sector
summary: The Direxion Daily Semiconductor Bull 3X Shares ETF (SOXL) is a leveraged fund that aims to triple the daily performance of the ICE Semiconductor Index. While it has seen significant gains from increased AI demand, its leveraged structure makes it volatile and more suitable for tactical trading. A conservative outlook projects SOXL to reach $100-$120 by 2028 from a current price near $80, considering industry cycles, interest rates, and trade uncertainties.

title: SOXL Set to Ride AI Boom with Projected Growth in Semiconductor Sector
summary: The Direxion Daily Semiconductor Bull 3X Shares ETF (SOXL) is analyzed for its potential performance in the semiconductor sector. Benefiting from increased demand in AI technologies, SOXL has shown significant rallies, though its leveraged nature makes it suitable for tactical trading rather than long-term holding. A conservative three-year outlook projects SOXL to reach $100-$120 by 2028, reflecting industry cycles and market pressures.

title: Here's Why Shareholders May Consider Paying TTM Technologies, Inc.'s (NASDAQ:TTMI) CEO A Little More
summary: TTM Technologies shareholders will hold their AGM on May 8th, where they will review strong recent results and consider executive compensation. CEO Tom Edman's total compensation of US$4.0m in 2024 is 38% below the industry average for similar-sized companies. Given the company's 38% total shareholder return and modest EPS growth over the past three years, shareholders may be open to a CEO pay raise, especially considering Edman's significant personal investment in the company's success.

title: Huntsman Expands Semiconductor Capabilities with New E-GRADE® Unit in Texas
summary: Huntsman Corp. has announced the expansion of its Conroe, Texas, facility with a new purification and packaging unit for its E-GRADE® product line to meet the rising global demand for high-purity materials in semiconductor manufacturing. This investment strengthens Huntsman’s position in the semiconductor market by supplying critical low-trace metal amines for advanced chip production, driven by AI and next-generation technologies. The new unit offers end-to-end quality control and is expected to boost the regional economy through job creation.

title: Catalyst Watch: Powell presser, AMD earnings, IBM event, and Lions Gate splits in two (SPY:NYSEARCA)
summary: This "Catalyst Watch" article highlights key upcoming events that could impact stock prices next week, including a press conference with Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, earnings reports from AMD, an event from IBM, and the proposed split of Lions Gate into two companies. It also mentions other notable events and company news for investors to monitor.

title: This low-profile CEO is the highest-paid in America with a $101 million paycheck that beat out Starbucks, Microsoft, and Apple chiefs
summary: Jim Anderson, the CEO of Coherent, has emerged as America's highest-paid CEO with a 2024 compensation package totaling over $101 million, largely due to stock awards. This figure surpassed the earnings of well-known executives like those from Starbucks, Microsoft, and Apple. Anderson's low-profile company, Coherent, specializes in network and laser equipment, and his compensation highlights a rising trend in CEO pay driven by significant stock payouts.

title: Qualcomm Beats Analyst Forecasts
summary: Qualcomm (Nasdaq: QCOM) reported better-than-estimated revenue for its second quarter and provided guidance for the third quarter, projecting $9.9 billion to $10.7 billion in revenue. Despite beating analyst consensus for diluted earnings per share and revenue, shares saw a dip due to Wall Street's cautious outlook regarding economic uncertainty and challenges in global trade. The company highlighted strong performance in automotive chips and IoT, along with a significant increase in smartphone handset revenue.

title: AT&S sees no immediate tariff impact, keeping close watch on supply chain shifts
summary: AT&S is currently not impacted by US tariffs targeting semiconductors as its IC substrates are not classified as such and are shipped to OSAT companies in Taiwan and Malaysia, not directly to the US. However, the company is closely monitoring geopolitical shifts, especially as major players like TSMC and Amkor establish US operations, which could eventually require AT&S to ship directly to the US and expose it to future tariff risks. The Kulim plant, which has started high-volume manufacturing of IC substrates for AMD, is expected to be a significant growth driver for AT&S, aiming to make it a top three IC substrate supplier globally.

title: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD) Is a Good Buy on Weakness, Commentator Suggests
summary: A commentator, Ryan Patel, suggests that Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD) is a good buy on weakness, noting Cathie Wood's recent purchase and AMD's strong first-quarter earnings. Patel believes AMD has significant growth potential compared to Nvidia, provided a U.S.-China trade deal materializes. However, the article also suggests that other AI stocks might offer greater returns in a shorter timeframe, directing readers to a report on a cheaper AI stock.

title: Chipmakers AMD, GlobalFoundries and Cirrus Logic post solid earnings and guidance, easing tariff fears
summary: U.S. chipmakers AMD, GlobalFoundries, and Cirrus Logic reported strong quarterly earnings and optimistic guidance, easing concerns about the impact of President Donald Trump's tariffs. Despite challenges like new export regulations for AI chips to China affecting AMD, and a slowdown in the smartphone market for GlobalFoundries, all three companies expressed confidence in their ability to navigate current market conditions. GlobalFoundries, in particular, anticipates benefiting from tariffs on foreign-made chips, projecting increased demand for its U.S.-manufactured products.

title: AMD Earnings: Strong PC Processor Revenue Is Encouraging
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) reported strong first-quarter revenue growth of 36% year over year, driven by a 68% increase in its client (PC) segment. The company's performance, particularly in higher-priced CPUs, reinforces the thesis of AMD gaining market share from Intel in both PCs and servers. Morningstar maintains its $120 fair value estimate but raised the Uncertainty Rating to Very High due to tariff and geopolitical concerns despite the positive earnings.

title: ASML vs. TSM: Which Semiconductor Stock is the Smarter Buy?
summary: The article compares ASML Holding (ASML) and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSM), both critical to the semiconductor industry. It concludes that ASML is currently the smarter investment due to its monopoly on EUV technology and strong earnings growth, despite TSM's impressive growth in AI chips. TSM faces greater geopolitical, cost, and supply-chain risks, reflected in its recent downward earnings revisions and increased capital spending.

title: Winning the AI race: Strengthening U.S. capabilities in computing and innovation
summary: Brad Smith, Vice Chair and President of Microsoft, testified before the Senate Commerce Committee on the critical issue of artificial intelligence, emphasizing three strategic priorities for the U.S. to win the AI race: innovation, diffusion, and exporting AI to allies. He highlighted the need for federal support in expanding datacenters and electrical grids, training a skilled AI workforce, and investing in research and development. The article stresses that winning the AI race requires a robust ecosystem including both private sector efforts and government policies, focusing on both technological advancement and widespread adoption of AI across all sectors.

title: Quantum Computing Stocks: D-Wave Earnings, Revenue Top Views
summary: D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) reported a smaller-than-expected first-quarter loss and revenue that topped views, leading to a surge in its stock. The company's revenue increased by 509% to $15 million, and its stock has advanced 206% in 2025, outperforming other quantum computing stocks. Investors are focusing on the near-term growth outlook for quantum computing companies.

title: Which Stocks Had Uncertainty Ratings Raised on Tariff Concerns?
summary: Morningstar analysts have raised the Uncertainty Ratings for 74 stocks in 2025, with 53 of these changes occurring between Trump's tariff announcement on April 2nd and May 7th. These increases reflect the significant impact of tariffs on the financial outlook for many companies, particularly in the auto, airline, and semiconductor sectors. Morningstar's Uncertainty Ratings influence star ratings for stocks, requiring a larger margin of safety for highly uncertain investments.

title: Best Buy Stock Soars as Tariff Rollback Sparks Rally
summary: Best Buy Co Inc (NYSE:BBY) stock is rallying significantly after the U.S. and China agreed to suspend most tariffs, a move that provides immediate relief for retailers heavily exposed to Chinese imports. The stock is up 7.6% and call traders are betting on further upside, with new positions being opened at the May 75 call. This surge has pushed BBY back above its year-over-year breakeven level, recovering from a previous dip after new tariffs were introduced.

title: Cathie Wood Doubles Down on Nvidia (NVDA) Stock Despite Selloff. Should You Copy Her?
summary: Cathie Wood's ARK Invest recently purchased over 107,000 shares of Nvidia (NVDA) and also invested in AMD, following a significant tech selloff and concerns about competition and chip export rules. The article discusses her rationale, including potential changes in U.S. chip export policies under a new administration, Nvidia's strong market position in AI accelerators, and growing demand from major tech companies. It then advises whether individual investors should follow Wood's lead, highlighting both the long-term potential of Nvidia as an AI pure-play and short-term risks such as increasing competition from Huawei and potential worsening chip restrictions.

title: New AMD EPYC 4005 Series Server CPUs Now Available Exclusively at Newegg for North American Retail Launch
summary: Newegg Commerce, Inc. announced the exclusive North American retail launch of the new AMD EPYC 4005 Series processors through Newegg and Newegg Business. These advanced, energy-efficient server CPUs are designed for small and growing businesses, offering performance, efficiency, and value with ‘Zen 5’ cores. Newegg Business will also host a free webinar on June 18, 2025, to delve into the processors' capabilities and feature a Q&A with AMD.

title: Is BigBear.ai Holdings, Inc. (BBAI) the Best AI Stock to Buy Under $10?
summary: This article evaluates BigBear.ai Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:BBAI) as a potential AI stock investment under $10, amidst strong market sentiment and continued investment in AI infrastructure despite earlier fears of a bubble burst. It highlights BBAI's recent financial performance, strategic partnerships, and positioning in the crowded AI market, noting its popularity among hedge funds and analysts while also presenting a recent price target cut. The article concludes by ranking BBAI tenth on a list of best AI stocks under $10, suggesting other AI stocks might offer higher returns.

title: AMD Announces New $6 Billion Share Repurchase Authorization
summary: AMD announced a new $6 billion share repurchase program, bringing its total current repurchase authority to approximately $10 billion. This decision reflects the company's confidence in its strategic direction, growth prospects, and ability to generate strong free cash flow. AMD remains committed to disciplined capital allocation and driving strong shareholder returns.

title: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD) Gains Boost As Bank Of America Raises Target On AI Deal Momentum
summary: Bank of America has reiterated its "Buy" rating for Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and raised its price target to $130 from $120. This boost comes from the potential upside in long-term AI opportunities, including multi-year AI infrastructure projects with Saudi Arabian AI startup HUMAIN, estimated at $3B-$5B annually. The bank believes "Sovereign AI" could mitigate power limitations for data centers in the U.S. and reduce the impact of export restrictions to China, positioning AMD as a strong player in the expanding global AI market.

title: NetApp’s strategy aligns AI and data with real-time demand
summary: NetApp is focusing on a strategy that aligns AI and data with real-time demand, emphasizing nuance over hype to help enterprises derive meaning from vast amounts of data. CEO George Kurian highlights four key market drivers—Flash, AI, block, and cloud—and a customer-first approach that prioritizes long-term responsibility and innovation. The company's Intelligent Data Infrastructure strategy aims to manage risk, empower AI, ensure performance at scale, and deliver real-time ransomware detection, with applications seen in partnerships with organizations like the San Francisco 49ers and Aston Martin Formula One Team.

title: Intel spars with EU regulators over $421.4 million antitrust fine
summary: U.S. chipmaker Intel is challenging a 376 million euro ($421.4 million) antitrust fine imposed by EU regulators, arguing it is disproportionate and unfair. The fine stems from payments made by Intel to HP, Acer, and Lenovo to delay or halt rival products between 2002 and 2006. Intel's lawyer argued that these were "narrow, tactical moves" and did not represent a market-wide foreclosure strategy, while the EU watchdog maintains the fine is appropriate and not disproportionate.

title: A New Path! US Capital to Jointly Build Entire Rare Earth Industry Chain in Saudi Arabia
summary: US rare earth miner MP Materials has partnered with Saudi Arabia's Ma’aden to establish a comprehensive rare earth supply chain in the Kingdom, encompassing mining, separation, refining, and magnet production. This collaboration aims to diversify the global rare earth supply chain, leveraging Saudi Arabia's resources and MP Materials' expertise. The initiative is part of broader US investment in Saudi Arabia, with the goal of strengthening critical mineral supply chains amidst growing demand for advanced technologies.

title: Clean energy stocks shine bright as improved trade outlook sparks rally
summary: Clean energy stocks, particularly solar names, experienced a significant rally following an improved U.S.-China trade outlook, which included tariff cuts and prompted economists to upgrade growth forecasts. This surge occurred despite a broader equity rally and strong performance in semiconductors, with First Solar Inc. leading the clean energy sector. Meanwhile, UnitedHealth Group faced a severe crisis with a 27% stock drop, CEO resignation, and a criminal probe, while economic data showed softening inflation but cautious consumer sentiment.

title: Sanmina Agrees To Acquire ZT Systems' Data Center Infrastructure Manufacturing Business From AMD
summary: Sanmina Corp. announced its agreement to acquire ZT Systems' data center infrastructure manufacturing business from AMD Corp. for $3 billion. This acquisition will establish Sanmina as a vertically integrated manufacturing solutions provider focused on accelerating AI systems deployment for data centers. The deal is expected to be accretive to Sanmina's non-GAAP EPS in its first year and will position the company with advanced liquid cooling capabilities and global manufacturing facilities.

title: SANMINA ANNOUNCES ACQUISITION OF DATA CENTER INFRASTRUCTURE MANUFACTURING BUSINESS OF ZT SYSTEMS FROM AMD
summary: Sanmina Corporation announced its definitive agreement to acquire the data center infrastructure manufacturing business of ZT Systems from AMD for up to $3 billion, comprising cash, equity, and contingent consideration. This acquisition is expected to significantly enhance Sanmina's presence in the Cloud and AI infrastructure market, doubling its revenue scale within three years and strengthening its end-to-end manufacturing solutions. The transaction also establishes a strategic partnership with AMD, positioning Sanmina as a preferred U.S.-based NPI manufacturing partner for AMD's AI rack and cluster-scale systems.

title: AMD and Red Hat double down on AI infrastructure flexibility
summary: AMD and Red Hat are strengthening their partnership to provide flexible AI infrastructure solutions, emphasizing open architectures and hybrid approaches to help companies modernize. This collaboration aims to give customers optionality, choice, and flexibility in deploying AI across cloud, on-premises, and edge environments, leading to significant cost savings. The companies are working together to enable enterprises to scale AI adoption pragmatically by defining early use cases and proving value quickly.

title: Mizuho Raises Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Target to $135, Reiterates Outperform
summary: Mizuho has increased its price target for Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) to $135 from $117, maintaining an "Outperform" rating, following an artificial intelligence server supply chain call. The article also discusses the broader context of AI's energy demands, mentioning former US President Donald Trump's push for coal-fired plants to meet this need, despite the tech industry's focus on renewable energy and natural gas. AMD is highlighted as a significant AI stock, ranking 9th on Wall Street's radar, with a strong presence in semiconductors, processors, and GPUs for AI.

title: Efficiency upgrade for OLED screens: A route to blue PHOLED longevity
summary: Researchers at the University of Michigan have developed a blue phosphorescent OLED (PHOLED) that matches the longevity and efficiency of existing green PHOLEDs. This breakthrough addresses a long-standing challenge in OLED technology, where blue OLEDs typically use less efficient fluorescent methods, limiting overall display performance. The team achieved this by implementing design innovations, including a special coating on the negative electrode to enhance energy conversion into blue light and a tandem OLED structure to reduce stress on light-emitting layers.

title: Intel, Micron, Qualcomm, TI join call for chip tariff exemptions ...
summary: US chip giants Intel, Micron, Qualcomm, and Texas Instruments have asked the US Department of Commerce for semiconductor import tariff exemptions or lighter burdens. Their submissions are a response to a "Section 232" investigation into semiconductor imports, coming after TSMC also appealed to the Trump administration. The companies, while supporting reshoring, warn that poorly designed tariffs could harm US interests and the complex semiconductor supply chain.

title: KEPCO to invest $53.1 bil. by 2038 to expand power supply infrastructure for advanced industries
summary: Korea Electric Power Corp. (KEPCO) announced a plan to invest $53.1 billion by 2038 to enhance power supply infrastructure for key industries like semiconductors and improve its electricity transmission system. This investment includes building a substation in a Yongin industrial cluster to power AI memory chip production and establishing advanced high-voltage direct current (HVDC) systems. KEPCO projects this initiative will generate significant economic impact, valued at approximately 134 trillion won, and create 480,000 jobs.

title: Semiconductor ETF options show caution ahead of Nvidia results
summary: Traders in the options market are showing caution ahead of Nvidia's earnings report, with significant defensive options contracts being traded on the VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH). This reflects concerns about potential industry-wide volatility, even as options activity on Nvidia itself is more mixed, with some investors selling calls to monetize elevated premiums. The heightened activity underscores investor interest and some apprehension following an earlier strong market run.

title: Nvidia delivers another earnings and revenue beat on rampant data center growth
summary: Nvidia's shares rose almost 5% after reporting better-than-expected earnings and revenue for the first quarter, driven by a 73% year-over-year growth in its data center business. Despite missing guidance due to U.S. export restrictions on advanced chips to China, the company's strong performance in AI infrastructure and other sectors, including gaming and automotive, reassured investors. CEO Jensen Huang acknowledged the closure of the Chinese AI chip market for U.S. industry but emphasized the overwhelming global demand for Nvidia's AI solutions.

title: Notable ETF Outflow Detected - TQQQ, AMD, ADBE, AMGN
summary: The ProShares UltraPro QQQ (TQQQ) ETF experienced a significant outflow of approximately $610.1 million, marking a 2.4% decrease week-over-week. This outflow indicates a reduction in units, which can lead to the selling of underlying holdings like Advanced Micro Devices Inc (AMD), Adobe Inc (ADBE), and Amgen Inc (AMGN). The article also highlights TQQQ's recent price performance, noting its 52-week range and current trading price compared to its 200-day moving average.

title: CHT ITG delivers sustainable performance cloud with AMD
summary: Chunghwa Telecom Information Technology Group (CHT ITG) has successfully improved its cloud service performance and met environmental goals by deploying 3rd Gen AMD EPYC™ CPUs. This strategic move resulted in a 1.6x reduction in data center space, optimized hardware costs, and reduced energy consumption, aligning with ESG targets. The high core density and performance of AMD EPYC processors proved ideal for CHT ITG's diverse cloud workloads, including evolving AI applications, leading to enhanced service offerings and a roadmap for future expansion with upcoming generations of AMD EPYC CPUs.

title: Salesforce Earnings: Stock Attractive With Agentforce Momentum Building
summary: Salesforce reported strong fiscal first-quarter results, exceeding guidance with 8% revenue growth to $9.83 billion, primarily driven by Data Cloud and Agentforce. The company raised its fair value estimate to $325 per share due to positive results and increased full-year guidance, despite uncertainties from the Informatica deal. Agentforce, generating $100 million in ARR, continues to build momentum, making Salesforce shares attractive.

title: Alliance entertainment executive chairman Bruce Ogilvie buys $5,400 in stock
summary: Alliance Entertainment Holding Corp's Executive Chairman, Bruce A. Ogilvie Jr., recently invested $5,400 in the company's common stock and acquired numerous warrants. These transactions reflect his continued commitment to the company, whose stock has declined by 44% in the last six months but recently returned to profitability in Q3 FY25 with a net income of $1.9 million. The company is also expanding its distribution through strategic partnerships and a focus on direct-to-consumer fulfillment.

title: Malaysia's delicate balancing act
summary: Malaysia is facing increasing pressure to abandon its neutral stance amidst the escalating US-China tech war, particularly concerning semiconductor supply chains. The US's efforts to reshore chip production are reconfiguring the global industry, impacting Malaysia, which is a significant semiconductor exporter. A recent incident involving Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching and Huawei’s Ascend chips highlighted the difficulties Malaysia faces in maintaining its neutrality given geopolitical tensions.

title: Synopsys suspends China sales; Ansys merger gets FTC nod
summary: Synopsys has suspended all sales to China due to new trade restrictions from the Trump administration, impacting a market that accounts for about 10% of its revenues. Concurrently, the Federal Trade Commission has conditionally approved Synopsys' $35 billion acquisition of Ansys, requiring divestment of certain assets—specifically optical software tools, photonic software tools, and power consumption analysis tools—to Keysight Technologies due to anticompetitive concerns. This follows similar regulatory hurdles in the European Union, while the China sales suspension may complicate the Ansys acquisition's approval in China.

title: GlobalFoundries Announces $16B U.S. Investment to Reshore Essential Chip Manufacturing and Accelerate AI Growth
summary: GlobalFoundries (GF) announced a $16 billion investment to expand its semiconductor manufacturing and advanced packaging capabilities across its facilities in New York and Vermont. This strategic investment is backed by major tech companies like Apple, SpaceX, AMD, Qualcomm, NXP, and GM, and aims to reshore essential chip manufacturing to the U.S., accelerating innovation in AI-enabling and power-efficient technologies. The investment supports the explosive growth in AI and strengthens the domestic semiconductor supply chain and leadership.

title: GlobalFoundries pledges $16bn investment to reshore chip manufacturing at New York and Vermont fabs
summary: GlobalFoundries (GF) has committed an additional $3 billion investment to enhance semiconductor manufacturing at its New York and Vermont facilities, increasing its total US investment to $16 billion. This expansion supports advanced R&D in packaging innovation, silicon photonics, and gallium nitride technologies, with collaborations including major companies like Apple, AMD, and Qualcomm. The investment is partly supported by $1.5 billion from the CHIPS and Science Act funding and New York State Green CHIPS Program, aiming to strengthen domestic semiconductor supply chains despite former President Trump's past criticisms of the CHIPS Act.

title: Foley Represents Mayville Engineering Company in Acquisition of Accu-Fab
summary: Foley & Lardner LLP represented Mayville Engineering Company (MEC) in its definitive agreement to acquire Accu-Fab, LLC from Tide Rock for $140.5 million. This acquisition supports MEC’s strategic goal of expanding into high-growth adjacent end-markets, including critical power infrastructure, data centers, and renewable energy. The transaction, expected to close in Q3 2025, aims to enhance MEC's commercial reach, achieve revenue synergies, and generate operational efficiencies.

title: Cathie Wood Snaps Up Amazon and AMD Stocks but Offloads Palantir and CoreWeave
summary: Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs recently acquired significant shares of Amazon (AMZN) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), signaling confidence in these tech giants' growth potential despite market concerns. Conversely, ARK offloaded shares of Palantir Technologies (PLTR) and CoreWeave (CRWV), with analysts noting valuation concerns for both. Wood also made other notable purchases including Guardant Health Inc. (GH) and 10X Genomics Inc. (TXG).

title: Broadcom, Nvidia, and AMD Could Help This Unstoppable ETF Turn $250,000 Into $1 Million in 10 Years
summary: The iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX), with top holdings in Nvidia, Broadcom, and AMD, could be a strong investment due to the surging demand for AI computing capacity, with data center spending potentially reaching $1 trillion annually by 2028. The article suggests that an investment of $250,000 in this ETF could grow to $1 million within a decade, based on its historical performance and the anticipated growth in the AI hardware sector. The ETF focuses on companies that design, manufacture, and distribute semiconductors, making it well-positioned to benefit from the AI megatrend.

title: NVIDIA's new tweaked-for-China B40 AI GPU expected to race into mass production this month
summary: NVIDIA's new B40 AI GPU, specifically designed for the Chinese market, is anticipated to enter mass production in June 2025. This chip, based on the Blackwell architecture and featuring GDDR7 memory, aims to serve mid-to-high-end users as a more cost-effective alternative to the H20 GPU. Manufacturers expect to ship over one million units by the end of 2025.

title: Imperial College London Chooses Intel Xeon 6 for Latest HPC Supercomputer
summary: Imperial College London has selected Intel Xeon 6 6900P series processors to power its new HX2 supercomputer, built on Lenovo’s ThinkSystem SC750 V4 Neptune platform. This water-cooled system, developed in collaboration with ICICLE, is set to deploy this year to advance high-performance computing and artificial intelligence research. The HX2 supercomputer emphasizes sustainability through its liquid cooling technology, aiming to significantly reduce energy consumption and environmental impact while delivering enhanced computational performance for scientific research.

title: Big movements in NuCana plc stock price on Wednesday moving 32.83% between high and low
summary: The NuCana plc (NCNA) stock price surged by 14.98% on Wednesday, June 11, 2025, continuing a four-day gaining streak. Despite a positive short-term Moving Average signal and a recent pivot bottom buy signal, the stock faces a general sell signal from its long-term Moving Average and is considered extremely overbought on RSI14. Analysts from William Blair downgraded NCNA to "Market Perform" in August 2024, and the stock is anticipated to perform weakly in the near future due to high volatility and conflicting technical signals.

title: Supermicro Delivers Performance and Efficiency Optimized Liquid-Cooled and Air-Cooled AI Solutions with AMD Instinct™ MI350 Series GPUs and Platforms
summary: Supermicro has launched new highly optimized AI solutions featuring AMD Instinct MI350 Series GPUs, offering breakthrough inference performance and power efficiency for both liquid-cooled and air-cooled systems. These H14 GPU solutions, powered by the 4th generation AMD CDNA™ architecture, are designed for large AI training models and high-speed inference workloads, providing significant memory capacity and computational improvements. The solutions aim to maximize performance, scalability, and efficiency while reducing TCO for AI-driven data centers.

title: DigitalOcean and AMD Collaborate to Advance AI Using Cloud-Based GPUs
summary: DigitalOcean and AMD are partnering to provide DigitalOcean customers with access to AMD Instinct™ GPUs through DigitalOcean GPU Droplets, starting with the MI300X and later including the MI325X. This collaboration aims to make powerful AI capabilities more accessible and affordable for digital native enterprises, offering competitive pricing, simplified setup, and access to the AMD Developer Cloud. This initiative empowers businesses to integrate AI into their applications and handle demanding AI workloads efficiently.

title: Nvidia Stock Dips Amid Market Sell-Off and Geopolitical Tension
summary: Nvidia's stock experienced a dip of 1.3% due to a broader market sell-off triggered by rising geopolitical tensions, specifically an attack by Israel on Iran. Despite this short-term setback, Nvidia’s strong position in the AI market, commanding about 90% of the AI GPU market, and strategic expansion plans in Europe, including launching 20 new AI factories, suggest a robust long-term outlook. The dip is seen as a market reaction to external events rather than an indication of inherent company weakness, as Nvidia's fundamentals in AI hardware and software remain strong.

title: Ai drives power management into top ten fabless chip list ...
summary: The latest TrendForce data reveals a record-high global IC design industry revenue of US$77.4 billion in Q1 2025, driven by AI data center construction and early stocking. This surge has propelled power management chip designer Monolithic Power System (MPS) into the top ten fabless chip designers for the first time, reaching $638 million in Q1 revenue. While Nvidia remains dominant, the growing demand for AI is significantly increasing the importance and market share of power management solutions.

title: Top Stock Movers Now: United Airlines, AMD, Lockheed Martin, and More
summary: U.S. equities saw a midday jump, and oil prices fell due to optimism that Mideast tensions would be contained. United Airlines and other travel-related stocks rose, while oil producers and defense contractors declined. Advanced Micro Devices was the top performer in the S&P 500 after a price target boost.

title: AMD Stock Soars as Piper Sandler Raises Price Target After 'Advancing AI' Event
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) shares surged nearly 10% after Piper Sandler raised its price target to $140 from $125 and maintained an "overweight" rating, following AMD's "Advancing AI" event. The upgrade was driven by the unveiling of the new Helios server rack architecture, which analysts believe is pivotal for AMD Instinct GPUs. Additionally, Bank of America anticipates a future high-profile partnership announcement with Amazon Web Services.

title: Marvell Introduces Optimized Integrated Power Solutions to Boost Performance, Efficiency, and ROI of Accelerated Infrastructure
summary: Marvell Technology, Inc. has launched its Package Integrated Voltage Regulator (PIVR) power solutions, designed to significantly enhance the performance, efficiency, and ROI of AI and cloud infrastructure for hyperscalers. These pre-validated solutions transition from board-level power delivery to integrated silicon chips, offering up to 2X higher current density and an 85% reduction in transmission losses. This innovation supports the development of 4+ kilowatt compute platforms, providing a foundation for elevating computing operations in data centers.

title: STMicroelectronics Introduces Advanced Human Presence Detection Solution to Enhance Laptop and PC User Experience
summary: STMicroelectronics has launched a new Human Presence Detection (HPD) solution for laptops and PCs, designed to improve user experience, security, and power efficiency. This technology integrates ST's FlightSense Time-of-Flight (ToF) sensors with AI algorithms to enable features like adaptive screen dimming, automatic lock/wake, and multi-person detection, all while prioritizing user privacy by not using cameras. The solution is expected to reduce power consumption by over 20% daily and is based on extensive AI development and data collection efforts.

title: Texas Instruments plans $60 billion U.S. investment amid Trump's onshoring push
summary: Texas Instruments announced plans for a $60 billion U.S. investment to expand its manufacturing footprint, responding to government pressure to onshore semiconductor production. This investment, aimed at building or expanding facilities in Texas and Utah and creating 60,000 jobs, follows similar commitments from other chipmakers. The move is seen as an overture to the Trump administration, which has advocated for domestic semiconductor manufacturing and threatened tariffs.

title: Keysight Enables AMD to Showcase Electrical PCI Express® Compliance up to 64 GT/s
summary: Keysight Technologies enabled AMD to accelerate testing for electrical compliance of PCI Express (PCIe) specification for pre-production AMD server CPUs. Through early access to Keysight's beta software and CEM test tools, AMD was able to develop and test a PCIe 6.0-capable server motherboard operating at up to 64 GT/s. This collaboration allowed AMD to validate its new server CPUs before official tool releases, ensuring market readiness for high-speed I/O critical for AI and networking applications.

title: ASML Holding Rises 10% YTD: Is the Stock Worth a Good Buy?
summary: ASML Holding has seen a 10% year-to-date gain, outperforming the broader Zacks Computer and Technology sector and several semiconductor peers. The company's dominance in EUV lithography, crucial for advanced chip manufacturing, and its strong financial performance, including a 46% year-over-year net sales increase in Q1 2025, position it well for future growth, especially with increasing AI demand. Despite trading in line with the sector on a P/E basis, its critical role in semiconductor advancement makes it a compelling buy for long-term investors.

title: Cabaletta Bio, Inc. (NASDAQ:CABA) Receives Consensus Recommendation of "Buy" from Analysts
summary: Cabaletta Bio, Inc. (NASDAQ:CABA) has received a consensus "Buy" recommendation from nine research firms, with an average twelve-month price target of $18.88. Analysts have issued various ratings and price target adjustments, including several "buy" and "overweight" ratings despite some target price reductions. Hedge funds have also shown increased activity in CABA shares, with several firms raising their stakes, while the company reported lower-than-expected earnings per share for the last quarter.

title: Chip Stocks Slide on Report of New Possible Limits on China
summary: Shares of major chipmakers like Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC), AMD, and ASML fell after a report indicated the Trump administration is considering new restrictions on China regarding chip technology. A US official reportedly suggested revoking waivers that allowed semiconductor makers to access American equipment in China, a move expected to escalate trade tensions.

title: Double-Leverage Bitcoin's Rise With This Crypto Industry ETF
summary: The Direxion Daily Crypto Industry Bull 2X Shares (LMBO) ETF offers investors a way to capitalize on Bitcoin's bullish momentum by investing in companies within the crypto sphere. This fund focuses on businesses involved in distributed ledger and decentralized payment technology, including blockchain and DeFi, rather than directly holding cryptocurrencies. It provides a less volatile approach to cryptocurrency exposure with companies like Visa, Mastercard, AMD, and Nvidia as top holdings.

title: AMD, Keysight Technologies Collaborate to Develop PCIe 6.0 for High-Speed AI Applications
summary: AMD and Keysight Technologies have partnered to advance PCIe 6.0 technology, crucial for high-speed data transfer and AI applications. This collaboration led to the development and testing of a PCIe 6.0-capable server motherboard, demonstrated at the PCI-SIG Developers’ Conference. PCIe 6.0 offers significantly faster data transfer rates, lower latency, and improved energy efficiency, essential for deploying advanced AI and next-gen Ethernet communications.

title: CFRA has upgraded AMD (AMD.US) rating due to optimism about its open-source AI software progress and potential return to the Chinese market.
summary: CFRA upgraded AMD's stock rating from "Buy" to "Strong Buy," significantly raising the target price to $165, due to optimism surrounding AMD's open-source AI software progress and potential return to the Chinese market. This upgrade is driven by anticipated shifts in the competitive landscape with NVIDIA, the upcoming MI400x AI accelerator, and AMD's expanding customer ecosystem with its ROCm framework. Analyst Angelo Zino also predicts a robust recovery in the GPU server market, further boosting AMD's medium to long-term prospects.

title: Why You Shouldn’t Be Buying Rocket Lab Stock?

summary: Wall Street saw a rebound fueled by optimism following yesterday's declines, with the Dow Jones, S&P 500, and Nasdaq all posting gains. title: AMD to Buy $150 Million of Nutanix Stock in New Partnership
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is investing $150 million in Nutanix stock and forming a strategic partnership with the company. The collaboration includes joint engineering and sales initiatives, with AMD providing up to an additional $100 million to fund the development and sale of an infrastructure platform for AI applications. This partnership aims to leverage both companies' expertise to advance AI infrastructure.

title: US Bancorp DE Boosts Holdings in Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. $AMD
